emotional_choices.source_moment: Inside the airborne house after Dorothy is thrown down and before she settles herself.
emotional_choices.narrative_lead_in: It was very dark, and the wind howled horribly around you. After the first fright, your heart still had to choose what shape that fright would take.
emotional_choices.choice_question: How does the fear first show itself in you?

options.label: A
options.text: You clutch Toto and let yourself cry out at the dreadful noise.
options.outcome: You caught Toto close and cried out, for the noise was indeed dreadful and there is no wickedness in naming fear when fear is upon you. The little dog wriggled and barked, and for a moment the room seemed fuller because your own voice answered the storm. Then, having spent some of your fright in that way, you breathed a little easier. The house still swayed, and the wind still shrieked, but you had at least proved to yourself that you were alive inside it, and not merely being carried like a bundle through the dark sky.
options.tonal_effect: Narration becomes more openly childlike and bodily, with fear named plainly before calm returns.

npc_dispositions.shift: Bond emphasis strengthened through mutual distress.

emotional_ledger_entries: defining_moral_choices +1: named fear openly in peril

options.world_noticed_signal: Toto answered your cry with barking of his own, as if he too wished the storm to know he was there.

options.label: B
options.text: You sit very still and wait to see what the house will do next.
options.outcome: You sat very still upon the floor and waited, for when a thing cannot be stopped there is often wisdom in watching it closely. The wind made a great uproar, and once the house tipped badly, yet after that it rode more gently than you had thought possible. So your fear did not master you altogether. It became a quiet fear, and then a waiting one. Even Toto's running seemed less wild because you yourself were not adding to the confusion. In that manner you began to learn the strange motion of the house aloft, and the dark room grew a little less dreadful for being endured so steadily.
options.tonal_effect: Narration leans toward steadiness, order, and practical endurance.

emotional_ledger_entries: defining_moral_choices +1: bore fear with steadiness

options.world_noticed_signal: The floor still swayed beneath you, but after a time it seemed to rock rather than strike. The darkness was no kinder, yet it was less surprising.

options.label: C
options.text: You whisper to Toto and try to make sense of the queer motion.
options.outcome: You spoke to Toto in a low voice and tried to notice how the house moved, for even in fear the mind sometimes wishes to know what sort of trouble has taken hold of it. The room was dark, and the wind howled, but the floor under you did not leap as a falling house might leap. Instead it rocked and swayed in a queer, almost cradle-like way. This did not make the danger good, of course; but it made it a little more understandable. Toto quieted somewhat at the sound of your voice, and your own heart followed him, becoming less wild because it had found something to observe.
options.tonal_effect: Narration becomes curious within fear, noticing practical details and naming them plainly.

npc_dispositions.shift: Comfort association deepened by Dorothy's soothing attention.

emotional_ledger_entries: secrets_discovered +1: observed the house's strange airborne steadiness

options.world_noticed_signal: Toto's barking grew less sharp when you spoke to him. The swaying of the house began to seem queer before it seemed terrible.

emotional_choices.all_paths_arrive_at: Toto slips toward the open trap, and Dorothy must pull him back.

emotional_choices.source_moment: When Toto falls toward the open trap and Dorothy pulls him up by the ear and shuts the door.
emotional_choices.narrative_lead_in: One of Toto's ears stuck up through the hole, for the strong pressure of the air was keeping him from falling. You crept to him at once, but even rescue has more than one feeling in it.
emotional_choices.choice_question: What is strongest in you as you save him?

options.label: A
options.text: You murmur to him as if he were a frightened little child.
options.outcome: You caught Toto by the ear and drew him back into the room, and while you did so you spoke to him softly, as one might soothe a frightened child. He trembled all over, and his black eyes were very wide, so it was easy to pity him. Afterward you shut the trap door firmly, that no more accidents might happen. This small practical act had much love in it, for kindness is often shown best by preventing the next trouble. Then Toto crept close beside you as if he knew very well who had saved him, and the dark room felt less lonely because there were now two frightened creatures safe upon the same floor.
options.tonal_effect: Narration grows warm, domestic, and approving of protective care.

emotional_ledger_entries: npcs_helped_or_harmed +1: rescued Toto from the trap
acts_of_mercy +1

options.world_noticed_signal: When the trap was shut, the room seemed at once a safer place, though it was still in the sky. Toto pressed close against your skirt.

options.label: B
options.text: You scold him through your relief because he nearly frightened you to death.
options.outcome: You dragged Toto back and then scolded him in the same breath, which was only natural, for relief often speaks sharply when love has been badly frightened. Toto could not understand the words, of course, but he understood your tone well enough, and hung his head for a moment before creeping nearer again. Then you shut the trap door with more force than before, being resolved that he should not tumble through it a second time. The little burst of temper did you good, and afterward your hands were steadier. You had not ceased to love him in the least; indeed, it was because you loved him so dearly that his danger had made you almost angry with fear.
options.tonal_effect: Narration acknowledges brisk, lively affection with a flash of relieved temper.

npc_dispositions.shift: Bond remains strong, colored by lively protectiveness.

emotional_ledger_entries: npcs_helped_or_harmed +1: rescued Toto from the trap
defining_moral_choices +1: protective fear expressed sharply

options.world_noticed_signal: The trap door banged shut under your hand. Toto blinked, shook himself, and then came back to you all the same.

options.label: C
options.text: You check the trap carefully and make sure no such accident can happen again.
options.outcome: You pulled Toto safely into the room and then gave your chief attention to the trap door itself, seeing that it was properly shut so that no more accidents could happen. This was a sensible kindness, and perhaps the best sort in a dangerous place, because it guarded both the little dog and yourself from fresh trouble. Toto soon recovered enough to follow close behind you, and that seemed proof enough that he bore no grudge for being saved in so practical a fashion. Afterward the room, though still dark and noisy, felt better ordered than before. One danger had been plainly met and put away, and plain victories are a comfort when greater dangers still remain.
options.tonal_effect: Narration emphasizes competent caretaking and practical tenderness.

emotional_ledger_entries: npcs_helped_or_harmed +1: rescued Toto from the trap
key_successes_failures +1: prevented repeat accident in airborne house

options.world_noticed_signal: The shut trap lay flat and harmless in the floor after that. Even the wild house seemed easier to endure when one danger had been set in order.

emotional_choices.all_paths_arrive_at: The trap is closed, the immediate danger passes, and Dorothy endures the long hours aloft.

emotional_choices.source_moment: Dorothy opens the door in Chapter II and sees the marvelous country of Oz.
emotional_choices.narrative_lead_in: The bright sunshine flooded the little room, and when you opened the door the gray prairie was gone. Before you lay green banks, rich fruits, gorgeous flowers, and birds with rare and brilliant plumage.
emotional_choices.choice_question: What kind of amazement takes hold of you first?

options.label: A
options.text: You stand still and take it all in as if afraid the beauty may vanish.
options.outcome: You stood quite still in the doorway, for the country before you was so beautiful that it seemed wise not to disturb it by too sudden a movement. The green grass, the rich fruits, the flowers on every hand, and the little brook all entered your eyes one after another, until your heart felt almost too full for speech. It was not the kind of beauty Kansas knew, and that made it stranger as well as lovelier. Yet because you paused, the marvel came to you in good order, and not as a blur. Toto, too, stood looking, though perhaps he cared more for smells than colors, as dogs commonly do.
options.tonal_effect: Narration becomes reverent, scenic, and orderly in its listing of marvels.

emotional_ledger_entries: secrets_discovered +1: first sight of Oz's marvelous beauty

options.world_noticed_signal: The brook murmured on as if such beauty were an everyday matter in that country. The door of the little house framed the wonder like a picture.

options.label: B
options.text: You look at the brook, the fruits, and the flowers as if they might help you live here.
options.outcome: Your amazement was great, but it was mixed at once with practical noticing. You saw the brook and thought of water, the fruit trees and thought of food, the soft grass and thought how unlike all this was to the dry, gray prairie. In that way the beauty of Oz did not remain only beauty. It became also a place that might hold a little girl alive, at least for a time. This did not make you less homesick, but it made the strange land more legible. Toto sniffed eagerly at the threshold, and his readiness to inspect the place made it seem, in a small way, more inhabitable than before.
options.tonal_effect: Narration colors wonder with practical inventory and survivable detail.

emotional_ledger_entries: secrets_discovered +1: first practical reading of Oz's landscape

options.world_noticed_signal: The bright country looked not only beautiful but useful, with water to drink and fruit to gather. Even wonder had solid things in it.

options.label: C
options.text: You laugh aloud because the world has turned so strange and so pretty at once.
options.outcome: The sight was so sudden and so pretty that a laugh came from you before any careful thought could stop it. It was a merry little sound, and in that bright place it did not seem at all out of order. The green banks, the flowers, the birds, and the sparkling brook were too queerly unlike Kansas to be met with solemnity alone. So your wonder leaped first and asked questions afterward. Toto sprang out with lively feet, as if your laughter had given him leave to trust the place. Then the country of Oz, for all its strangeness, seemed less like a dream and more like a marvelous adventure that had truly opened its door to you.
options.tonal_effect: Narration becomes lighter, more buoyant, and gently comic in its acceptance of marvel.

emotional_ledger_entries: secrets_discovered +1: embraced first wonder of Oz openly

options.world_noticed_signal: The birds did not stop singing at your laugh, but seemed almost to answer it. Toto bounded across the threshold as if the whole bright country had invited him.

emotional_choices.all_paths_arrive_at: The Munchkins and the Witch of the North approach the house.

emotional_choices.source_moment: Dorothy sees the silver-shod feet beneath the house and realizes someone has died.
emotional_choices.narrative_lead_in: The little old woman pointed, and there under the beam were two feet, shod in silver shoes with pointed toes. The bright country did not look quite so simple after that.
emotional_choices.choice_question: How does the shock of it strike you?

options.label: A
options.text: You clasp your hands and cry out at once that this is dreadful.
options.outcome: You clasped your hands and cried out, for it was indeed dreadful to think that your house had fallen upon anyone, wicked witch or no wicked witch. The sorrow of it came to you before the explanation did, and that was much to your credit. The old woman remained calm, but the Munchkins, seeing your distress, looked upon you with greater kindness. They understood then that whatever strange power had arrived with the cyclone, it had not brought a hard heart with it. So the silver shoes became not only a prize from a dead witch, but a troublesome gift laid before a child who had never wished to harm anyone in all her life.
options.tonal_effect: Narration grows morally direct and sympathetic, naming dismay plainly.

npc_dispositions.shift: Sees Dorothy's innocence and compassion more clearly.
East-country people read Dorothy as tender-hearted rather than triumphant.

emotional_ledger_entries: crimes_committed_or_witnessed +1: witnessed accidental death of the Wicked Witch of the East
acts_of_mercy +1

options.world_noticed_signal: The Munchkins' gratitude did not cease, but it became softer in the face of your distress. Even the silver shoes looked less bright for a moment.

options.label: B
options.text: You master yourself and ask what can be done now.
options.outcome: The sight shocked you greatly, but after the first little cry you tried to master yourself and ask what was to be done. This did not mean you felt less horror. It meant only that practical questions came quickly to your lips when trouble stood before you. The old woman answered that there was nothing to be done, and because she spoke so calmly you were forced to see that in Oz even dreadful things could be handled with plain words. The Munchkins seemed comforted by your effort to be sensible. Thus your dismay did not vanish, but it was put into order, and the strange business of the silver shoes could go forward without making you cruel or wild.
options.tonal_effect: Narration keeps sorrow visible but moves briskly toward practical consequence.

npc_dispositions.shift: Respects Dorothy's composure under moral shock.

emotional_ledger_entries: crimes_committed_or_witnessed +1: witnessed accidental death of the Wicked Witch of the East
key_successes_failures +1: contained panic and sought next steps

options.world_noticed_signal: The old woman's calm answer seemed to steady the air around the house. The bright land remained bright, though now it held grave things as well as pretty ones.

options.label: C
options.text: You stare at the silver shoes and try to understand what sort of country this is.
options.outcome: You looked from the dead witch's feet to the silver shoes and then to the faces of the Munchkins, trying to make sense of a country where a wicked ruler might be mourned by no one and yet her death still trouble a good little girl. In that moment your dismay became mixed with wondering thought. The shoes were plainly important, though no one seemed to know exactly why, and the old woman's calmness made the whole matter stranger still. So you did not only pity the dead. You also began to understand that Oz was a place with rules of its own, and that if you meant to cross it you must learn them quickly.
options.tonal_effect: Narration turns dismay into inquiry, emphasizing Oz's moral and magical strangeness.

emotional_ledger_entries: crimes_committed_or_witnessed +1: witnessed accidental death of the Wicked Witch of the East
secrets_discovered +1: noticed significance of the silver shoes

options.world_noticed_signal: The silver shoes drew the eye even while the sight beneath them troubled the heart. Oz seemed, all at once, a country of bright things and grave meanings together.

emotional_choices.all_paths_arrive_at: The Witch of the East vanishes, and the silver shoes are given to Dorothy.

emotional_choices.source_moment: The Witch's cap turns to a slate and gives Dorothy a plain instruction toward Emerald City.
emotional_choices.narrative_lead_in: The little old woman balanced the point of her cap upon the end of her nose and counted, “One, two, three.” At once the cap changed to a slate, and on it was written in big white letters where you must go.
emotional_choices.choice_question: How does hope first return to you?

options.label: A
options.text: You ask at once where the City of Emeralds is and how to reach it.
options.outcome: Hope came back to you in the form of questions, which was very natural, for a road is most useful when one knows where it begins and where it leads. So instead of weeping longer, you dried your eyes and asked where the City of Emeralds was, who ruled it, and how you might get there. The old Witch answered plainly, and each answer gave your hope a little more body. It was no longer merely a wish to go home. It had become a direction, a distance, and a name. Even the strange slate seemed less like a wonder then and more like a useful signpost, which is often the best kind of magic.
options.tonal_effect: Hope is rendered as practical clarity and forward orientation.

npc_dispositions.shift: Approves Dorothy's quick turn from tears to practical inquiry.

emotional_ledger_entries: promises_made +1: turned toward Emerald City as actionable hope

options.world_noticed_signal: Your tears dried more quickly once the city had a place and the road had a name.

options.label: B
options.text: You hold fast to the message as if a written answer were something to trust.
options.outcome: The words upon the slate comforted you greatly, because they were plain and settled, and did not vanish like a dream. “LET DOROTHY GO TO THE CITY OF EMERALDS” was not home, to be sure, but it was instruction, and instruction is a fine thing for the lonely. So you took heart from the very firmness of the message. Someone, or something, in this strange land had answered your trouble directly. The old Witch's magic no longer seemed only queer. It seemed kindly and orderly besides. Thus hope returned to you as trust in the road that had been named before you.
options.tonal_effect: Hope is framed through order, instruction, and confidence in given guidance.

npc_dispositions.shift: Feels more confirmed as Dorothy's rightful guide.

emotional_ledger_entries: promises_made +1: accepted the slate's instruction in spirit

options.world_noticed_signal: The white letters seemed very sure of themselves, and their certainty steadied your own heart.

options.label: C
options.text: You cannot help smiling at such a queer and useful piece of magic.
options.outcome: The cap upon the nose, the solemn counting, and then the slate with its big white letters were so queer and yet so useful that a smile came to you through your tears. It was a gentle little smile, but it mattered. For once you had smiled, the City of Emeralds no longer seemed only far away and fearful. It seemed also possible. The absurdity of the magic did not lessen its goodness. Indeed, it almost made the answer kinder, as if this strange land wished to comfort you in a way that was both marvelous and plain. So hope returned with a touch of amusement, which is often a brave thing in sorrow.
options.tonal_effect: Hope is colored by shy amusement and gentle comic relief.

emotional_ledger_entries: defining_moral_choices +1: allowed wonder and humor to soften grief

options.world_noticed_signal: The Munchkins wept less when they saw you smile. Even the cap, now only a slate again, seemed to have done a kind office.

emotional_choices.all_paths_arrive_at: Dorothy asks the Witch of the North about Oz, the city, and the road.

posture_shifts.placement: Early Setup: cyclone scramble in the Kansas house, with Aunt Em below and the floor beginning to shake.
posture_shifts.narrator_line: The wind cried around the little house, and your body answered before your thoughts were all in order.

options.label: A
options.text: You move in a frightened hurry.
options.narration_adjustment: Your hands were quick, but not always steady. The room seemed full of sharp corners and sudden noises, and every second felt too small.

options.label: B
options.text: You set your teeth and move briskly.
options.narration_adjustment: You were frightened, but your fear went into your feet instead of stopping them. One thing followed another as quickly as you could manage.

options.label: C
options.text: You fix on Toto and nothing else.
options.narration_adjustment: The cellar, the wind, and even Aunt Em's cry grew dim beside the one little life you meant to keep near you. Such narrow focus can be dangerous, but it can also be very faithful.

posture_shifts.placement: Mid Setup: just before or during the reach toward Toto under the bed.
posture_shifts.narrator_line: Toto's black eyes shone from the shadow under the bed, and your hand went after him with its own meaning.

options.label: A
options.text: You coax him as gently as you can.
options.narration_adjustment: Your voice dropped at once into the tone reserved for frightened little creatures. Even in haste, you tried kindness first.

options.label: B
options.text: You snatch for him before the house can shake again.
options.narration_adjustment: There was no time, you thought, for coaxing and delay. Love in you took the shape of speed.

posture_shifts.placement: Early Escalation: in the dark airborne house after the first violent whirls.
posture_shifts.narrator_line: After a little while the swaying of the house grew queerly regular, and you might either notice it or hide from it.

options.label: A
options.text: You notice how the room rocks.
options.narration_adjustment: The motion was still dreadful, but you began to mark its pattern. Once a thing has a pattern, it is less like pure terror.

options.label: B
options.text: You shut your eyes against it all.
options.narration_adjustment: The dark outside was enough without measuring every sway within. So you drew inward and let the house carry you for a time.

posture_shifts.placement: Mid Escalation: Dorothy opens the door and first beholds the marvelous country of Oz.
posture_shifts.narrator_line: The bright country spread before you in green, blue, red, and gold, and your face could not help but answer it somehow.

options.label: A
options.text: You lean forward to see everything.
options.narration_adjustment: Your eyes went greedily from brook to flower to fruit tree. The world had turned beautiful, and you meant to look your fill.

options.label: B
options.text: You pause at the threshold first.
options.narration_adjustment: The beauty was so great that it checked your feet for a moment. You entered wonder as one enters a fine room, not noisily but with care.

posture_shifts.placement: Mid Escalation: the Munchkins call Dorothy a sorceress and bow before her.
posture_shifts.narrator_line: Their little bells tinkled, and all their eyes were upon you, as if you were greater than you knew how to be.

options.label: A
options.text: You shrink a little from the title.
options.narration_adjustment: You were not used to bows and grand names, and they sat oddly upon you. The white frock and little house behind you felt more real than sorcery did.

options.label: B
options.text: You stand politely and let them speak.
options.narration_adjustment: Though confused, you did not break the form of the meeting. Sometimes politeness carries one safely across the first bridge of strangeness.

posture_shifts.placement: During Breath beat: the Witch balances her cap on her nose and counts until it becomes a slate.
posture_shifts.narrator_line: The old Witch's cap stood upon her nose in so solemn and foolish a way that even sorrow had to make room for some new expression.

options.label: A
options.text: You smile in spite of yourself.
options.narration_adjustment: The sight was too queer to meet with tears alone. Your smile came softly, but it made the world friendlier at once.

options.label: B
options.text: You watch very solemnly.
options.narration_adjustment: You gave the magic your full attention, as if every number counted might matter to your future. In a way, it did.

options.label: C
options.text: You blink, puzzled but hopeful.
options.narration_adjustment: You did not pretend to understand such business, yet you could see it meant help of some kind. That was enough to keep your eyes fixed upon it.

posture_shifts.placement: Late Twist: after learning the deserts block every border and Oz may be the only hope.
posture_shifts.narrator_line: The word Kansas pulled at your heart, but there were still questions to ask before tears could be of much use.

options.label: A
options.text: You ask the next question at once.
options.narration_adjustment: Loneliness did not vanish, but you put it to one side long enough to seek the next useful fact. That is often how courage begins in a child.

options.label: B
options.text: You let the homesickness show plainly.
options.narration_adjustment: Your sorrow was simple and honest, and no one there mistook it. The bright land of Oz could not keep Kansas from aching in your heart.

posture_shifts.placement: Late Resolution: the Witch has kissed Dorothy, vanished, and the yellow brick road lies ahead.
posture_shifts.narrator_line: The shining mark was on your forehead, Toto was at your heels, and the yellow brick road waited to learn what sort of traveler you would be.

options.label: A
options.text: You square yourself and look ahead.
options.narration_adjustment: You were still a little girl far from home, but you stood as straight as you could. The road looked long, yet not impossible.

options.label: B
options.text: You glance back once at the house.
options.narration_adjustment: The little house had brought you into trouble, but it was still your house from Kansas. Looking back at it made the road ahead feel lonelier and dearer both at once.

present_npcs.current_disposition: Frightened, urgent, wholly focused on getting Dorothy below.
Already running for the stock; absent from direct interaction.

scene_rules_layer_4.character_knowledge_limits: No one knows the house will rise into the air; Aunt Em knows only the cellar is safety.
scene_rules_layer_4.emotional_context: Alarm, divided loyalty, bodily urgency.
canon_boundaries_active: Cyclones can lift Dorothy's house into the air while the center remains relatively still.
Dorothy cannot simply escape once the house begins its lift.
Toto is a real little dog and cannot speak or reason abstractly.
freeform_risk_areas: Trying to stop the cyclone by ordinary means
Keeping Aunt Em in the room too long
Leaving Toto permanently behind
Inventing alternate exits or modern safety logic

scene_rules_layer_4.character_knowledge_limits: Dorothy does not know where she is or whether the house will fall; she can only observe the motion and endure it.
scene_rules_layer_4.emotional_context: Fear softening into practical endurance and tenderness.
canon_boundaries_active: The house remains airborne and relatively rideable in the cyclone's center.
The trap incident occurs but Toto survives.
No outside rescue or communication is possible.
freeform_risk_areas: Opening the house to the storm mid-flight
Jumping from the airborne house
Turning the cyclone into ordinary weather
Giving Dorothy knowledge of Oz before arrival

present_npcs.current_disposition: Curious, lively, still close to Dorothy.

scene_rules_layer_4.character_knowledge_limits: Dorothy knows nothing of Oz; the Witch knows the geography of Oz but not Kansas; the Munchkins know local bondage and the silver shoes' importance only dimly.
scene_rules_layer_4.emotional_context: Wonder, embarrassment, moral dismay, and social uncertainty.
canon_boundaries_active: Dorothy has accidentally killed the Wicked Witch of the East by landing on her.
The Witch of the North is good and cannot send Dorothy home herself.
The silver shoes pass to Dorothy.
freeform_risk_areas: Refusing the silver shoes permanently
Reversing the death of the Wicked Witch of the East
Making the Witch of the North sinister or withholding
Letting Dorothy already know how Oz works

present_npcs.current_disposition: Watchful, ready to bark at strange magic.

scene_rules_layer_4.character_knowledge_limits: The Witch can interpret the magic instruction but still cannot replace Oz; Dorothy does not yet know the road in detail.
scene_rules_layer_4.emotional_context: Grief turning into gentle comic wonder and first hope.
canon_boundaries_active: The cap becomes a slate with the message 'LET DOROTHY GO TO THE CITY OF EMERALDS'.
Humor remains gentle and sincere, not snide.
Hope must become actionable through plain instruction.
freeform_risk_areas: Overcomplicating the magic with opaque lore
Turning the beat sarcastic or absurdist in a modern way
Having the slate give a different destination

scene_rules_layer_4.character_knowledge_limits: Dorothy knows Oz is the only named hope and that the road leads to Emerald City; she does not know whom she will meet next.
scene_rules_layer_4.emotional_context: Loneliness, commitment, and threshold courage.
canon_boundaries_active: The Witch of the North kisses Dorothy for protection.
The road to the City of Emeralds is paved with yellow brick.
The Witch disappears and does not accompany Dorothy.
The session ends before the Scarecrow scene properly begins.
freeform_risk_areas: Trying to make the Witch escort Dorothy
Resolving the next session's companion encounter now
Using the silver shoes knowingly for transport before Glinda's explanation
Leaving the road entirely before the session close

paths.label: A
paths.immediate_effect: At the trap door Dorothy's hand catches the iron ring, but Toto's frightened barking comes thinly from under the bed; when the floor heaves, she must leave the open trap and clutch the table edge instead of reaching the ladder.
paths.current_session_echo: Cyclone Cabin Aftermath scene: once the house is steady in the sky, Toto stays a little way off at first and must be called back from under the chair before he curls against Dorothy's skirt.
paths.next_session_payoff: Scarecrow Pole scene: when the Scarecrow asks why she came to Oz, Dorothy first says, "I tried to do as I was told, but Toto would not come," which frames her as dutiful before tender-hearted.
paths.later_session_legacy: Balloon Field scene in Session 4: when Toto runs after the cat, the narrator explicitly recalls the cellar moment, and Dorothy's failure to leave him a second time lands as a repeated sacrifice rather than a sudden impulse.
paths.defining_line_captured: You tried to be good first, and brave afterward.

consequence_visibility.what_changes: Dorothy's first quiet interaction with Toto after the lift carries a brief distance and recall of the missed ladder.
consequence_visibility.how_player_sees_it: Toto hangs back, whines, and only comes when Dorothy kneels and calls; the narration names the open trap she nearly reached.

npc_shifts.direction: warmer in remembered self-conception of obedience

world_state_delta.emotional_ledger_entry: defining_moral_choices +1; sacrifices_made +1

resets: Aunt Em live-scene reaction availability clears at episode boundary because she is absent from Oz scenes.
persists: Archive memory that Dorothy obeyed first under danger persists across the full story.
Lawful tendency toward instruction-following persists for later authority scenes.
escalates: Repeated obedience-first choices make gatekeepers and orderly helpers more forthcoming.
Repeated sacrifice of immediate advantage for Toto compounds the moral continuity of the balloon failure.

paths.label: B
paths.immediate_effect: Dorothy gets Toto into her arms before the room throws her down; the player sees him trembling against her dress while she lands hard on the floor with no clean path left to the cellar.
paths.current_session_echo: Cyclone Cabin Meal-and-Water scene: when Dorothy steadies herself after the first fright, Toto remains in her lap while she looks about the room, and the narration notes that she saved him without ever reaching safety herself.
paths.next_session_payoff: Scarecrow Pole scene: Dorothy's first approach is cautious but kind, and the Scarecrow's opening line lands against a Dorothy already established as one who bargains for both safety and care.
paths.later_session_legacy: Oz Reveal scene in Session 4: Dorothy's questioning of Oz plays as consistent balance rather than rebellion, because she has already shown she tries to save affection and prudence together.
paths.defining_line_captured: You bargained with danger for one more second.

consequence_visibility.what_changes: Toto is already secured in Dorothy's arms during the airborne-house beat, changing her physical posture and the emotional tone of the scene.
consequence_visibility.how_player_sees_it: Narration shows Dorothy seated on the floor with Toto clutched close instead of scrambling after him again.

world_state_delta.emotional_ledger_entry: defining_moral_choices +1; npcs_helped_or_harmed +1

resets: Immediate cyclone-floor physical staging clears at episode boundary.
persists: Archive memory that Dorothy protected Toto while still trying to act sensibly persists across the full story.
Neutral tendency toward balancing care and practicality persists.
escalates: Repeated balanced choices tilt narration toward Dorothy as practical and tender at once.
Companion trust rises faster when Dorothy repeatedly chooses care without grand speeches.

paths.label: C
paths.immediate_effect: Under the bed Dorothy catches Toto in both arms before the house gives its great leap, and when she is flung across the room the player sees dust on her face and Toto held fast against her breast.
paths.current_session_echo: Cyclone Cabin Window-Looking scene: Toto never leaves Dorothy's body for the rest of the airborne sequence, and the narration plainly says the cellar was lost the moment she chose him.
paths.next_session_payoff: Scarecrow Pole scene: if the player hesitates at the talking figure, Dorothy's narration already leans toward helping the strange at once, because she has established loyalty over procedure.
paths.later_session_legacy: Balloon Field scene in Session 4: Toto's chase and Dorothy's turn back for him become a direct rhyme with this first rash rescue, making the missed escape feel inevitable and earned.
paths.defining_line_captured: You would not save yourself by leaving him behind.

consequence_visibility.what_changes: Dorothy's body position, dust-covered appearance, and uninterrupted hold on Toto mark a more reckless rescue path.
consequence_visibility.how_player_sees_it: The narration shows her crawling from beneath the bed, then being thrown with Toto still pressed to her, instead of pausing at the trap.

world_state_delta.emotional_ledger_entry: defining_moral_choices +1; npcs_helped_or_harmed +1; sacrifices_made +1

resets: Dust-under-the-bed physical detail clears after the cyclone sequence.
persists: Archive memory that Dorothy chose loyalty over obedience persists across the full story.
Chaotic tendency toward feeling-first action persists for later humbug-piercing and rescue scenes.
escalates: Repeated loyalty-over-order choices make later abandon-or-delay dilemmas more morally charged.
Toto-centered sacrifices accumulate into stronger symbolic framing at the balloon failure and homecoming.

paths.label: A
paths.immediate_effect: The Witch of the North stops the bowing ceremony and answers Dorothy point by point, first showing the feet beneath the house and then naming the Munchkins, the dead Witch, and the silver shoes in orderly sequence.
paths.current_session_echo: East Cottage Supper Invitation scene: Boq's welcome includes a practical warning line, "The road is good at first, but not all the country is so easy," instead of mere praise.
paths.next_session_payoff: Scarecrow Conversation scene: Dorothy asks, "How came you to be upon that pole?" before offering help, and the Scarecrow responds with easy explanation because her question-first habit is already established.
paths.later_session_legacy: Oz Audience and Oz Reveal scenes in Session 4: Dorothy's request for plain answers lands as long-standing method rather than sudden suspicion, and Oz's humbug nature is surfaced through her continued questioning.
paths.defining_line_captured: You would be grateful later, but first you meant to understand.

consequence_visibility.what_changes: The welcome shifts from ceremony to explanation, with the Witch answering several direct questions in sequence.
consequence_visibility.how_player_sees_it: Dialogue changes immediately: the Witch names facts one by one, and the Munchkins stop bowing to listen.

npc_shifts.direction: more respectful of Dorothy's practical questioning

world_state_delta.emotional_ledger_entry: defining_moral_choices +1; secrets_discovered +1

resets: Witch of the North live-scene explanatory warmth clears after Session 1 because her persistence scope is SESSION_RESET.
Boq's supper-scene ceremonial staging clears after Session 1.
persists: Archive memory that Dorothy sought truth before accepting titles persists across the full story.
Trust-vs-caution tendency toward practical inquiry persists.
escalates: Repeated question-first choices make hidden mechanisms and plain explanations surface earlier in later sessions.
Companions become more candid when Dorothy repeatedly invites explanation rather than pageantry.

paths.label: B
paths.immediate_effect: At Dorothy's blunt refusal of the title, the old Witch laughs aloud, and the nearest Munchkins lower their bowed heads just enough to look at her as a little girl rather than a grand sorceress.
paths.current_session_echo: East Cottage Supper Invitation scene: Boq greets Dorothy with less ceremony and says, "You are welcome in my little house," instead of piling up honorifics.
paths.next_session_payoff: Scarecrow Pole scene: when the Scarecrow first speaks, Dorothy answers him more directly than fearfully, and his oddity is treated as another queer fact rather than a sacred marvel.
paths.later_session_legacy: Session 4 Oz Reveal scene: Dorothy's exposure of humbug resonates strongly, because she has been resisting borrowed grandeur since the first hour in Oz.
paths.defining_line_captured: You would not wear a crown merely because it was offered.

consequence_visibility.what_changes: The ceremonial mood breaks, and the Munchkins' awe softens into easier friendliness.
consequence_visibility.how_player_sees_it: The Witch laughs kindly, the bells on the blue hats quiet, and Boq later drops some of his grander phrasing.

world_state_delta.emotional_ledger_entry: defining_moral_choices +1; secrets_discovered +1

resets: Munchkin crowd ceremonial posture clears at the next episode boundary.
persists: Archive memory that Dorothy rejected false grandeur persists across the full story.
Chaotic trust-vs-caution tendency toward puncturing ceremony persists.
escalates: Repeated title-resisting choices make fraudulent or theatrical authorities lose dignity faster.
Honest common folk warm more quickly when Dorothy repeatedly refuses grandeur.

paths.label: C
paths.immediate_effect: The Witch of the North answers in a smooth, orderly lesson, and the Munchkins bow lower after Dorothy's courtesy; the silver shoes are presented in a more formal hush, as though a proper transfer has been witnessed.
paths.current_session_echo: East Cottage Supper Invitation scene: Boq receives Dorothy at the threshold with a deeper bow and calls her "most welcome," and the meal feels like a little ceremony of safe lodging.
paths.next_session_payoff: Scarecrow Pole scene: Dorothy's first words to the talking figure are polite and careful, which makes the Scarecrow answer with immediate gratitude instead of needing extra coaxing.
paths.later_session_legacy: Guardian of the Gates scene in Session 2 and Oz audience scenes later: orderly authorities respond more readily to Dorothy's habit of courtesy and proper listening.
paths.defining_line_captured: You entered wonder by the front door, and not by force.

consequence_visibility.what_changes: The East-country welcome becomes more ceremonious, and the Witch's exposition comes as a calm formal instruction.
consequence_visibility.how_player_sees_it: The Munchkins bow lower, the Witch stands straighter, and Boq later uses more honorific language.

world_state_delta.emotional_ledger_entry: defining_moral_choices +1; npcs_helped_or_harmed +1

resets: Witch of the North live-scene protectiveness clears after Session 1 because her persistence scope is SESSION_RESET.

Dorothy's liberator reputation grows more ceremonious when paired with repeated modest behavior.

paths.label: A
paths.immediate_effect: The Witch of the North stops smiling and answers more gravely; instead of praising Oz's greatness, she says plainly that he is only the one power in the middle of the land who might know a road home.
paths.current_session_echo: Protective Kiss and Farewell scene: the Witch's final instruction is shorter and firmer, ending with "Go, then, and keep your eyes open," which is not said on the more trusting paths.
paths.next_session_payoff: Scarecrow First March scene: when the Scarecrow speaks admiringly of Oz, Dorothy answers, "Perhaps he is great, but I shall see for myself," giving the company an early humbug-piercing tone.
paths.later_session_legacy: Session 4 Oz Reveal scene: Dorothy's skepticism pays off in sharper, less stunned recognition of the little man behind the screen, and Oz grows defensive sooner in private talk.
paths.defining_line_captured: You would walk the road, but not kneel to it.

consequence_visibility.what_changes: The Witch's guidance becomes graver and less glowing, and Oz is framed as a test rather than a promise.
consequence_visibility.how_player_sees_it: Her dialogue loses some warm praise, and her farewell line explicitly cautions Dorothy to keep watch.

npc_shifts.direction: future reception more vulnerable to skepticism

world_state_delta.emotional_ledger_entry: defining_moral_choices +1; promises_made +1

paths.current_session_echo: Protective Kiss and Farewell scene: the Witch adds, "Tell your story honestly, and he may help you," and the kiss feels like a blessing laid upon an accepted errand.
paths.next_session_payoff: Scarecrow First March scene: Dorothy speaks of Oz with hopeful respect, and the Scarecrow joins her expectation, making the road feel like a proper quest under a named ruler.
paths.later_session_legacy: Session 4 Balloon and Oz scenes: disappointment at Oz's fraud lands harder, because Dorothy had truly made hope out of his title and the road's promise.
paths.defining_line_captured: You made hope out of directions.

consequence_visibility.what_changes: The road is narrated as a reassuring instruction, and the Witch responds with fuller comfort.
consequence_visibility.how_player_sees_it: Her dialogue softens, she gives a warmer blessing, and the road's image becomes bright and orderly in the narration.

paths.current_session_echo: Protective Kiss and Farewell scene: Dorothy repeats the road instructions to herself before stepping away from the house, showing she has turned fear into a little plan.
paths.next_session_payoff: Scarecrow First March scene: Dorothy asks the Scarecrow whether he has seen the road ahead and what lies near the cornfield, keeping travel dialogue practical from the first meeting.

paths.defining_line_captured: You would not be comforted until the map had edges.
