paths.immediate_effect: N/A — session ends on this choice
paths.current_session_echo: The session closes on a little silence, then on all three companions stepping nearer instead of away, so the corridor beat ends in chosen fellowship rather than command.
paths.next_session_payoff: Session 3 opens with each companion voicing his own reason for remaining: the Tin Woodman speaks of friendship, the Scarecrow of beginning together, and the Lion of shame at leaving Dorothy alone.
paths.next_session_opening: Tender and heavy. First image: the yellow road west lying outside the green city with no banner over it. First character: the Lion, close beside Dorothy but ashamed of his trembling. Immediate stakes: the company must walk into danger freely chosen, which makes every step more personal.
paths.later_session_legacy: This path most strongly deepens later voluntary loyalty, especially the Lion's protectiveness and the Tin Woodman's sorrowful devotion in captivity and farewell scenes.
paths.defining_line_captured: You opened your hand, and your friends chose to stay in it.

consequence_visibility.what_changes: The next session begins with voluntary declarations from companions instead of a single shared duty frame.
consequence_visibility.how_player_sees_it: Session 3 opens with the companions each stating why they came, and their language centers on friendship rather than Oz's bargain.

npc_shifts.direction: deeply moved by Dorothy's selfless honesty
loyalty strengthened because Dorothy granted freedom
protectiveness rises because Dorothy would not compel him

world_state_delta.emotional_ledger_entry: sacrifices_made: Refused to claim companions by right.; defining_moral_choices: Offered friends freedom before danger.

resets: The exact corridor silence ends at the session boundary.
persists: Each companion remembers that Dorothy released rather than commanded him.
duty_vs_refusal tendency records friendship-over-command as the basis of the western march.
escalates: Repeated freedom-granting choices make companions volunteer sacrifice more readily.
Repeated refusal-to-command choices deepen emotional rather than procedural loyalty.

reactivity_trigger_specs.condition: If S2_C1 path A was chosen.
reactivity_trigger_specs.default_behavior: The Lion follows Dorothy with ordinary gratitude after the poppy rescue.
reactivity_trigger_specs.triggered_behavior: On the first western hazard in Session 3, the Lion lets the Scarecrow move first and says, "You did right to save yourself," before he joins the defense.
reactivity_trigger_specs.narrative_execution: State the little difference plainly, and let the great beast hang back for one moment before kindness brings him on.

npc_reactions: Cowardly Lion shows a beat of distance before loyalty.
Scarecrow takes practical lead sooner.
environmental_details: The road scene feels orderly before it feels affectionate.
dialogue_variations: Lion line changes to a forgiving but cooler acknowledgment of Dorothy's earlier priority.

reactivity_trigger_specs.condition: If home_vs_companion_devotion has accumulated a home-leaning practical pattern including S2_C1 path A.
reactivity_trigger_specs.default_behavior: Dorothy's farewell to the Lion balances home and friendship evenly.
reactivity_trigger_specs.triggered_behavior: In Session 6, Dorothy speaks first of Kansas and then of the Lion, and the narrator notes that even in the scarlet poppies her mind ran toward the road home.
reactivity_trigger_specs.narrative_execution: Let the farewell begin with the plain pull of home, and only then turn back to the friend who must be left.

npc_reactions: Cowardly Lion accepts the parting with a little more dignity than hurt.
environmental_details: Kansas motifs enter the farewell paragraph earlier.
dialogue_variations: Dorothy's first farewell clause names home before companionship.

reactivity_trigger_specs.condition: If S2_C1 path B was chosen.
reactivity_trigger_specs.default_behavior: The Lion stays near Dorothy but does not take the first protective position unless danger is clear.
reactivity_trigger_specs.triggered_behavior: At the first western threat in Session 3, the Lion steps bodily before Dorothy and says, "You remembered me in the flowers, and I will remember it now."
reactivity_trigger_specs.narrative_execution: Make the gratitude visible in the Lion's great body before you put it into his plain speech.

npc_reactions: Cowardly Lion becomes physically protective earlier.
Tin Woodman reads the moment with quiet approval.
environmental_details: Dorothy is briefly shadowed by the Lion's mane and shoulders.
dialogue_variations: Lion gains a direct callback line to the poppy field.
affected_elements.available_options: Future hazard choices may include the Lion volunteering first.

reactivity_trigger_specs.condition: If mercy_vs_fear includes companion-loyalty beats and S2_C1 path B is present.
reactivity_trigger_specs.default_behavior: The Lion asks timidly whether he ought to stay and be king.
reactivity_trigger_specs.triggered_behavior: In Session 5, the Lion speaks more declaratively to the forest beasts and to Dorothy, as one who has long believed he was not forgotten when afraid.
reactivity_trigger_specs.narrative_execution: Show that courage has grown by inches, and let one of those inches come from being chosen in the flowers.

npc_reactions: Cowardly Lion sounds more kingly.
Forest beasts receive him with less coaxing.
environmental_details: The forest assembly feels less doubtful.
dialogue_variations: Lion's kingship speech loses one apologetic hedge.

reactivity_trigger_specs.condition: If S2_C1 path C was chosen.
reactivity_trigger_specs.default_behavior: The Tin Woodman protects Dorothy in his usual courteous way.
reactivity_trigger_specs.triggered_behavior: At the start of Session 3, the Tin Woodman explicitly includes Toto in his vow, saying that little creatures have the first claim upon kind hearts.
reactivity_trigger_specs.narrative_execution: Let the Tin Woodman's tenderness widen from the girl to the little dog beside her, for that is how his heart showed itself even before he had one.

npc_reactions: Tin Woodman becomes more openly affectionate toward Toto.
Dorothy is treated as caretaker of the helpless.
environmental_details: Toto is kept close and watched over in departure staging.
dialogue_variations: Tin Woodman gains a line linking kindness to little creatures.

reactivity_trigger_specs.condition: If S2_C1 path C was chosen and later Toto-loyalty beats remain consistent.
reactivity_trigger_specs.default_behavior: The homecoming emphasizes Dorothy and Kansas broadly.
reactivity_trigger_specs.triggered_behavior: In Session 6, the narration frames Toto as the little piece of Kansas Dorothy guarded even in Oz, sharpening the moral continuity of the return.
reactivity_trigger_specs.narrative_execution: Name the dog simply, and let the reader feel that home had black eyes and four feet long before the prairie came into sight again.

npc_reactions: Aunt Em's first notice may include Toto as well as Dorothy.
environmental_details: Domestic imagery gathers around Toto's presence.
dialogue_variations: Dorothy's farewell and return lines mention Toto more readily.

reactivity_trigger_specs.condition: If S2_C2 path A was chosen and Dorothy still possesses the Mouse Queen's Whistle.
reactivity_trigger_specs.default_behavior: The Queen of the Field Mice comes when called and gives the needed information.
reactivity_trigger_specs.triggered_behavior: In Session 4, the Queen arrives quickly, comes close without fear, and begins with a warm remembrance of Dorothy's courtesy before explaining the Golden Cap and the Winged Monkeys.
reactivity_trigger_specs.narrative_execution: Bring the little Queen in briskly, and let her gratitude speak in the same plain breath as the useful knowledge.

npc_reactions: Queen of the Field Mice is warm and ceremonious.
Her subjects cluster near Dorothy's shoes.
environmental_details: The mice appear orderly and unafraid.
dialogue_variations: Opening line recalls Dorothy's kindness in the grass.

reactivity_trigger_specs.condition: If Dorothy has two or more prior acts in the archive that honor small creatures, including S2_C2 path A or similar beats.
reactivity_trigger_specs.default_behavior: Small allies are helpful but reserved.
reactivity_trigger_specs.triggered_behavior: Later mouse or tiny-creature scenes become quicker to trust Dorothy, with less startle and more immediate willingness to gather, guide, or explain.
reactivity_trigger_specs.narrative_execution: Let the little creatures come out sooner each time, as if goodness had made a path through the grass before Dorothy's feet.

npc_reactions: Queen of the Field Mice and similar small allies show easier trust.
environmental_details: Grass and little pathways feel more welcoming in tiny-creature scenes.
dialogue_variations: Allies use friend-language sooner.
affected_elements.available_options: Future summons may skip one skepticism beat.

reactivity_trigger_specs.condition: If S2_C2 path B was chosen and Dorothy still possesses the Mouse Queen's Whistle.
reactivity_trigger_specs.default_behavior: The Queen responds with general gratitude.
reactivity_trigger_specs.triggered_behavior: In Session 4, the Queen frames her aid as the honoring of Dorothy's remembered promise and moves quickly to practical explanation without ceremonial flourishes.
reactivity_trigger_specs.narrative_execution: Keep the little Queen brisk and sensible, as if one honest bargain had simply led to the next honest help.

npc_reactions: Queen of the Field Mice is friendly but businesslike.
environmental_details: The mice move in efficient clusters rather than festive ones.
dialogue_variations: Opening line recalls Dorothy's word and promise.

reactivity_trigger_specs.condition: If S2_C2 path B was chosen.
reactivity_trigger_specs.default_behavior: The Scarecrow offers plans in his usual eager fashion.
reactivity_trigger_specs.triggered_behavior: In later planning beats, the Scarecrow cites Dorothy's good sense with helpers and is quicker to suggest exchanges, trades, or mutual-aid solutions.
reactivity_trigger_specs.narrative_execution: Let the Scarecrow reason from what he has seen, for he always proved cleverest when he forgot he had no brains.

npc_reactions: Scarecrow grows more confident in practical negotiation.
environmental_details: Planning scenes feel more orderly and solution-minded.
dialogue_variations: Scarecrow may say, "You see, a fair bargain often answers best."
affected_elements.available_options: Future expressive options may mention bargaining language.

reactivity_trigger_specs.condition: If S2_C2 path C was chosen and Dorothy still possesses the Mouse Queen's Whistle.
reactivity_trigger_specs.default_behavior: The Queen comes promptly and warmly when summoned.
reactivity_trigger_specs.triggered_behavior: In Session 4, the Queen still comes, but she pauses to ask whether the need is true before giving the Golden Cap explanation, and some mice remain a little farther back from Toto and Dorothy's shoes.
reactivity_trigger_specs.narrative_execution: Show the help arriving all the same, but keep a little space in the grass before trust closes it.

npc_reactions: Queen of the Field Mice is respectful but watchful.
Her subjects are slower to crowd near.
environmental_details: The mice keep a cautious ring before settling.
dialogue_variations: Opening line references Dorothy's former haste.

reactivity_trigger_specs.condition: If Dorothy repeatedly presses status, urgency, or magical signs upon weaker allies, including S2_C2 path C.
reactivity_trigger_specs.default_behavior: Small allies respond as simple friends once called.
reactivity_trigger_specs.triggered_behavior: Small allies still obey or assist, but they watch Dorothy first for gentleness, and their first spoken lines test need rather than assume friendship.
reactivity_trigger_specs.narrative_execution: Let the little creatures be brave enough to help and wise enough to remember the difference between kindness and command.

npc_reactions: Mouse Queen and similar allies become more cautious.
environmental_details: Tiny-creature scenes begin with more distance and less clustering.
dialogue_variations: Helpers ask why they are needed before offering warmth.

reactivity_trigger_specs.condition: If S2_C3 path A was chosen.
reactivity_trigger_specs.default_behavior: Emerald City attendants remain politely neutral as the party prepares to leave west.
reactivity_trigger_specs.triggered_behavior: At the opening of Session 3, Oz's servants bow correctly but offer no extra comfort, and the green halls feel more official than welcoming.
reactivity_trigger_specs.narrative_execution: Strip the palace of a little cheer, and let its politeness remain while its warmth goes out of it.

npc_reactions: Palace attendants are stiff and procedural.
environmental_details: Green corridors feel colder and less marvelous.
dialogue_variations: Servants speak in short formal lines.

reactivity_trigger_specs.condition: If S2_C3 path A was chosen and Oz is later exposed as a humbug.
reactivity_trigger_specs.default_behavior: Oz greets exposure with embarrassment and excuses.
reactivity_trigger_specs.triggered_behavior: When Dorothy confronts the revealed Oz in Session 4, he begins defensively, saying in effect that she had always judged him harshly, before he softens into confession.
reactivity_trigger_specs.narrative_execution: Let the great fraud shrink into a little old man, but not before he tries one last weak shelter of self-defense.

npc_reactions: Oz is more defensive before becoming candid.
environmental_details: The exposed chamber feels barer and more awkward.
dialogue_variations: Oz's first private line is more self-protective.

reactivity_trigger_specs.condition: If S2_C3 path B was chosen.
reactivity_trigger_specs.default_behavior: The company leaves for the West with dread but loose coordination.
reactivity_trigger_specs.triggered_behavior: Session 3 opens with the company already arranged for departure, the Scarecrow checking practical readiness and the Lion naming the promise aloud.
reactivity_trigger_specs.narrative_execution: March them out in good order, for once a hard promise had been spoken, one thing followed another plainly.

npc_reactions: Scarecrow becomes more directive.
Lion frames courage through promise-keeping.
environmental_details: Departure staging is orderly and prepared.
dialogue_variations: Lion says, "We have promised."

reactivity_trigger_specs.condition: If S2_C3 path B was chosen and Oz later deals privately with Dorothy.
reactivity_trigger_specs.default_behavior: Oz treats Dorothy as another disappointed petitioner after his exposure.
reactivity_trigger_specs.triggered_behavior: Oz speaks to Dorothy as someone who proved useful and obedient under pressure, which makes him a little quicker to recruit her help in practical schemes like the balloon.
reactivity_trigger_specs.narrative_execution: Let the old humbug remember usefulness as warmly as better men remember virtue.

npc_reactions: Oz is a little more cooperative in practical planning.
environmental_details: Private work scenes with Oz feel less adversarial.
dialogue_variations: Oz uses partner-like language sooner.

reactivity_trigger_specs.condition: If S2_C3 path C was chosen.
reactivity_trigger_specs.default_behavior: The western road is framed chiefly as dreadful duty.
reactivity_trigger_specs.triggered_behavior: Session 3 opens with the road west narrated as both danger and unanswered question, and companions speak of what they do not know about the Witch rather than only what they fear.
reactivity_trigger_specs.narrative_execution: Keep the road plain before them, but lay a little doubt upon it, as if even the yellow bricks could not answer every question.

npc_reactions: Scarecrow and Lion echo Dorothy's uncertainty.
environmental_details: The western gate feels watchful rather than merely ominous.
dialogue_variations: Companions ask what sort of wickedness awaits them.

reactivity_trigger_specs.condition: If S2_C3 path C was chosen and Oz's deception is later revealed.
reactivity_trigger_specs.default_behavior: Dorothy is surprised by the exposure of Oz.
reactivity_trigger_specs.triggered_behavior: Dorothy is still disappointed in Session 4, but the narration notes that she had already learned that great voices sometimes answer questions with commands instead of truth.
reactivity_trigger_specs.narrative_execution: Do not make her worldly; only let one plain lesson prepare her a little for the next.

npc_reactions: Scarecrow is quicker to accept the plain truth beside Dorothy.
environmental_details: The green apparatus reads as less shocking and more confirming.
dialogue_variations: Dorothy asks a clearer first question after the reveal.

reactivity_trigger_specs.condition: If S2_C4 path A was chosen.
reactivity_trigger_specs.default_behavior: Session 3 opens in immediate departure mode.
reactivity_trigger_specs.triggered_behavior: Session 3 opens in morning counsel, with the Scarecrow asking whether any other way may yet be found and the Lion showing relief that the march did not begin in darkness.
reactivity_trigger_specs.narrative_execution: Begin with morning and thought instead of footsteps, but let the waiting West stand outside the walls all the same.

npc_reactions: Scarecrow is thoughtful and active in counsel.
Lion is relieved but ashamed of his relief.
environmental_details: Emerald City morning feels borrowed and temporary.
dialogue_variations: Scarecrow opens with a plan-question instead of a departure statement.
affected_elements.available_options: Future expressive choices may include asking for more counsel before action.

reactivity_trigger_specs.condition: If S2_C4 path A was chosen and palace intermediaries report the delay.
reactivity_trigger_specs.default_behavior: Oz remains distant and unchanged before the western departure.
reactivity_trigger_specs.triggered_behavior: A palace functionary or attendant delivers a stiffer reminder that Oz's condition still stands, making the delay feel watched rather than private.
reactivity_trigger_specs.narrative_execution: Let authority return not with thunder but with a message, for even borrowed time may be counted by those who gave none of it.

npc_reactions: Palace servant becomes more procedural.
Scarecrow grows more anxious.
environmental_details: The green lodging feels less safe and more supervised.
dialogue_variations: A reminder line invokes Oz's standing command.

reactivity_trigger_specs.condition: If S2_C4 path B was chosen.
reactivity_trigger_specs.default_behavior: Session 3 opens with some lingering uncertainty among the company.
reactivity_trigger_specs.triggered_behavior: Session 3 opens at the western gate with the company already assembled, and each companion is physically prepared for travel because Dorothy settled the matter before the session break.
reactivity_trigger_specs.narrative_execution: Put them at the gate at once, and let the morning show that dread had already been made into action.

npc_reactions: Scarecrow is brisk.
Tin Woodman is steady.
Lion is fearful but present.
environmental_details: The gate and road are foregrounded immediately.
dialogue_variations: Opening lines concern readiness and departure.

reactivity_trigger_specs.condition: If Dorothy has accumulated two or more promises_made that she is actively keeping, including S2_C4 path B.
reactivity_trigger_specs.default_behavior: Companions support Dorothy with ordinary loyalty.
reactivity_trigger_specs.triggered_behavior: In later danger scenes, companions cite Dorothy's kept word and hold formation more firmly around her, especially when fear might otherwise scatter them.
reactivity_trigger_specs.narrative_execution: Let promise harden into habit, so that the company stands together almost before it thinks to do so.

npc_reactions: Scarecrow, Tin Woodman, and Lion show stronger collective steadiness.
environmental_details: Travel scenes feel more orderly and companioned.
dialogue_variations: Companions reference what was promised together.
affected_elements.available_options: Future group choices may begin from unity rather than persuasion.

reactivity_trigger_specs.condition: If S2_C4 path C was chosen.
reactivity_trigger_specs.default_behavior: Session 3 opens with the group bound chiefly by Oz's bargain.
reactivity_trigger_specs.triggered_behavior: Session 3 opens with each companion voicing his own reason for staying, so the western march is framed as chosen friendship rather than commanded duty.
reactivity_trigger_specs.narrative_execution: Let each friend speak in turn, and make the road heavier because nobody was forced upon it.

npc_reactions: Tin Woodman is tenderer.
Scarecrow is more personally loyal.
Lion is more protective.
environmental_details: The road west feels intimate rather than official.
dialogue_variations: Companions each declare a personal motive for continuing.
affected_elements.available_options: Future expressive choices may foreground friendship language.

reactivity_trigger_specs.condition: If S2_C4 path C was chosen, especially with prior Lion-loyalty beats.
reactivity_trigger_specs.default_behavior: The Lion follows Dorothy out of mixed fear and hope for courage.
reactivity_trigger_specs.triggered_behavior: The Lion becomes more protective in Session 3 because Dorothy refused to compel him, and he treats staying as his own brave act rather than her command.
reactivity_trigger_specs.narrative_execution: Give the Lion a little pride in his fear, for a freely chosen fright is nearer courage than a driven one.

npc_reactions: Cowardly Lion speaks with more personal resolve.
environmental_details: He keeps physically close to Dorothy on the road.
dialogue_variations: Lion says he stayed because he would be ashamed to leave her.
affected_elements.available_options: Future hazard options may have the Lion volunteer shielding actions sooner.

resets_between_episodes.element: Poppy-field bodily weakness and sleep-heaviness
resets_between_episodes.why: It belongs to the scarlet-flower hazard scene and should not linger as a physical debuff once the company has reached Emerald City.

resets_between_episodes.element: Immediate mouse-hauling logistics of cords, ranks, and field labor
resets_between_episodes.why: Those practical rescue details are scene-specific and end once the Lion is safely out of the flowers.

resets_between_episodes.element: Throne-room acoustic pressure and the Voice's immediate dread
resets_between_episodes.why: The chamber's performed terror belongs to the audience beat and should clear when the party leaves Oz's presence.

resets_between_episodes.element: Green-corridor pause before the western decision
resets_between_episodes.why: The corridor is a session-end staging space, not a lasting world condition.

persists_across_full_story.why: It directly shapes Session 6 farewell tone and how Dorothy balances Kansas against the friends of Oz.

persists_across_full_story.element: Queen of the Field Mice disposition and Mouse Queen's Whistle possession from S2_C2
persists_across_full_story.why: This governs Session 4 ally recall, information delivery, and Dorothy's long-term bond with small helpers.

persists_across_full_story.element: mercy_vs_fear stance toward Oz's bargain from S2_C3
persists_across_full_story.why: It colors Dorothy's later dealings with cruelty, false authority, and the Lion's confidence arc.
It determines the opening posture of Session 3 and the emotional basis on which the western campaign proceeds.

persists_across_full_story.element: promises_made and promises_kept_or_broken archive entries created in Session 2
persists_across_full_story.why: These are core relationship memory tracks that later companions and rulers can cite plainly.

persists_across_full_story.element: Dorothy's relationship shifts with Scarecrow, Tin Woodman, Lion, Oz, and the Queen of the Field Mice
persists_across_full_story.why: Named NPC memory is the main engine of visible reactivity across the remaining sessions.

escalates_with_accumulation.behavioral_pattern: Choosing companions or helpless creatures before speed, comfort, or clean escape

thresholds.at_2_instances: Companions speak more warmly and volunteer small protective acts without being asked.
thresholds.at_4_instances: The Lion and Tin Woodman become markedly more self-sacrificing, and farewell scenes gather stronger tenderness.
thresholds.at_6_or_more_instances: Dorothy is framed across the story as plainly tender-hearted, and allies repeatedly return help before she must ask twice.

escalates_with_accumulation.behavioral_pattern: Using courtesy and humility with small allies, rulers, or gatekeepers

thresholds.at_2_instances: Orderly or ceremonious helpers answer with easier civility and less testing.
thresholds.at_4_instances: Ally summons become faster, and explanatory NPCs offer fuller guidance with less resistance.
thresholds.at_6_or_more_instances: Dorothy acquires a broad reputation for good conduct, and lawful societies receive her with open ceremonial goodwill.

escalates_with_accumulation.behavioral_pattern: Pressing urgency, status, or command over ceremony when frightened

thresholds.at_2_instances: Weaker allies obey but show a visible first flinch or pause.
thresholds.at_4_instances: Small or formal helpers become watchful and ask Dorothy's purpose before offering warmth.
thresholds.at_6_or_more_instances: Dorothy is treated as bold but difficult, and aid still comes yet is increasingly cautious, conditional, or cool in tone.

escalates_with_accumulation.behavioral_pattern: Challenging false or cruel authority with plain truth

thresholds.at_2_instances: The narrator more readily strips splendor away to show the little true thing beneath it.
thresholds.at_4_instances: Authorities answer Dorothy more defensively, while honest companions trust her judgment more openly.
thresholds.at_6_or_more_instances: Illusions and performances lose their hold quickly around Dorothy, and humbugs find it hard to keep their grandeur before her.

escalates_with_accumulation.behavioral_pattern: Accepting hard duty and binding the company through promise

thresholds.at_2_instances: Companions fall into practical order more quickly when danger rises.
thresholds.at_4_instances: Promises become a visible moral force in dialogue, and companions cite Dorothy's steadiness as reason to endure.
thresholds.at_6_or_more_instances: Dorothy is treated as the road-making center of the company, and group scenes begin from cohesion rather than persuasion.

freeform_guidelines.narrator_behavior: Surface this path by noticing first the road, the next task, and the practical shape of rescue before turning back to feeling.
freeform_guidelines.spirit: Practical survival and onward motion matter even in tenderness.
hard_limits: Do not let Dorothy ignore the poppies without consequence.
Do not allow Dorothy to rescue the Lion by herself.
Do not let Scarecrow or Tin Woodman fail to remove Dorothy from the flowers first.
freeform_guidelines.fold_back_acknowledge: Dorothy may try to direct every detail of the rescue, and the narration should honor her clear wish to get free at once.
freeform_guidelines.fold_back_redirect: But sleep is already upon her, and the only hands that can act quickly are the Scarecrow's and the Tin Woodman's, who must carry her before anything else can be attempted.

freeform_guidelines.narrator_behavior: Surface this path by letting the Lion remember that Dorothy named him in danger, so his loyalty appears in body and speech before it is explained.
freeform_guidelines.spirit: A friend is not to be left behind merely because he is troublesome to save.
freeform_guidelines.freeform_alignment_input: aggressive/defiant for a friend's sake, protective, emotionally urgent
hard_limits: Do not let Dorothy remain in the poppies to die beside the Lion.
Do not permit immediate successful rescue of the Lion before Dorothy is carried out.
Do not give Toto speech or strategic agency.
freeform_guidelines.fold_back_acknowledge: Dorothy may insist that the Lion be saved before herself, and the narration should make plain that her heart ran to him first.
freeform_guidelines.fold_back_redirect: Yet the wicked perfume has already overcome her, so the others must save the little girl before they can return their strength to the great beast.

freeform_guidelines.narrator_behavior: Surface this path by tying Toto to home and helplessness whenever Dorothy's care is recalled.
freeform_guidelines.spirit: Faithfulness begins with the smallest dear creature already in one's arms.
freeform_guidelines.freeform_alignment_input: cautious/respectful, duty-bound, protective of the helpless
hard_limits: Do not let Dorothy abandon Toto in the flowers.
Do not let Toto act like a speaking familiar.
Do not replace the Lion's peril with Toto's death or disappearance.
freeform_guidelines.fold_back_acknowledge: Dorothy may clutch Toto and beg that nothing happen to him, and the narration should honor that faithful instinct.
freeform_guidelines.fold_back_redirect: But the companions can save both dog and girl only by bearing them together from the flowers before turning to the Lion.

freeform_guidelines.narrator_behavior: Surface this path by making little creatures come near Dorothy without fear and by giving the Queen warm ceremonial speech.
freeform_guidelines.spirit: Respect shown downward becomes strength returned upward.
hard_limits: Do not let Dorothy threaten or bribe the Queen with impossible magic.
Do not remove the mice as the canon rescue method.
Do not grant the whistle before the rescue gratitude beat.
freeform_guidelines.fold_back_acknowledge: Dorothy may bow, kneel, or speak with very great politeness, and the narration should reward that humility plainly.
freeform_guidelines.fold_back_redirect: The Queen still needs practical facts and time to summon her people, for courtesy does not change how many mice are required to move a Lion.

freeform_guidelines.narrator_behavior: Surface this path by having allies remember Dorothy's word as something solid and useful, like a tool kept clean in the basket.
freeform_guidelines.spirit: Fair exchange can be as honorable as pleading.
hard_limits: Do not let Dorothy offer future powers she does not possess.
Do not convert the scene into haggling comedy or cynicism.
Do not delay the Lion rescue beyond the canon flow.
freeform_guidelines.fold_back_acknowledge: Dorothy may try to make a plain bargain, and the narration should treat that as sensible rather than cold.
freeform_guidelines.fold_back_redirect: The Queen accepts only what a little girl can honestly promise, which is future friendship and aid, not grand rewards beyond her power.

freeform_guidelines.narrator_behavior: Surface this path by showing a first startle in the mice before obedience gathers under the Queen's steadiness.
freeform_guidelines.spirit: Fear can make even a good child use borrowed authority sharply.
hard_limits: Do not let the Silver Shoes perform new magic here.
Do not let Dorothy become cruel or mocking toward the mice.
Do not let the Queen submit as if she were foolish or servile.
freeform_guidelines.fold_back_acknowledge: Dorothy may speak sharply because the Lion is in deadly danger, and the narration should preserve the urgency of that fear.
freeform_guidelines.fold_back_redirect: Yet the mice are little living creatures and must be startled before they can be rallied, so command wins speed but not easy warmth.

freeform_guidelines.narrator_behavior: Surface this path by stripping grandeur from cruel authority and letting companions answer Dorothy's plain moral speech with visible respect.
freeform_guidelines.spirit: A wicked demand remains wicked even when spoken from a throne.
hard_limits: Do not expose Oz as a humbug in this scene.
Do not let Dorothy overturn the bargain here.
Do not turn the exchange into modern sarcasm or witty banter.
freeform_guidelines.fold_back_acknowledge: Dorothy may condemn the bargain in stronger words, and the narration should preserve her honest recoil from cruelty.
freeform_guidelines.fold_back_redirect: But the great Voice is still master of the audience chamber, and it answers contradiction only by standing upon its condition more coldly than before.

freeform_guidelines.narrator_behavior: Surface this path by making one thing follow another in sober order, so fear becomes a road because Dorothy has named it so.
freeform_guidelines.spirit: Duty may require walking a cruel road when no kinder road is open.
hard_limits: Do not let Dorothy become eager to kill.
Do not let Oz reward her immediately.
Do not soften the Witch errand into a harmless task.
freeform_guidelines.fold_back_acknowledge: Dorothy may accept the task with tears, trembling, or brave plainness, and the narration should honor the steadiness of that acceptance.
freeform_guidelines.fold_back_redirect: Yet acceptance cannot make the road less dreadful, and the company must still carry the full weight of what Oz has asked.

freeform_guidelines.narrator_behavior: Surface this path by letting questions linger in the green air after Oz has refused to answer them.
freeform_guidelines.spirit: Before blood is shed, truth ought to be asked for plainly.
hard_limits: Do not let Oz enter sincere moral debate.
Do not provide proof about the Witch beyond Oz's repeated command.
Do not let Dorothy discover the apparatus behind the Voice yet.
freeform_guidelines.fold_back_acknowledge: Dorothy may ask for reasons, proof, or a gentler way, and the narration should treat that as a sensible question.
freeform_guidelines.fold_back_redirect: But questions trouble those who prefer obedience, so Oz gives no answer beyond the bargain itself and leaves the doubt alive.

freeform_guidelines.narrator_behavior: Surface this path by opening the next session with morning counsel, borrowed time, and the West waiting outside the walls.
freeform_guidelines.spirit: Thought and delay may be mercy, but they do not cancel danger.
hard_limits: Do not let the party abandon the western quest entirely.
Do not let Oz revoke the bargain overnight.
Do not invent a new easy route home before Session 3 begins.
freeform_guidelines.fold_back_acknowledge: Dorothy may wish for one night's rest, more counsel, or another plan, and the narration should grant the human sense in that wish.
freeform_guidelines.fold_back_redirect: But morning comes all the same, and nothing in the green city has changed the plain fact that the West still lies before them.

freeform_guidelines.narrator_behavior: Surface this path by beginning the next session already at the gate, with companions prepared because Dorothy has made dread into duty.
freeform_guidelines.spirit: A hard promise can bind frightened hearts into one company.
hard_limits: Do not let the company rejoice at the errand.
Do not resolve the western campaign before it begins.
Do not separate the companions from Dorothy at the session break.
freeform_guidelines.fold_back_acknowledge: Dorothy may speak very firmly and bind the company to the road at once, and the narration should respect the steadiness of that choice.
freeform_guidelines.fold_back_redirect: Yet firmness cannot shorten the journey or sweeten the bargain, and so the next morning must still open upon the dreadful western gate.

freeform_guidelines.narrator_behavior: Surface this path by letting companions choose Dorothy aloud, so later loyalty feels freely given rather than owed.
freeform_guidelines.spirit: Love that is released and still remains is stronger than obedience.
hard_limits: Do not let any main companion actually leave the company here.
Do not permit Dorothy to command them away against canon.
Do not turn the beat into sentimental modern confession.
freeform_guidelines.fold_back_acknowledge: Dorothy may offer to go alone or release her friends from all claim, and the narration should honor the selfless honesty in that offer.
freeform_guidelines.fold_back_redirect: But these companions are already bound by affection and shared road, so freedom only makes them step nearer and choose her more plainly.

session_close_design.resolution_prose: At last you come through the green streets and under the high arches of the Palace, and you know the thing you set out to do has truly been done. You have crossed the rough country together. The deep ditches, the broad river, the dreadful Kalidahs, the deadly flowers, and the long road have not parted you. Instead they have made your little company fast friends. Now servants in green lead you to fine rooms, and the air is cool and sweet after the dusty road. The green light shines softly on polished floors, and the great doors close behind you with a gentle sound that means safety for this night. Then the soldier brings Oz’s answer, and it is plain enough. The Great Oz will see you, but only one by one, and only one each day. So your journey to the Emerald City is ended, and another sort of trial begins; for you have reached the place you sought, but you do not yet know what the Great Oz will ask of you.
session_close_design.hook_transition: For a little while, it is enough to be inside the Emerald City at last. But then you learn that only one of you may stand before Oz first, and the choice is not a small one.

session_end_choice.choice_question: When the morning comes, who shall go first to ask a favor of the Great Oz?

option_a.text: Dorothy goes first, because her longing for Kansas is the plainest and most pressing.
option_a.next_session_opens: Next session opens with Dorothy being dressed in green and led alone toward the Throne Room.

option_b.text: The Scarecrow goes first, because he has long desired brains and reasons that he may better advise the others afterward.
option_b.next_session_opens: Next session opens with the Scarecrow being summoned from his room to make his first audience with Oz.

option_c.text: The Lion goes first, because he is most afraid, and you judge it best to have the hardest thing done at once.
option_c.next_session_opens: Next session opens with the Lion being urged toward the Throne Room to face Oz before his courage fails.

session_end_choice.final_line: You have reached the green city; now you must decide who will speak first.

q1_entry_point.detail: The session starts at the first true playable crisis: Dorothy collapsing in the scarlet poppies. No passive runway precedes the danger.

q3_first_choice_timing.detail: Branching Choice #1 is reached within the cold open setup at approximately 170 words from the start, well under 300.

q4_consequential_choices.detail: S2_C4 fails consequence architecture against Phase 6 and cross-session planning. Its downstream payoff is only framed as Session 3 opening tone, but Q4 requires each branching choice to change what the story tracks with a named downstream payoff in a future session. Revision: in Phase 6, add at least one later-than-next-session payoff for duty_vs_refusal, such as how companions cite chosen duty vs chosen freedom in Session 5 or Session 6 farewell behavior.

q5_stakes_within_60_seconds.detail: Internal stakes are clear before the first branch: Dorothy wants to survive, protect Toto, and continue toward the Emerald City and home.

q6_early_decision_visible_later.detail: S2_C1, S2_C2, and S2_C3 all have named future-session payoffs, including Session 4 Mouse Queen aid, Session 5 Lion confidence, and Session 6 farewell tone.

q7_breath_beat.detail: The breath beat is placed at minutes 8-10 in Phase 3 beat map, but this checklist requires a breath beat between minutes 10-13. Revision: in Phase 3, shift or extend the wonder/comic release so the green-spectacles entry and first city marvel clearly function as the deliberate release in the 10-13 minute band before Oz's demand.

q8_moral_gray_area.detail: The moral-weight choice is S2_C3. Values in tension: mercy, duty to companions, homeward necessity. No option is framed as objectively correct.

q9_exit_emotional_state.detail: The complete session design contains a contradictory ending. Phase 3/5 correctly ends on the unresolved westward commitment choice, but Phase 7 session_close_design replaces it with a different hook about who sees Oz first. This produces confusion rather than clean anticipation. Revision: return to Phase 7 and align the session close with S2_C4 so the user exits on the unresolved decision to go west, delay, or release companions.

q10_talkability.detail: Choices such as naming Toto or the Lion first, challenging Oz's bargain, or releasing companions from obligation are immediately discussable.

q11_hard_ban_scan.detail: Banned element found: morally gray framing imported as design language and partially embedded in generated evaluative prose structure. Most critically, Q8 support relies on 'moral gray area' framing while the Voice Lock bans default moral ambiguity for Baum. Also Phase 7 resolution prose reintroduces cut material ('deep ditches, broad river, dreadful Kalidahs') as recap setup rather than brisk action cue. Revision: in Phase 7, remove the alternate close prose entirely and keep only Baum-aligned, direct moral framing around Oz's cruel bargain without gray-area terminology in player-facing prose.

q12_trailing_simile_scan.detail: Trailing similes appear in action/narrative lines: S2_C1A 'held you as carefully as if you had been made of glass'; S2_C2B 'mice moved smartly, like workers who understood both the need and the agreement'; S2_C4B 'as if the road had already begun beneath the green floor'; multiple posture/world-noticed lines use similar constructions. Revision: in Phase 5, replace action-line similes with plain declarative physical description.

q13_sentence_mold_scan.detail: No banned contrast molds or balanced modern scaffolds were found in the generated prose.

q14_voice_authenticity.detail: Cold open clearly carries Baum markers: plain marvel delivery, direct emotion naming, color motif saturation, and peril-then-practicality. Random outcomes also retain signature habits: sequential connective chaining in S2_C2A and S2_C3B, gentle comic deflation in S2_E2B, and question-answer/plain moral framing around Oz.

q15_fourteen_point_audit.detail: Not all 14 points pass. Flags cluster around Point 5 Moral Clarity, Point 7 Paragraph Architecture, and Point 11 Narrator Compliance because the Phase 7 alternate close contradicts the actual session spine and reads like a different chapter opening/closing unit.

q16_diction_fingerprint.detail: Dorothy's internal and spoken diction stays plain, courteous, and practical. Scarecrow, Tin Woodman, Lion, Guardian, Mouse Queen, and Oz remain vocally differentiated in Baum-compatible patterns.

fourteen_point_audit_results.location: Session-level framing conflict between Phase 3/5 westward moral bargain and Phase 7 alternate hook about audience order weakens the direct moral line.
Phase 7 resolution_prose functions as a separate chapter close for a different session and overloads recap, arrival, lodging, and new hook.
Phase 7 session_close_design contradicts the established session chronology and reads as an external editorial splice rather than the same narrator's continuous session.

q17_canon_integrity.detail: Phase 7 session_close_design breaks canon/session integrity by ending on 'who shall go first to ask a favor of the Great Oz?' after this design has already included the Oz audience and bargain. Revision: return to Phase 7 and replace the entire close package with the post-audience westward commitment hook from S2_C4.

q18_character_truth.detail: NPC behavior remains within StoryGuard character rules: Scarecrow practical, Tin Woodman tender, Lion ashamed yet loyal, Oz theatrical and transactional, Guardian formal, Mouse Queen ceremonious.

q19_world_reactivity.detail: World reactivity is visible through NPC memory, altered next-session openings, object-state handling for the Mouse Queen's Whistle and green spectacles, and tonal shifts tied to Dorothy's treatment of allies and authority.

q20_persistent_state_consistency.detail: Mouse Queen's Whistle state is inconsistent between Phase 5 and Phase 6. In S2_C2C Phase 5 it 'remains uncertain until gratitude is later shown,' but in Phase 6 S2_C2C world_state_delta it is definitively received after the Lion rescue. Revision: in Phase 5 and Phase 6, unify the whistle acquisition timing and possession state across all S2_C2 paths.

q21_world_noticed_signals.detail: Significant state changes generally receive subtle in-world acknowledgment in authorial voice: posture, distance, silence, clustering mice, stiffened attendants, and companion movement all signal remembered choice without gamey language.

q22_social_echo_defining_lines.detail: All four branching choices have three defining lines. They are in voice, under 20 words, provocative, and curiosity-generating.

q23_alignment_balance.detail: Alignment mapping is not consistently balanced to the schema's chaotic/lawful/neutral spread. Most seriously, S2_C1 labels Toto-first protection as LAWFUL and Lion-first companion rescue as CHAOTIC, but the session's own alignment labels in Phase 2 map Tender-Hearted mercy to neutral and Humbug-Piercing truth-challenge to chaotic. The register spread feels imposed rather than organically distinct. Revision: in Phase 5, recalibrate each branching choice so the three options cleanly express Yellow-Road Faithful / Tender-Hearted / Humbug-Piercing style differences without forcing compassion into lawful by default.

revision_instructions.instruction: Add a named later-session payoff for S2_C4 beyond Session 3 opening tone so all four branching choices have future-session consequence architecture.
Shift or extend the breath beat so a clear humor/wonder release lands in the 10-13 minute window before the Twist.
Delete the alternate close about who sees Oz first and align the session ending with S2_C4's unresolved westward commitment choice.
Remove the conflicting alternate close prose and keep only player-facing prose that preserves Baum's direct moral framing and session continuity.
Replace action-line similes in outcomes and world-noticed signals with plain declarative physical description.
Repair the session-close continuity so the full read passes Moral Clarity, Paragraph Architecture, and Narrator Compliance together.
Restore canon/session integrity by ending after Oz's bargain, not before the audience, and keep the hook on the westward decision.
Unify Mouse Queen's Whistle acquisition and possession state across S2_C2 path C in both Phase 5 and Phase 6.
Re-map branching options to the lawful/neutral/chaotic registers so each choice spans distinct alignment energies without misclassifying compassion by default.

entry_point_diagnosis.editorial_diagnosis: The first pages provided do not begin where Session 3's real dramatic energy lives. They open with aftermath and travel comfort: the Lion waking from the poppies, explanation of how he was saved, a pleasant walk, green country description, a farmhouse stop, and a long question-and-answer onboarding about Oz. Then the Emerald City arrival continues with more spectacle, etiquette, lodging, and palace procedure. All of that material is charming in the novel, but for an interactive session it delays the actual engine of this session: Oz's cruel bargain has already been made, and the party must decide whether to go west and face the Wicked Witch. That is the first moment when a new user can immediately feel the stakes from what is on the page: help will not be given unless a wicked ruler is confronted and killed. Everything before that is passive setup, scenic wonder, or deferred audience structure. Therefore the cut should begin at the group's resolution to seek the Witch. This preserves the session's true question, puts obligation and danger in the foreground at once, and lets worldbuilding be reintroduced through action as the company prepares, travels west, and is attacked.

format_specific_cut.cut_point: Event 104: Group resolves to seek the Witch
format_specific_cut.original_before_cut: Approximately 8 source-book pages of pre-cut material in the provided excerpt, plus Events 99-103 within this session
format_specific_cut.cut_eliminates: Recovery aftermath, travel transition, scenic description, Emerald City onboarding, question-answer exposition about Oz, palace hospitality, audience-delay procedure, and repeated report-backs from individual meetings with Oz
format_specific_cut.must_reintroduce: Dorothy wants to return to Kansas; Oz has promised each companion what they seek; Oz appears in different forms and has ordered that the Wicked Witch of the West must be killed first; the party is already formed and loyal; Emerald City's green pageantry can be referenced briefly only as contrast to the hard western road

entry_point_diagnosis.cold_open:
The axe-edge sings softly as you draw the whetstone down it, and the smell of oil clings to the morning air. Around you, your little company makes ready in silence, and no one speaks lightly now. The Scarecrow stands while fresh straw is pressed into him, and Toto's new bell gives a small, cheerful sound that does not cheer you at all.

Yesterday the Great Oz promised help. Today he asks for a wicked life in payment.

You check the bread in your basket, and then you look from the Lion to the Tin Woodman to the Scarecrow. Each has his wish set before him, and your own heart turns toward Kansas, Aunt Em, and the gray little house on the prairie. But the road west leads first to the land of the Winkies and the Wicked Witch who rules there.

Morning has come, the gate will soon be opened, and you must decide how you mean to go toward so terrible an errand.
entry_point_diagnosis.emotional_promise: The emotional promise of this cold open is: resolve. A user arrives feeling burdened and wanting to choose.

setup.source_moment: Event 104, using the diagnosed cut: the company prepares to leave Emerald City after Oz has demanded the death of the Wicked Witch of the West in exchange for help. Dorothy checks her basket, sees her companions making ready, and must decide how to go toward the western errand.
setup.why_it_qualifies: This is the first live dramatic tension of the session. The goal is already clear, the cost has been named, and the party stands at the threshold of action. It satisfies the cold-open rule because location, situation, and stakes can be established at once, and the first branching choice can arrive immediately as Dorothy defines her stance toward the quest.

escalation.source_moment: The westward journey and the Witch's first assaults: the party goes into the rough western country, the Witch uses the silver whistle to send wolves, crows, and bees, and then the Winged Monkeys break the company apart and carry Dorothy into captivity.
escalation.why_it_qualifies: This beat gives the session its visible goal and obstacle in the clearest possible form. The visible goal is to survive the road west and somehow fulfill Oz's demand so Dorothy may get home. The clear obstacle is the Wicked Witch's active, escalating resistance through servants, magic, and eventual separation of the party. The danger compounds practically, in Baum's manner, from travel into attack into capture.

breath.source_moment: Dorothy in the Witch's castle before the worst pressure closes in: the yellow Winkie country, the Witch's fear of the protective kiss, the absurdly practical chores, and Dorothy's secret kindness to the Lion despite captivity.
breath.why_it_qualifies: This is the best available release beat inside the source. It offers gentle comic deflation and wonder rather than safety: a terrible Witch who cannot touch the little girl because of a kiss-mark, a captive routine governed by sweeping and kitchen work, and the plain strangeness of feeding a great Lion in secret. It lands as a Schell-rule reward because the player gets a change of texture after attack-heavy escalation.

twist.source_moment: The confrontation over the Silver Shoe and the water-bucket reversal: the Witch steals one shoe, torments Dorothy, and Dorothy at last throws water in anger and destroys her.
twist.why_it_qualifies: This is the moral-weight center because Dorothy is not choosing between good and evil in abstract, but between submission, endurance, and sudden force under cruelty. There is no perfectly clean answer in player terms: yielding protects Dorothy briefly but concedes injustice; resisting risks punishment; throwing water is self-defense but also a killing act. It is the session's sharpest moment of assertion under pressure.

resolution.source_moment: After the Witch melts, Dorothy frees the Winkies, recovers her companions, takes the Golden Cap, and turns back toward Emerald City to claim Oz's promise.
resolution.why_it_qualifies: The primary arc appears resolved because the western task has been completed and the immediate tyrant has fallen. The seed for the next session is planted naturally: Dorothy now expects payment, but the recovered Golden Cap, the return journey, and the plain fact that Oz must finally answer create the handoff to the humbug-revelation session. The session should end on Dorothy's own unresolved decision about how she will return to Oz and what she means to demand there.

branching_choices.dramatic_question: As the company sets out west, do you frame this errand chiefly as the price of getting back to Kansas, as a duty shared with your companions, or as something each friend must freely choose?

When the western road proves truly dangerous and the Witch begins to strike at the company, do you press on under Oz's bargain, seek a safer and slower approach, or question whether the errand ought to be carried through at all?
When the Witch steals the Silver Shoe and torments you, do you answer with open resistance, outward obedience while waiting, or immediate desperate action?

Now that the Witch is dead and Oz's promise can be claimed, will you return to Emerald City ready to trust the Great Oz once more, to press him for plain truth, or to keep your doubts to yourself until you stand before him?

emotional_choices.source_attachment: Departure from Emerald City as the companions make ready for the western road.
First attacks by the Witch's servants, especially the wolves and crows, as the company realizes the Witch is already acting against them.
Dorothy secretly bringing food to the captive Lion in the Witch's yard.
Dorothy noticing the Witch's fear of the protective kiss and learning the practical rules of her captivity.
Dorothy seeing the Winkies beaten and ordered about, sharpening her moral anger before the final confrontation.

posture_shifts.player_attitude_exposed: Whether Dorothy notices her companions' fears and preparations practically or turns inward toward Kansas.

posture_shifts.placement: late Setup
posture_shifts.player_attitude_exposed: Whether Dorothy comforts the Lion and Scarecrow before departure.

posture_shifts.placement: mid Escalation
posture_shifts.player_attitude_exposed: Whether Dorothy meets the first attacks with stubborn courage or visible alarm.

posture_shifts.placement: mid Escalation
posture_shifts.player_attitude_exposed: Whether Dorothy asks practical questions about the Witch's power and the road rather than merely enduring events.

posture_shifts.placement: during Breath beat
posture_shifts.player_attitude_exposed: Whether Dorothy uses gentle cheer to hearten the captive Lion.

posture_shifts.placement: during Breath beat
posture_shifts.player_attitude_exposed: Whether Dorothy hides her homesickness and fear while under the Witch's eye.

posture_shifts.placement: early Twist
posture_shifts.player_attitude_exposed: Whether Dorothy tries to evade the Witch's demand for the Silver Shoe without open quarrel.

posture_shifts.placement: mid Twist
posture_shifts.player_attitude_exposed: Whether Dorothy's anger at cruelty rises into moral bluntness.

content_budget.narration_token_budget: Approximately 3,400-4,300 words of narrator output across the full session.
compression_targets: The departure logistics from Emerald City into the western road should be compressed into one setup sequence rather than a long travel preamble.
The successive attacks of wolves, crows, and bees should be chained briskly as one escalating hazard run, preserving Baum's step-by-step practicality without overplaying each as a full scene.
The Monkey assault and the separation of companions should be compressed into a decisive reversal sequence.
Winkie celebration and companion repair after the Witch's death should be handled as a compact recovery-and-liberation passage leading toward the return.
expansion_targets: The opening decision to go west, because it defines Dorothy's session identity and must land within the first 300 words.
Dorothy's captivity and secret feeding of the Lion, because it supplies the Breath beat and the compassion branch dimension.
The Silver Shoe confrontation and water-bucket reversal, because consequences must be designed before the moral-weight choice and this is the session's core turn.
The final return-facing choice after the Witch's death, because it is the retention hook into Session 4.

must_survive_verbatim: Oz's demand in essence: help will come only if the Wicked Witch of the West is killed.
Dorothy's plain moral protest in essence during the Witch confrontation.
The Witch's melting plea: 'What a world! What a world!' if that line is available in the adaptation source tradition; if not, preserve the plain melting panic in Baum-like directness.
Oz's title 'the Great and Terrible' should remain available in references leading into the next session.
can_be_reshaped: Companion travel talk on the western road.
The Witch's command dialogue to wolves, crows, bees, and Monkeys.
Dorothy and the Lion's secret captivity exchanges.
Winkie gratitude and practical repair dialogue after liberation.
can_be_cut: Extended scenic travel description not tied to hazard or choice.
Redundant attack beats if they repeat function without new agency.
Lengthy post-victory celebration details that delay the return-facing hook.

beat_map.moment: Cold open in Emerald City departure prep: oil, whetstone, bread basket, silent companions, and Oz's cruel price hanging over the morning.
beat_map.dramatic_function: Opening protocol orientation; expose whether the player looks first to home or to companions.

beat_map.moment: Dorothy must define how she means to go west before the gate opens.
beat_map.dramatic_function: Branching Choice 1 - Identity via home_vs_companion_devotion.

beat_map.moment: First visible consequence: companion tone and marching order shift according to Dorothy's stated motive.
beat_map.dramatic_function: Show consequence within first two minutes; softening or steadiness toward the company.

beat_map.moment: The western country opens rough before them, and the errand ceases to feel like palace talk.
beat_map.dramatic_function: End beat on COMPLICATION.

beat_map.moment: The Witch learns they are coming and sends the first servants against them.
beat_map.dramatic_function: Explore resolve versus fear as danger becomes active.

beat_map.moment: The company survives the first attacks by practical means, but each victory proves the Witch is watching.
beat_map.dramatic_function: Observation and intensifying responses to repeated peril.

beat_map.moment: Dorothy must decide how to continue now that the road has become a campaign.
beat_map.dramatic_function: Branching Choice 2 - Methodology via duty_vs_refusal.

beat_map.moment: The Witch escalates beyond animal servants and calls the Winged Monkeys; the company is broken apart.
beat_map.dramatic_function: Fear, helplessness, and loyalty under separation.

beat_map.moment: Dorothy arrives captive in the yellow castle and discovers the Witch cannot touch the mark upon her forehead.
beat_map.dramatic_function: Curiosity and wonder through plain magical rule explanation.

beat_map.moment: Captivity becomes absurdly practical: chores, watchfulness, and the strange safety of being feared by the Witch herself.
beat_map.dramatic_function: Joking or withdrawing under pressure; gentle comic deflation.

beat_map.moment: Dorothy steals a little time to feed the Lion in secret.
beat_map.dramatic_function: Tenderness and compassion under captivity.

beat_map.moment: The Breath beat closes as Dorothy sees the Winkies' bondage and feels the castle's cruelty more plainly.
beat_map.dramatic_function: Defiance begins to replace mere endurance.

beat_map.moment: The Witch schemes for the Silver Shoes and increases pressure around Dorothy.
beat_map.dramatic_function: Deflection and guarded resistance before the direct clash.

beat_map.moment: The Witch takes one Silver Shoe and Dorothy must answer the injustice.
beat_map.dramatic_function: Branching Choice 3 - Moral Weight via assertion_vs_submission.

beat_map.moment: Consequence sequence: quarrel, anger, water thrown, and the Witch melts away.
beat_map.dramatic_function: Immediate consequence and character assertion; end on DISCOVERY that the tyrant can be destroyed.

beat_map.moment: The Winkies rejoice, Dorothy takes the Golden Cap, and the practical work of freedom begins.
beat_map.dramatic_function: Primary arc resolution; peril turns into practicality.

beat_map.moment: Dorothy finds and restores her companions with the Winkies' help.
beat_map.dramatic_function: Softening and gratitude toward repaired companions.

beat_map.moment: The road back to Emerald City becomes visible again, and Oz's promise returns to the front of the story.
beat_map.dramatic_function: Escalation into next-session expectation.

beat_map.moment: Session-end hook: before turning east, Dorothy decides what posture she will carry back toward the Great Oz.
beat_map.dramatic_function: Branching Choice 4 - Future Commitment via truth_vs_comforting_illusion; unresolved end decision.

pressure_points.source_moment: Departure from Emerald City after Oz's bargain, with the company making ready in silence.
pressure_points.emotional_pressure: Resolve, dread, and protective companionship. The player wants to react to the burden before action begins.
pressure_points.micro_agency_without_diverting_spine: These posture shifts let Dorothy notice, comfort, or steel herself, but the road west still begins on schedule.

pressure_points.source_moment: The Witch's chained attacks on the road, culminating in the Monkey assault.
pressure_points.emotional_pressure: Threat and mounting helplessness. The player wants to lean in, intensify, or ask practical questions as each attack proves the Witch's reach.
pressure_points.micro_agency_without_diverting_spine: The shifts color Dorothy's courage and attention without changing the canon of escalating assaults and capture.

pressure_points.source_moment: Dorothy's captivity in the yellow castle, especially while secretly feeding the Lion.
pressure_points.emotional_pressure: Tenderness, secrecy, and absurdity. The player naturally wants to comfort, joke gently, or hide fear.
pressure_points.micro_agency_without_diverting_spine: These shifts deepen Baum-like companionship and comic practicality while keeping the captivity plot moving toward confrontation.

pressure_points.source_moment: The Silver Shoe quarrel and the moment just before the water is thrown.
pressure_points.emotional_pressure: Humiliation, anger, and moral resistance under coercion. The player wants to evade, harden, or flare into blunt protest.
pressure_points.micro_agency_without_diverting_spine: The shifts sharpen Dorothy's stance toward unjust authority, but the scene still resolves through the canonical melting reversal.

next_session_awareness.seed_for_next_session: Dorothy must leave Session 3 with the western task completed, the Golden Cap in hand, her companions recoverable, and a renewed intention to collect Oz's promised help. The key seed is expectation: after so much danger, surely Oz must now answer plainly.

branching_choices.source_moment: Event 104: the company prepares to leave Emerald City after Oz has demanded the death of the Wicked Witch of the West.
branching_choices.narrative_setup: Morning had come in the Emerald City, and the green brightness did not make the errand look any kinder. You saw your basket made ready, the Tin Woodman testing his axe, the Scarecrow settling his straw, and the Lion trying to look brave for your sake. Then the Palace gates stood behind you, and the western road waited before you like a hard promise.
branching_choices.choice_question: As you begin the western journey, what do you say this errand is to you?

options.label: A
options.text: You say this cruel task must be done in the proper way, because Oz has named the price and Kansas lies beyond it.
options.outcome: Dorothy spoke very steadily, although she was sorry in her heart that any good could be bought at so wicked a price. Yet she had come so far along the yellow brick road, and Oz had spoken plainly, and so she felt that one thing must follow another until the end was reached. The Scarecrow nodded as if a plan had been put in order. The Tin Woodman sighed, but he tightened his bright joints and said he would go where duty led. Even the Lion walked a little straighter, for there is comfort to timid people when someone else names the road. So they set their faces toward the West, and the journey became less a wish than a task.
options.downstream_effect: The company adopts a formal marching order and treats the western road as an accepted obligation.

emotional_ledger_entries: defining_moral_choices: accepted cruel bargain as duty
promises_made: agreed to go west under Oz's terms

options.world_noticed_signal: The Lion stopped glancing back at the green towers. Even the road seemed to run straighter when the matter was spoken of as duty.
options.defining_line: You put your heart behind the road because the road is there.

options.label: B
options.text: You say each friend must choose freely, and you will not ask anyone to pay too dearly for your road home.
options.outcome: Dorothy did not like to command those she loved, for she knew very well that Kansas was her own wish and not theirs. So she spoke gently, and said each one might turn back if he thought the West too dangerous. This made the matter quieter, but also more solemn; for a free promise is a heavier thing than an order. The Scarecrow answered first, saying he would not desert a friend. Then the Tin Woodman declared that kindness must not stop halfway. Last of all the Lion, who trembled a little, said he would go too, because being afraid was no reason to be faithless. Thus the company held together, not by command, but by choice.
options.downstream_effect: Each companion verbally recommits, making later loyalty beats more personal and less procedural.

npc_dispositions.shift: loyalty +1 for being asked, not driven

emotional_ledger_entries: defining_moral_choices: refused to compel companions
promises_made: companions freely chose the western journey

options.world_noticed_signal: They came after you not because the road pulled them, but because their own feet did. That made the little company seem smaller and stronger at once.
options.defining_line: You do not ask love to march unless it marches willingly.

options.label: C
options.text: You say Oz has no right to bargain for a life, but you will go west and judge the matter for yourself.
options.outcome: This speech surprised the company, for it was plain and bold, and Dorothy was only a little girl in a white frock. She said it was wicked to trade help for killing, and yet she would not sit still beneath such injustice either. So she would go West, not as Oz's servant, but to see with her own eyes what sort of Witch ruled there and what might truly be done. The Scarecrow looked thoughtful, because he liked plain reasoning. The Tin Woodman approved the goodness of the protest, though he feared the danger of it. The Lion gave a low growl of agreement, for false greatness always angered him after he had time to think. So the road began with doubt in it.
options.downstream_effect: The company advances with suspicion toward Oz's bargain, sharpening later truth-and-authority reactions.

emotional_ledger_entries: defining_moral_choices: challenged unjust authority while continuing quest
crimes_committed_or_witnessed: named Oz's bargain as wicked

options.world_noticed_signal: No one answered at once, and the morning air felt plainer after such words. The green City behind you seemed less marvelous and more far away.
options.defining_line: You take the road, but not the bargain, into your heart.

branching_choices.all_paths_arrive_at: The company leaves Emerald City and enters the rough western country, with companion tone altered by Dorothy's stated motive.

canon_boundaries: Oz's demand for the Witch's death remains the cause of the western journey.
The company must leave Emerald City and head west in this session.
Dorothy remains courteous and practical even when morally blunt.
No option allows abandoning the western campaign entirely at this point.

character_truth.authentic_reaction_to_unexpected_input: He responds with practical reasoning, and if Dorothy questions Oz he becomes thoughtful rather than offended.
He answers moral protest with sympathy, but he still fears needless violence and wants orderly action.
He is frightened by the journey, yet he is relieved when Dorothy names a clear course or honors his freedom to choose.

scene_integrity.character_knowledge_limits: Dorothy and companions know only Oz's demand and the general danger of the West; they do not yet know the Witch's methods, the Monkeys, or the captivity to come.
scene_integrity.emotional_context: Resolve mixed with dread, obligation, and companionship under strain.

fold_back_path.nearest_safe_outcome: The party still departs west together.
fold_back_path.why: The session spine requires the western journey to begin immediately, but Dorothy's reason for going changes companion tone and later moral framing.

freeform_alignment_mapping.maps_to_chaotic: Questioning Oz's right, rejecting his moral authority, or declaring you will judge the matter yourself.
freeform_alignment_mapping.maps_to_lawful: Accepting the named task as the proper road toward Kansas and urging orderly departure.
freeform_alignment_mapping.maps_to_neutral: Refusing to command others, inviting each friend to choose, and holding the company together by consent.
freeform_alignment_mapping.spirit_at_this_moment: Define whether Dorothy goes west as obedient traveler, fair-minded companion, or challenger of unjust power.

branching_choices.cross_session_payoff_reference: Branch opportunity echo of session 2 home_vs_companion_devotion; payoff session 6 farewell tone.

branching_choices.source_moment: The westward road becomes a campaign as the Witch sends wolves, crows, bees, and then the Winged Monkeys.
branching_choices.narrative_setup: The country grew rough and yellow, and the good road was left behind. First came the wolves, and then the crows, and then the bees, so that each danger seemed only to call the next after it. It was plain now that the Wicked Witch watched your coming and meant to make an end of it before you reached her door.
branching_choices.choice_question: When the western road proves truly dangerous, how do you mean to go on?

options.label: A
options.text: You say the company must watch, adapt, and take each danger as it comes instead of rushing blindly onward.
options.outcome: Dorothy had never been in a war, and she did not wish to begin one merely because another person was cruel. So she said they must keep their eyes open and meet each evil by the handiest means, one after another, as it appeared. This suited the Scarecrow very well, for he liked to think upon what was nearest. The Tin Woodman also approved, because practical care seemed kinder than desperate haste. Even the Lion was comforted; for a beast may face one enemy better than ten imagined ones. Therefore the company went on in a wary fashion, pausing to listen, looking often behind them, and treating the road not as a charge but as a hard lesson that had to be learned piece by piece.
options.downstream_effect: The party proceeds cautiously, foregrounding observation and improvised survival during the chained attacks.

emotional_ledger_entries: key_successes_failures: chose adaptive survival method on western road

options.world_noticed_signal: After that, every rustle in the grass and every black speck in the sky was counted. The road seemed less like a road and more like a thing that listened back.
options.defining_line: You do not hurry into danger when danger is already hurrying toward you.

options.label: B
options.text: You say Oz had no right to send you so, and you look for a way to break the Witch's power rather than meet it fairly.
options.outcome: Dorothy had begun to think there was little goodness in any bargain made by fright, and so she said they must stop marching as if wicked people set the rules for honest ones. If the Witch used treachery, then they must seek some crack in her power, some servant who feared her, some gate left unwatched, or some chance not named by Oz at all. The Scarecrow brightened at once, for such talk gave him many notions. The Tin Woodman was uneasy, yet he could not deny that wickedness seldom deserved fair ceremony. The Lion, though afraid, liked better to outwit a cruel enemy than to walk straight into her claws. Thus the company began to look sideways as well as forward.
options.downstream_effect: The party shifts from straightforward questing to seeking weaknesses, servants, and indirect openings in the Witch's regime.

emotional_ledger_entries: defining_moral_choices: rejected enemy's terms and sought indirect means

options.world_noticed_signal: From then on, even a broken fence, a frightened Winkie, or a shut gate looked useful. The West no longer seemed only terrible; it seemed full of seams.
options.defining_line: You stop asking wickedness to fight honestly.

options.label: C
options.text: You say the bargain must be carried through, and the company should press on at once before fear can master it.
options.outcome: Dorothy knew very well that delay feeds fear, especially in those who are already tired and troubled. So she said they must go straight on and not let the Witch frighten them out of the only hope they had. This was brave talk, and it did the Lion good to hear it, though his tail twitched uneasily. The Scarecrow agreed that a road is best traveled by walking upon it. The Tin Woodman swung his axe more firmly, as if a settled purpose oiled every joint. Therefore they hastened their pace and kept the West always before them, hoping that swift faithfulness would do what lingering doubt could never do. But wicked eyes were watching all the same, as Dorothy was soon to learn.
options.downstream_effect: The party accelerates, making the next assaults feel like direct tests of resolve rather than a creeping siege.

emotional_ledger_entries: promises_kept_or_broken: kept faith with Oz's western bargain under pressure

options.world_noticed_signal: Their feet beat faster upon the hard ground, and for a little while fear had to run behind them to keep up. Yet the sky itself seemed to be carrying word ahead.
options.defining_line: You answer fear by giving it less time to speak.

branching_choices.all_paths_arrive_at: The Witch escalates to the Winged Monkeys, and Dorothy is separated from her companions and carried into captivity.

Dorothy must be carried alive to the Witch's castle because of the protective kiss.
The companions must be broken apart by the Monkey assault.
No route here allows defeating the Witch before captivity.

character_truth.authentic_reaction_to_unexpected_input: She answers resistance with more command and cruelty, not negotiation or remorse.
He turns quickly to practical devices and likes any plan that gives him a problem to solve.
He will fight when needed, but he remains sorrowful about violence and prefers decent order.
He fears attack openly, yet follows Dorothy more bravely when she names a method.

scene_integrity.character_knowledge_limits: The company knows the Witch is attacking but does not yet know about the Golden Cap, the Monkey King's bondage, or the Witch's fear of the kiss-mark.
scene_integrity.emotional_context: Escalating peril, practical courage, and the dawning sense that the West is an organized tyranny.

fold_back_path.nearest_safe_outcome: Dorothy reaches the Witch's castle as captive after the Monkey assault.
