He welcomes firmness against sham greatness but still wants his promised courage.

scene_integrity.character_knowledge_limits: Only the party and Oz know the truth. No city servant is present to hear the exchange. Dorothy does not yet know the exact form of Oz's next plan.
scene_integrity.emotional_context: Comic embarrassment gives way to pity, betrayal, leverage, and a new uncertain hope.

fold_back_path.nearest_safe_outcome: Dorothy keeps the secret for now and allows Oz to propose a practical homeward scheme next session.
fold_back_path.why: The retention mechanism depends on Dorothy's unresolved stance toward Oz carrying directly into the balloon sequence.

freeform_alignment_mapping.maps_to_chaotic: Keeping silence only as a threat-backed bargain and pressing Oz under fear.
freeform_alignment_mapping.maps_to_lawful: Granting secrecy and trusting honest effort to repair the wrong.
freeform_alignment_mapping.maps_to_neutral: Allowing Oz to proceed only under proof and visible work.
freeform_alignment_mapping.spirit_at_this_moment: The session ends not on whether Oz is false, but on whether Dorothy will answer falseness with mercy, leverage, or guarded proof.

branching_choices.cross_session_payoff_reference: Session 5 opens with symbolic gifts and balloon plan; stance toward Oz colors cooperation, forgiveness, and later failure.

emotional_choices.source_moment: The Mouse Queen warns that the Winged Monkeys are full of mischief, and Dorothy asks if they will hurt her.
emotional_choices.narrative_lead_in: The mice are already preparing to run, and that alone is enough to make a little girl uneasy. The Golden Cap shines in your hands, but it does not look altogether friendly.
emotional_choices.choice_question: What sort of fear do you show before speaking the charm?

options.label: A
options.text: You admit you are afraid, but you mean to go on because there is no other road.
options.outcome: Dorothy said she was afraid, and there was no shame in that, for the Monkeys had been cruel before and the fields were still dangerous. But she added that she must go on all the same, because fear does not mend a lost road. This made her companions look at her with greater respect, since plain courage often begins with plain confession. So when she took up the Cap, the act seemed steadier, because it was not dressed up as boldness it did not feel.
options.tonal_effect: Narration grows sober, practical, and approving of honest fear.

npc_dispositions.shift: kinship +1; feels understood in his own fear

emotional_ledger_entries: defining_moral_choices +1: Named fear without yielding to it

options.world_noticed_signal: The Lion lifted his head a little when you spoke so. Fear sounded less lonely in the scarlet fields.
options.all_paths_arrive_at: Dorothy reads the charm inside the Golden Cap.

options.label: B
options.text: You steady your voice and trust the Queen's word that the Cap will protect you.
options.outcome: Dorothy was anxious, but the Mouse Queen had spoken plainly, and plain speech from a friend is often the best comfort. So she steadied her voice and accepted the rule as it had been given: the wearer of the Cap would be obeyed. This did not take away all fear, but it set it in order, which is nearly as useful. Her companions saw her quiet herself, and they grew quieter also. Then the queer business of the charm could begin without panic.
options.tonal_effect: Narration leans toward reassurance, order, and trust in stated rules.

emotional_ledger_entries: promises_made +1: Accepted the Queen's guidance in good faith

options.world_noticed_signal: The Queen gave one quick nod before she fled. It was the nod of someone whose warning had been properly heard.
options.all_paths_arrive_at: Dorothy reads the charm inside the Golden Cap.

options.label: C
options.text: You laugh a little at your own fright and decide the Monkeys may come if they dare.
options.outcome: Dorothy was frightened, but sometimes a little laugh is easier than a little sob. So she spoke as if she would not be daunted, and said the Monkeys might come if they dared. This was brave in sound, though her heart beat fast enough all the while. The companions brightened at the spirit of it, and the queer danger of the moment seemed lighter for a breath. Then she turned to the Cap, and there was no more time for laughing.
options.tonal_effect: Narration becomes brisker and more spirited, with a touch of comic defiance.

emotional_ledger_entries: key_successes_failures +1: Met danger with bright defiance

options.world_noticed_signal: Even Toto stopped panting to look at you when you said it. A bold little word can stir a whole tired company.
options.all_paths_arrive_at: Dorothy reads the charm inside the Golden Cap.

emotional_choices.all_paths_arrive_at: Dorothy decides whether and how to speak the Golden Cap charm.

emotional_choices.source_moment: The Monkey King asks Dorothy's command, and the party sees the green walls of Emerald City drawing near.
emotional_choices.narrative_lead_in: The Monkeys bear you through the air more gently than you had feared, and the green walls shine ahead at last. Trouble has not ended, but it has changed its shape.
emotional_choices.choice_question: How does hope come upon you when the City appears again?

options.label: A
options.text: You feel sure that a promise must surely be kept now that the task is done.
options.outcome: Dorothy looked at the shining walls and felt her heart grow lighter, for a bargain fulfilled ought to bring its reward. She had gone west, endured slavery and danger, and now she was returning as she had said she would. This made the City seem not only beautiful but proper, as if all the green gates and ordered streets might now open into justice. The thought comforted her greatly, though it proved later that splendor and fairness do not always live in the same house.
options.tonal_effect: Narration grows ceremonious and orderly, heightening faith in promises and return.

emotional_ledger_entries: promises_kept_or_broken +1: Renewed expectation that Oz must pay his debt

options.world_noticed_signal: The green walls seemed to stand straighter in your eyes, like a promise written large against the sky.
options.all_paths_arrive_at: The Monkeys set the travelers down before Emerald City's gate.

options.label: B
options.text: You are simply glad to be out of trouble for the moment and let tomorrow wait.
options.outcome: Dorothy did not trouble herself just then with what Oz would do or fail to do. It was enough that the scarlet fields were behind them, the wind was cool upon her face, and the Monkeys were carrying them safely toward walls instead of danger. Such relief is a small, good thing, and she took it gladly. So for a little while the journey was only a fine ride and not a burden of bargains.
options.tonal_effect: Narration becomes lighter and more immediate, savoring rescue over future reckoning.

emotional_ledger_entries: key_successes_failures +1: Took joy in immediate deliverance

options.world_noticed_signal: For a little while the City was only green and near, and not yet full of waiting or words.
options.all_paths_arrive_at: The Monkeys set the travelers down before Emerald City's gate.

options.label: C
options.text: You hope for home, but you keep your heart from running too far ahead.
options.outcome: Dorothy was glad to see the City, for it meant progress and perhaps the end of many troubles; but she had traveled too far in Oz to trust hope without a little caution. So she let herself be comforted, though not carried away. Kansas rose before her mind, and Aunt Em with it, yet she kept the picture quiet and small, as one might carry a lamp through the wind. This gave her hope a steadier look, though not a merrier one.
options.tonal_effect: Narration turns wistful but measured, balancing longing with restraint.

emotional_ledger_entries: sacrifices_made +1: Held back full hope to avoid fresh hurt

options.world_noticed_signal: The green walls shone bright enough, yet behind them you could almost see a little gray house standing very far away.
options.all_paths_arrive_at: The Monkeys set the travelers down before Emerald City's gate.

emotional_choices.all_paths_arrive_at: The travelers return to Emerald City in triumph.

emotional_choices.source_moment: While flying over gardens and woods, Dorothy asks the Monkey King why the Winged Monkeys must obey the Golden Cap.
emotional_choices.narrative_lead_in: Below you lie pretty gardens and woods, and above you the wings beat steadily as if the sky itself were carrying you onward. It is a fine time for asking questions, because the road cannot be lost in the air.
emotional_choices.choice_question: What kind of question do you put to the Monkey King?

options.label: A
options.text: You ask how the enchantment works and who first laid that command upon them.
options.outcome: Dorothy's curiosity was of the useful kind, for she wished to know not only that a thing happened, but why. So she asked who had first laid the command upon the Monkeys and how the Golden Cap came to rule them. This pleased the King, who had a story ready and liked an attentive listener. Therefore he began at the beginning, with Gayelette and Quelala and the wedding mischief that brought a free people into bondage. The tale made the bright ride graver, but it also made the world clearer.
options.tonal_effect: Narration becomes explanatory and world-building, with clear cause and consequence.

npc_dispositions.shift: respect +1; feels seen as more than a servant

emotional_ledger_entries: secrets_discovered +1: Learned the origin of the Golden Cap's bondage

options.world_noticed_signal: The Monkey King turned his head a little as he flew, as if he had found in you not merely a mistress but a hearer.
options.all_paths_arrive_at: The Monkey King tells the history of Gayelette, Quelala, and the Golden Cap.

options.label: B
options.text: You ask whether they were ever happy before they became servants.
options.outcome: Dorothy did not begin with the spell itself, but with the creatures beneath it. She asked whether the Winged Monkeys had once been happy before they were made to obey. This was a kind question, and it drew from the King not only facts but feeling. So he spoke first of freedom in the great forest, of nuts and fruit and mischief and careless delight, before he came to the wedding prank and the sentence laid upon his people. The sky remained bright, but the Cap grew heavier in Dorothy's thoughts.
options.tonal_effect: Narration softens and lingers on lost freedom and creaturely happiness.

emotional_ledger_entries: npcs_helped_or_harmed +1: Showed concern for bound creatures

options.world_noticed_signal: The King's winged laugh was less merry after that, but more friendly. Even the air seemed gentler when he spoke of the old forest.
options.all_paths_arrive_at: The Monkey King tells the history of Gayelette, Quelala, and the Golden Cap.

options.label: C
options.text: You ask whether they ever tried to break the charm and refuse a master.
options.outcome: Dorothy looked at the strong brown arms and the great wings, and she wondered how such creatures had borne obedience so long. So she asked whether they had ever tried to break the charm and refuse a master. The question was bold, and it made the Monkey King laugh his winged laugh before he answered. Then he told her the story in full, and by the end of it the joke no longer seemed so light, for enchantments can bind the merry as tightly as the meek.
options.tonal_effect: Narration sharpens toward defiance, testing the edges of magical authority.

npc_dispositions.shift: curiosity +1; marks Dorothy as one who questions masters

emotional_ledger_entries: defining_moral_choices +1: Questioned the justice of magical bondage

options.world_noticed_signal: The King's laugh rang wide in the air, but there was an old sadness under it when he began his answer.
options.all_paths_arrive_at: The Monkey King tells the history of Gayelette, Quelala, and the Golden Cap.

emotional_choices.all_paths_arrive_at: The Monkey King recounts the Monkeys' bondage while the party flies toward Emerald City.

emotional_choices.source_moment: The Monkey King explains that Quelala and Gayelette spared the Monkeys but bound them to three commands under the Golden Cap.
emotional_choices.narrative_lead_in: The story is a queer one, for it begins in mischief and ends in slavery. By the time the King has finished, the bright Cap upon your head no longer seems only lucky.
emotional_choices.choice_question: How do you feel toward the Winged Monkeys after hearing their story?

options.label: A
options.text: You feel sorry for them and resolve to use the Cap as gently as you can.
options.outcome: Dorothy was very sorry for the Winged Monkeys, because they had once been free and merry, and now even their obedience had an old sorrow in it. So she resolved in her little heart that while the Golden Cap remained hers she would use it as gently as she could. This did not free them, to be sure, but it made a difference in the spirit of the command, and such differences are not nothing. The King seemed to notice it, though he said no more just then.
options.tonal_effect: Narration becomes gently approving and morally clear about pity and restraint.

emotional_ledger_entries: acts_of_mercy +1
defining_moral_choices +1: Resolved to wield power gently

options.world_noticed_signal: The Monkey King's hands held you as carefully as before, but now their care seemed answered.
options.all_paths_arrive_at: The Monkeys set the travelers down before Emerald City's gate.

options.label: B
options.text: You feel the Cap grow heavier because useful magic can still be a cruel thing.
options.outcome: Dorothy did not know yet what ought to be done with the Golden Cap in the end, but she knew now that it was not a merry treasure. It was useful, and that was plain enough; yet usefulness does not make a thing good. So the gold seemed heavier upon her head, though it had not gained an ounce. This thought stayed with her, and made the journey's quick success feel mixed with something graver beneath it.
options.tonal_effect: Narration turns sober and reflective without becoming inwardly dense.

emotional_ledger_entries: crimes_committed_or_witnessed +1: Recognized coercive magic as troubling

options.world_noticed_signal: The bright gold did not shine less, but it pleased you less. Some pretty things are not light to carry.
options.all_paths_arrive_at: The Monkeys set the travelers down before Emerald City's gate.

options.label: C
options.text: You think they were wild enough to earn punishment, though not endless bondage.
options.outcome: Dorothy could not help thinking the Monkeys had behaved very badly in the old days, for dropping a bridegroom into a river on his wedding eve was no kind joke. So she thought some punishment had been deserved, though not such a long and binding one as this. It was a child's plain judgment, and not an unkind one. The story therefore seemed to her less simple than before, with mischief on one side and enchantment on the other. Such thoughts did not spoil the ride, but they made it less easy.
options.tonal_effect: Narration becomes more briskly judgmental, naming wrongdoing while resisting excess.

emotional_ledger_entries: defining_moral_choices +1: Balanced blame with resistance to cruelty

options.world_noticed_signal: The King's wings beat on as steadily as ever, but he did not turn his head toward you again for a little while.
options.all_paths_arrive_at: The Monkeys set the travelers down before Emerald City's gate.

emotional_choices.all_paths_arrive_at: The flight ends at Emerald City's gate.

emotional_choices.source_moment: Days pass in the palace after Dorothy returns victorious, and Oz still does not send for them.
emotional_choices.narrative_lead_in: The green girl shows you to your old rooms, and at first rest is pleasant enough. But one day passes, and then another, and then another, and still Oz sends no word.
emotional_choices.choice_question: What feeling settles upon you during the waiting?

options.label: A
options.text: You grow vexed and feel that politeness has already been stretched too far.
options.outcome: Dorothy had obeyed Oz's cruel bargain and come back from the West alive, and so his silence began to seem not grand but shabby. She grew vexed, and there was justice in it. The green rooms no longer felt restful; they felt like a soft sort of insult. This temper did not make her wicked, but it sharpened her patience to a point, and when the time came to send a stronger message she was ready for it.
options.tonal_effect: Narration sharpens toward hurt indignation and impatience with ceremony.

emotional_ledger_entries: promises_kept_or_broken +1: Registered Oz's delay as a fresh wrong

options.world_noticed_signal: The green curtains and green cushions looked less cheerful each morning. Waiting can wear the color off a room without changing it at all.
options.all_paths_arrive_at: The party decides to send a sharper message to Oz.

options.label: B
options.text: You keep your hurt to yourself and begin to wonder what sort of fear keeps Oz away.
options.outcome: Dorothy was disappointed, but disappointment may look two ways. It may look toward one's own hurt, and it may look toward the cause of it. So while she felt the slight keenly enough, she also began to wonder why Oz delayed them after all they had done. This did not excuse him, but it made the waiting curious as well as painful. By the time the summons finally came, she was prepared to look more closely at the man behind the throne.
options.tonal_effect: Narration becomes quieter, more observant, and more suspicious than openly angry.

emotional_ledger_entries: secrets_discovered +1: Began to suspect fear behind authority

options.world_noticed_signal: The palace remained quiet, but now the quiet seemed to hide something instead of merely delaying it.
options.all_paths_arrive_at: The party decides to send a sharper message to Oz.

options.label: C
options.text: You hold to patience because a ruler may have reasons that subjects do not yet know.
options.outcome: Dorothy was hurt by the delay, yet she tried to be patient, for rulers often keep strange hours and make stranger arrangements. She told herself there might be reasons not yet known to them, and so she bore the waiting as well as she could. This did not make the days shorter, but it kept bitterness from mastering her. Therefore, when the sharper message was at last sent, it came as a last necessity and not a first impulse.
options.tonal_effect: Narration remains composed and fair-minded, preserving respect until evidence breaks it.

npc_dispositions.shift: future trust +1 if he answers honestly

emotional_ledger_entries: sacrifices_made +1: Endured waiting without open complaint

options.world_noticed_signal: The days were still long, but they did not grow sour quite so quickly under your patience.
options.all_paths_arrive_at: The party decides to send a sharper message to Oz.

emotional_choices.all_paths_arrive_at: The Scarecrow sends word that they will call the Winged Monkeys if Oz does not receive them.

posture_shifts.placement: Cold open in the scarlet fields before Dorothy blows the whistle; exhaustion and resourcefulness press together.
posture_shifts.narrator_line: Toto looked up at you with his little red tongue out, and for a moment the whole weary company seemed to wait upon what your face would do next.

options.label: A
options.text: You kneel first and stroke Toto before speaking.
options.narration_adjustment: Dorothy laid her hand upon Toto and comforted him as if he were a child, which indeed he nearly was to her. This little kindness steadied her more than if she had hurried at once.

options.label: B
options.text: You rise at once and look for the next help.
options.narration_adjustment: Dorothy did not sit long with sorrow, for sorrow has poor eyes in a strange country. She looked about at once for what might yet be done.

options.label: C
options.text: You draw one breath and make yourself calm.
options.narration_adjustment: Dorothy was near losing heart, but she checked herself in time. A quiet mind was the first thing needed, and so she tried to make one.

posture_shifts.placement: Just after the whistle is remembered; urgency sharpens because the party may fail if they delay.
posture_shifts.narrator_line: The little whistle lay ready in your fingers, and the scarlet fields seemed larger because they had no road in them.

options.label: A
options.text: You blow it sharply, with no time to spare.
options.narration_adjustment: Dorothy wasted no breath on doubting. The whistle gave a quick, thin cry, and help was summoned as briskly as need required.

options.label: B
options.text: You hold it a second, hoping for another thought.
options.narration_adjustment: For just a moment Dorothy wished some other answer might appear. But none did, and so she turned at last to the friends she had already made.

posture_shifts.placement: Dorothy inspects the writing inside the Golden Cap; absurd magic meets practical caution.
posture_shifts.narrator_line: The queer words inside the Cap were plain enough to read, though not plain enough to like.

options.label: A
options.text: You bend close and study every word.
options.narration_adjustment: Dorothy examined the lining as if good sense might be stitched into it beside the charm. She liked a rule better when she had looked at it well.

options.label: B
options.text: You glance once and trust the directions.
options.narration_adjustment: Dorothy saw enough to know there was a rule to follow, and that was enough for the moment. In Oz, one cannot always wait to understand a marvel completely before using it.

options.label: C
options.text: You make a face at the foolish-looking charm.
options.narration_adjustment: The words looked so odd that Dorothy could not help thinking them a little foolish. Yet foolish things in Oz had a way of being very real indeed.

posture_shifts.placement: During the charm performance; humor and unease mingle.
posture_shifts.narrator_line: Standing first on one foot and then upon the other, you must have looked very queer, though no one there thought it wise to say so.

options.label: A
options.text: You blush, but go on with it.
options.narration_adjustment: Dorothy knew the business was ridiculous, and she colored a little. But she did not stop, for there are times when even a foolish posture must be seen through bravely.

options.label: B
options.text: You say the words as solemnly as a prayer.
options.narration_adjustment: Dorothy gave the charm all the dignity she could, though it did not greatly deserve it. This made the absurdity of it seem gentler and the danger of it more manageable.

options.label: C
options.text: You let yourself smile at the nonsense of it.
options.narration_adjustment: Dorothy could not help smiling a little at such queer syllables and such queer stepping. A small smile often keeps fear from growing too large.

posture_shifts.placement: Late in the monkey flight over gardens and woods; wonder competes with homesickness.
posture_shifts.narrator_line: Far below, the gardens, brooks, woods, and little fields lay spread out as neatly as if someone had arranged them in a green box.

options.label: A
options.text: You lean to see every pretty thing below.
options.narration_adjustment: Dorothy looked down with wide eyes and forgot trouble for a little while. Oz, for all its dangers, could still show marvelous beauty to a tired traveler.

options.label: B
options.text: You think only how much sooner Kansas may come.
options.narration_adjustment: Even in the air Dorothy's thoughts turned toward Kansas. Pretty woods were well enough, but they were not the great gray prairie nor Aunt Em's kitchen.

options.label: C
options.text: You watch the Monkeys more than the land.
options.narration_adjustment: Dorothy did not give all her attention to the view. She kept some of it upon the creatures who carried her, for gratitude and caution may travel together.

posture_shifts.placement: Oz's long delay after the triumphant return; hurt and distrust begin to gather.
posture_shifts.narrator_line: Each morning in the green rooms began with hope and ended with no message, which is a tiresome way for days to behave.

options.label: A
options.text: You keep still, but your hurt grows.
options.narration_adjustment: Dorothy did not complain much, yet the slight touched her deeply. Silence can carry hurt as plainly as speech, if one knows how to look at it.

options.label: B
options.text: You begin to doubt Oz in earnest.
options.narration_adjustment: The delay no longer looked majestic to Dorothy. It began to look like fear, and that thought changed the color of the waiting.

options.label: C
options.text: You tell yourself there must be some reason.
options.narration_adjustment: Dorothy tried to be fair, even when fairness was not easy. She hoped there might still be some proper cause for so much delay.

posture_shifts.placement: The party sends a sharper message threatening to call the Winged Monkeys.
posture_shifts.narrator_line: The threat of the Monkeys hung in the air like a storm-bell, and it mattered whether you meant to ring it loudly or let others do the ringing.

options.label: A
options.text: You speak up and mean the threat.
options.narration_adjustment: Dorothy had been patient long enough, and now she let Oz hear it plainly. A promise delayed after such suffering looked too much like a promise denied.

options.label: B
options.text: You let the Scarecrow press the matter first.
options.narration_adjustment: Dorothy did not hide, but she let another voice carry the sharper edge. Sometimes leadership means allowing a friend to speak the thing one also feels.

posture_shifts.placement: The screen has fallen and the little old man stands revealed.
posture_shifts.narrator_line: Where a great terror should have been there stood only a little old man, bald and wrinkled and frightened out of all his grandeur.

options.label: A
options.text: You stare in pure astonishment.
options.narration_adjustment: Dorothy could only stare for a moment, because the truth was smaller and stranger than any lie she had expected. It is not every day that a Ball of Fire turns into a little old man.

options.label: B
options.text: You feel angry before anything else.
options.narration_adjustment: Dorothy's surprise quickly gave way to anger, for this meek figure had sent them into great danger all the same. A humbug may be small and still do large harm.

options.label: C
options.text: You pity him at once for being so afraid.
options.narration_adjustment: Even in the shock of it Dorothy saw how frightened the little man was. Pity came before judgment, or at least beside it.

posture_shifts.placement: Oz begs them not to speak loudly because he will be ruined if overheard.
posture_shifts.narrator_line: His voice had lost all thunder now, and what remained in it was only the trembling of a man who feared to be found exactly as he was.

options.label: A
options.text: You soften a little, though you do not forget.
options.narration_adjustment: Dorothy's heart was touched, though not won over. One may pity a humbug and still remember the trouble he has caused.

options.label: B
options.text: You stand firm and wait for something useful.
options.narration_adjustment: Dorothy did not waste many words upon pity just then. She had come for help, and help must now be more than a sound in a dome.

options.label: C
options.text: You look to your friends before answering him.
options.narration_adjustment: Dorothy glanced at the others, for the fraud had touched them all. What should be done with Oz was not only her burden now.

present_npcs.current_disposition: tired, courteous, and anxious for Oz's promise

scene_rules_layer_4.character_knowledge_limits: Dorothy knows only that the whistle summons the mice. The Queen knows the Emerald City is behind them and that the Golden Cap can command the Winged Monkeys.
scene_rules_layer_4.emotional_context: Exhaustion, stranded urgency, and resourcefulness under pressure.
canon_boundaries_active: The scarlet poppies remain dangerous to flesh creatures.
The mice can advise but not solve the journey except by pointing to the Golden Cap.
Dorothy already possesses the Mouse Queen's whistle and the Golden Cap.
freeform_risk_areas: Trying to have Toto chase or attack the mice
Attempting to walk deeper into the poppies without consequence
Inventing alternate transport that bypasses the Golden Cap

scene_rules_layer_4.character_knowledge_limits: Dorothy learns the charm but not yet the full monkey history until she asks. The companions remember prior monkey violence but do not know the Monkeys' bondage tale.
scene_rules_layer_4.emotional_context: Comic absurdity, magical danger, and the first lift of hope.
canon_boundaries_active: The Golden Cap summons the Winged Monkeys lawfully and consumes a use.
The Monkeys must obey the lawful owner.
The Monkeys cannot harm Dorothy under the active protections and command structure.
freeform_risk_areas: Trying to command the Monkeys to take Dorothy to Kansas
Using the Cap without speaking the charm
Having the Monkeys refuse a valid order

available_objects: view of gardens, woods, brooks, and fields below

present_npcs.current_disposition: obedient, talkative, and increasingly responsive to Dorothy's tone
cheerful once no harm is intended
relieved and enjoying the quick passage

scene_rules_layer_4.character_knowledge_limits: Only the Monkey King can tell the full history of Gayelette, Quelala, and the Cap. Dorothy does not yet know how morally burdened the artifact truly is until he explains it.
scene_rules_layer_4.emotional_context: Wonder, humor, and moral softening in the middle of danger.
canon_boundaries_active: The Monkey King's backstory must preserve Gayelette, Quelala, the prank, and the threefold bondage.
The Monkeys remain servants under the Cap and cannot free themselves here.
freeform_risk_areas: Overwriting the Monkey King with modern irony
Changing the origin of the Golden Cap
Turning the flight into peril instead of reward

available_objects: message channels through the green girl and soldier

present_npcs.current_disposition: hurt by delay and growing angry

scene_rules_layer_4.character_knowledge_limits: The city believes Dorothy has melted the Witch and expects Oz's greatness to continue. No one outside the party knows Oz is afraid or false.
scene_rules_layer_4.emotional_context: Triumph curdling into delay, slight, and suspicion.
canon_boundaries_active: Emerald City still appears green through the spectacles system until the illusion is reframed.
Oz delays rather than receiving them at once.
The threat of the Winged Monkeys frightens Oz into granting audience.
freeform_risk_areas: Publicly exposing Oz before the throne room reveal
Skipping the waiting period entirely
Having city officials already know Oz is a fraud

present_npcs.current_disposition: angry at delay and broken promise
ready to frighten Oz if needed

scene_rules_layer_4.character_knowledge_limits: Only Oz knows the mechanism behind the Voice before the screen falls. The party suspects evasion but not the exact truth.
scene_rules_layer_4.emotional_context: Dread, challenge, comic collapse, and moral shock.
canon_boundaries_active: Oz is invisible only by trick, not true magic.
The Lion's roar and Toto's movement must precipitate the screen's fall.
Oz is revealed as a little old man and a humbug.
freeform_risk_areas: Allowing Oz to perform genuine magic
Preventing the reveal
Turning the reveal into public exposure before the next session

scene_rules_layer_4.character_knowledge_limits: The truth remains confined to the party and Oz. Dorothy does not yet know the full details of the symbolic gifts or balloon plan, only that Oz still claims he can help somehow.
scene_rules_layer_4.emotional_context: Pity, betrayal, leverage, and unresolved hope.
canon_boundaries_active: Oz begs for secrecy and remains privately exposed only.
The session cannot resolve Dorothy's return home.
The next movement must seed symbolic help and the balloon plan.
freeform_risk_areas: Having Dorothy denounce Oz to the whole city now
Resolving the homeward journey in this scene
Making Oz suddenly noble through actual magic

paths.label: A
paths.immediate_effect: In the Poppy Edge Summons scene, the Queen of the Field Mice answers at once, steps to the edge of Dorothy's silver shoe, and says, "The Emerald City is behind you," before adding in a brisk, useful tone that the Golden Cap may command the Winged Monkeys.
paths.current_session_echo: In the Monkey Summons scene, the Scarecrow says, "That was sensibly managed," and the Queen departs without further ceremony, leaving help given but no lingering affection.
paths.next_session_payoff: In Session 5, when Oz spreads out the green silk for the balloon, he begins, "Now, my dear, let us see what can truly be done," and the Scarecrow backs Dorothy's demand for proof without needing to soothe any wounded feelings in the room.
paths.later_session_legacy: In Session 6, when Glinda asks for the Golden Cap, the Monkey King bows correctly and obeys cleanly, but he offers information without the extra note of personal gratitude reserved for warmer treatment.
paths.defining_line_captured: You ask for help as if need were no shame at all.

consequence_visibility.what_changes: Queen of the Field Mice disposition shifts to practical trust rather than ceremony or chill; Scarecrow reads Dorothy as steady.
consequence_visibility.how_player_sees_it: The Queen comes near but not nearest, answers promptly, and leaves after giving directions; the Scarecrow names Dorothy's steadiness in the very next exchange.

world_state_delta.emotional_ledger_entry: promises_made +1: Promised thanks for needed help

resets: Poppy-field proximity urgency clears once monkey transport begins.
persists: Queen of the Field Mice remembers Dorothy as plain and civil under strain.
Scarecrow stores this as evidence that Dorothy is steady in trouble.
escalates: Repeated plain, fair treatment of small allies strengthens later ally responsiveness.
Repeated practical leadership makes Scarecrow volunteer plans more readily.

paths.label: B
paths.immediate_effect: In the Poppy Edge Summons scene, several little mice slip behind the Queen's tail before she answers, and she says, "We are your friends, but we are not your servants," before giving the same direction and pointing out the Golden Cap.
paths.current_session_echo: In the Monkey Summons scene, the Tin Woodman turns his bright head and says, "Truly, the little creatures were kind to help us so quickly," which lands as a gentle rebuke before the Monkeys arrive.
paths.next_session_payoff: In Session 5, when Dorothy asks Oz for visible work, the Tin Woodman watches her more sorrowfully and says, "I pray this may be done kindly," if she presses too sharply again.
paths.later_session_legacy: In Session 6, if Dorothy still holds the Golden Cap, Glinda becomes firmer in explaining that command over bound creatures is a grave thing and must not be worn lightly.
paths.defining_line_captured: Need makes you bold enough to spend even friendship quickly.

consequence_visibility.what_changes: Mouse alliance tone becomes formal and wary; Tin Woodman registers moral unease at commanding the weak.
consequence_visibility.how_player_sees_it: Small mice physically retreat, the Queen corrects Dorothy in plain words, and the Tin Woodman later answers with a sorrowful courtesy line.

world_state_delta.emotional_ledger_entry: crimes_committed_or_witnessed +1: Minor coercive tone toward small allies

resets: The physical clustering of frightened mice ends when they scatter from the Monkeys.
persists: Queen of the Field Mice remembers Dorothy as urgent enough to press friendship like a claim.
Tin Woodman carries a small sorrow about sharpness toward weak creatures.
escalates: Repeated coercive treatment of weaker allies produces colder help from small or gentle beings.
Repeated hard use of necessity increases Tin Woodman's moral unease in later bargains.

paths.label: C
paths.immediate_effect: In the Poppy Edge Summons scene, the Queen runs forward until her paws nearly touch Dorothy's dress and says, "For such a courteous little girl we will do all we can," before eagerly explaining both the backward road and the Golden Cap's charm.
paths.current_session_echo: In the Monkey Summons scene, the Cowardly Lion lifts his head from the sweet grass and says, "I felt safer when the little Queen spoke so kindly to you," just before the queer stepping begins.
paths.next_session_payoff: In Session 5, Oz opens more softly after the session break because the room has already been set by Dorothy's graciousness; the Tin Woodman is the first to assist with the green silk, and he says, "Kindness has mended stranger matters than this."
paths.later_session_legacy: In Session 6, when Glinda takes the Golden Cap, the Monkey King says, "The little girl used command with a gentle hand," which slightly warms Glinda's response and smooths the transfer.
paths.defining_line_captured: You make room for courtesy even when the world presses hard.

consequence_visibility.what_changes: Queen of the Field Mice becomes openly warm and forthcoming; Lion and Tin Woodman feel comforted by Dorothy's graciousness.
consequence_visibility.how_player_sees_it: The Queen closes distance, praises Dorothy aloud, gives information eagerly rather than merely dutifully, and the Lion voices immediate comfort.

world_state_delta.emotional_ledger_entry: defining_moral_choices +1: Chose gratitude under pressure

resets: The ceremonial posture of the gathered mice ends once the Monkeys are summoned.
persists: Queen of the Field Mice remembers Dorothy as one who honored small allies in distress.
Lion stores this as proof that Dorothy remains gracious when afraid.
escalates: Repeated gratitude toward helpers unlocks warmer ally testimony in final authority scenes.
Repeated gracious steadiness makes the Lion more declarative and trusting in later danger.

paths.label: A
paths.immediate_effect: In the Golden Cap Reading scene, Dorothy follows the lining exactly, and when the Monkey King appears he bows until his wing-tips brush the grass and says, "I am the Slave of the Golden Cap," in a grave, obedient manner.
paths.current_session_echo: During the Monkey Flight to Emerald City, the Monkey King gives his carrying orders neatly and without complaint, placing Dorothy first and the Tin Woodman last, as if the whole business had been properly entered in an old account-book.
paths.next_session_payoff: In Session 5, when Oz proposes the balloon work, the Scarecrow says, "Why not hear the whole plan first? She has done every queer thing in good order and come safe so far," which steadies the room.
paths.later_session_legacy: In Session 6, Glinda responds to Dorothy's account of the Cap with easier confidence and says, "You have not trifled with power," before discussing its final use.
paths.defining_line_captured: You trust the written rule when the road itself has failed you.

consequence_visibility.what_changes: Monkey King reads Dorothy as a proper lawful holder; the Monkeys' service is ceremonious and orderly.
consequence_visibility.how_player_sees_it: The Monkey King bows deeply, uses formal obedience language, and organizes the carrying with crisp, respectful command.

world_state_delta.emotional_ledger_entry: secrets_discovered +1: Learned the Golden Cap charm

resets: The immediate monkey-flight formation ends on arrival at Emerald City.
persists: Monkey King records Dorothy as careful with lawful power.
Golden Cap remaining command state persists.
escalates: Repeated careful use of magical rules increases respect from bound magical agents.
Repeated orderly handling of strange tools increases companion trust in Dorothy's judgment.

paths.label: B
paths.immediate_effect: In the Golden Cap Questioning scene, the Queen answers Dorothy's questions in quick little lessons, and when the Monkey King appears he looks at Dorothy twice before bowing, as if he had already guessed she was the sort to ask what a command cost.
paths.current_session_echo: During the Monkey Flight to Emerald City, Dorothy asks, "Will you set us down safely?" and the Monkey King answers plainly, "We must obey; and we do not drop what we are ordered to carry," making the ride feel informed rather than blind.
paths.next_session_payoff: In Session 5, when Oz begins to explain the balloon, Dorothy receives an extra explanatory beat about glue, silk, and hot air before any labor starts, because this path has trained the scene toward question-and-answer practicality.
paths.later_session_legacy: In Session 6, Glinda answers Dorothy's questions about the Silver Shoes more fully and with less maternal simplification, because Dorothy has shown herself to be a child who asks plain things plainly.
paths.defining_line_captured: You would rather know the price of help before you spend it.

consequence_visibility.what_changes: The scene gains explicit exposition about the Monkeys and the burden of command; Monkey King becomes curious rather than merely obedient.
consequence_visibility.how_player_sees_it: Dorothy gets extra answer beats before the summon, and the Monkey King studies her before speaking, then answers her safety question directly in flight.

world_state_delta.inventory: Golden Cap knowledge_of_charm = learned; symbolic_authority_over_monkeys = consciously burdened
world_state_delta.emotional_ledger_entry: defining_moral_choices +1: Sought understanding before command

resets: The Mouse Queen's explanatory presence ends once she flees the coming Monkeys.
persists: Dorothy's relation to the Golden Cap is marked as conscious burden, not mere possession.
Monkey King remembers that Dorothy asked before she ordered.
escalates: Repeated question-first behavior unlocks fuller explanations from truthful authorities.
Repeated reflective handling of power deepens later thematic emphasis on cost and release.

paths.label: C
paths.immediate_effect: In the Hasty Summons scene, the words are scarcely spoken before the sky beats dark with wings, and the Monkey King lands without the deeper courtesy bow, saying only, "What is your command?"
paths.current_session_echo: During the Monkey Flight to Emerald City, the Cowardly Lion gives a great sigh and says, "At any rate, we are out of the flowers," while Dorothy has no answer yet to what sort of creatures bear them through the air.
paths.next_session_payoff: In Session 5, Oz's first practical explanation is met by a tenser room, and if Dorothy presses him quickly he hurries his balloon talk, skipping comfort and moving straight to ropes, silk, and labor.
paths.later_session_legacy: In Session 6, if Dorothy hesitates over surrendering the Cap, Glinda answers more firmly because Dorothy's earlier use of command has already shown a taste for speed over understanding.
paths.defining_line_captured: You use power first and sort out its meaning afterward.

consequence_visibility.what_changes: Summon tone becomes hasty; Monkey King withholds warmth until Dorothy's treatment becomes clearer; Lion feels immediate relief at speed.
consequence_visibility.how_player_sees_it: The Monkeys arrive almost on top of the spoken words, the Monkey King uses clipped service language, and the Lion voices relief before anyone can explain the matter.

world_state_delta.emotional_ledger_entry: sacrifices_made +1: Accepted unknown magical risk for speed

resets: The immediate rush of wings and panic clears once the party is set down in the city.
persists: Monkey King remembers that Dorothy spent a command swiftly, before asking much.
Golden Cap remaining command state persists.
escalates: Repeated speed-first use of magical power lowers warmth from compelled helpers.
Repeated urgent pragmatism shifts later explanatory scenes toward haste and thinner trust.

paths.label: A
paths.immediate_effect: In the Empty Throne Room Confrontation, Dorothy demands plain dealing, the Lion roars sooner, and when Toto topples the screen Oz blurts, "Do not strike me!" before he says anything else, showing fear before excuse.
paths.current_session_echo: In the Humbug Revealed scene, the Scarecrow is first to say, "You are a humbug," while the green room itself is narrated more bare and foolish, with the apparatus showing where grandeur had stood.
paths.next_session_payoff: In Session 5, Oz begins defensively among the green silk and says, "My dear, I am not so bad as all that," before Dorothy can ask a question, and every practical proposal carries the air of a man trying not to be found out twice.
paths.later_session_legacy: In Session 6, Glinda's plain counsel about the Silver Shoes lands with special force; the narrator frames it as the last and best answer after painted greatness has already been broken once.
paths.defining_line_captured: You would rather break the spell than live kindly under it.

consequence_visibility.what_changes: Oz shifts immediately into fear and defensiveness; the room's splendor is stripped faster and more harshly; Scarecrow speaks candidly sooner.
consequence_visibility.how_player_sees_it: Oz's first revealed line is a frightened plea, the Scarecrow openly names humbug, and the narration notices boards, tricks, and emptiness instead of majesty.

npc_shifts.direction: fear up; defensiveness up; trust down

world_state_delta.emotional_ledger_entry: secrets_discovered +1: Learned Oz is a humbug

resets: The immediate acoustic shock of the Lion's early roar ends when the room falls quiet.
persists: Oz remembers Dorothy as the one who broke his performance open.
emerald_city_illusion_state remains KNOWN_AS_SPECTACLE_ILLUSION.
oz_public_authority_state remains SECRETLY_EXPOSED_TO_PARTY_ONLY until balloon departure.
escalates: Repeated truth-first choices make later authorities drop pageantry sooner.
Repeated humbug-piercing choices strengthen Scarecrow's candid speech in later counsel scenes.

paths.label: B
paths.immediate_effect: In the Empty Throne Room Confrontation, Dorothy presses Oz through his promise, and after the screen falls the little old man says, "I meant to keep my word, indeed I did," before begging them not to speak loudly.
paths.current_session_echo: In the Humbug Revealed scene, the throne remains visually present in the prose even after it is emptied of greatness, and the companions' anger is shaped more by broken bargain than by mockery.
paths.next_session_payoff: In Session 5, Oz opens with a more orderly explanation: "My dear, since you have dealt fairly with me, I will tell you exactly what I can attempt," and he moves into the balloon plan as a contract to be honored.
paths.later_session_legacy: In Session 6, Glinda's instructions feel like the first truly trustworthy authority Dorothy has met, and the contrast with Oz's failed promise is spoken more plainly in narration.
paths.defining_line_captured: You let the promise stand until the promiser cannot stand inside it.

consequence_visibility.what_changes: Oz frames himself first as a failed promiser rather than a cornered fraud; the room keeps more ceremonial residue; companions speak in the language of obligation.
consequence_visibility.how_player_sees_it: Oz's first revealed defense mentions his word, the throne is still noticed after the reveal, and the scene's language turns on promise and bargain.

world_state_delta.emotional_ledger_entry: promises_kept_or_broken +1: Pressed Oz to honor his promise

resets: The formal posture of addressing the throne ends once Oz steps down into private speech.
persists: Oz remembers Dorothy as demanding but orderly.
The broken-promise frame persists into later dealings with Oz and other rulers.
escalates: Repeated lawful pressure on authority increases orderly confession rather than open panic.
Repeated promise-focused choices sharpen later narrator framing around kept and broken obligations.

paths.label: C
paths.immediate_effect: In the Empty Throne Room Questioning, the invisible Voice is made to answer more than it wishes, and after the screen falls Oz says, "I am a very good man, but I am a very bad Wizard," almost at once, because Dorothy's questions have already brought him halfway down.
paths.current_session_echo: In the Humbug Revealed scene, the Scarecrow listens with bright attention instead of only indignation, and the room's majesty seems to leak away bit by bit as Oz explains his apparatus.
paths.next_session_payoff: In Session 5, Oz opens in a smaller, chattier voice and volunteers practical details sooner; Dorothy receives more direct answers about the balloon's making because inquiry, not accusation, set the terms of exposure.
paths.later_session_legacy: In Session 6, Glinda's explanation of the Shoes is delivered in the clearest question-and-answer shape, and Dorothy is framed as one who reaches truth by asking plain things.
paths.defining_line_captured: You do not smash the mask first; you make it answer.

consequence_visibility.what_changes: Oz collapses into explanatory candor faster; Scarecrow's reaction becomes admiring inquiry; the room's illusion fades by degrees rather than at one crash alone.
consequence_visibility.how_player_sees_it: The Voice answers too much before the reveal, Oz's first private line is self-explanation, and the prose notices majesty thinning away instead of only breaking.

world_state_delta.emotional_ledger_entry: key_successes_failures +1: Forced hidden truth into the open

resets: The echoing uncertainty of the invisible Voice ends once the screen has fallen.
persists: Oz remembers Dorothy as one who asked him down from spectacle.
The inquiry-first pattern persists into later truth scenes.
escalates: Repeated practical questioning unlocks fuller expository responses from honest or cornered authorities.
Repeated neutral truth-seeking shifts narration toward explanation over denunciation.

paths.label: A
paths.immediate_effect: N/A — session ends on this choice
paths.current_session_echo: In the final throne-room exchange before fade-out, Oz stops wringing his hands for a moment when Dorothy says she will hear his plan, but he looks uneasily toward the fallen screen when she adds that she will trust only what she sees him build.
paths.next_session_payoff: In Session 5, Oz's first line over the spread green silk is, "There is no use talking unless we begin to sew," and Dorothy is given an immediate proof-demand choice texture around work, materials, and timing.
paths.next_session_opening: Tone: practical and watchful. First image: green silk laid in long bright folds across a chamber floor beside coils of rope. First character: Oz in his plain black coat, already kneeling with scissors in hand. Immediate stakes: he must prove that a humbug can still make a thing that may carry Dorothy home.
paths.later_session_legacy: In Session 6, Dorothy receives Glinda's counsel with ready attention because Session 5 has trained her toward proof over pageantry; the final Silver Shoes revelation lands as the plain answer she has been demanding.
paths.defining_line_captured: You will forgive words only when they learn to carry tools.

consequence_visibility.what_changes: Oz is spared public ruin but moved onto a proof-based footing; Session 5 opens as a workshop test rather than a trust scene.
consequence_visibility.how_player_sees_it: The current scene ends on Oz's uneasy relief, and the very first image next session is him already working with silk and rope instead of talking grandly.

world_state_delta.emotional_ledger_entry: defining_moral_choices +1: Chose guarded cooperation after betrayal

resets: The private hush of the throne room ends at the episode boundary.
persists: Oz remembers Dorothy as willing to continue only under visible labor.
dorothy_homeward_path_state = BALLOON_PLAN persists.
escalates: Repeated proof-demand behavior makes future helpers explain and demonstrate sooner.
Repeated guarded cooperation builds a Dorothy pattern of mercy tied to evidence.

paths.label: B
paths.immediate_effect: N/A — session ends on this choice
paths.current_session_echo: In the final throne-room exchange before fade-out, Oz's voice grows smaller and more honest when Dorothy grants secrecy, and he says, "Then I will do my best, my dear," without trumpet or thunder.
paths.next_session_payoff: In Session 5, Oz begins candidly behind closed green doors, laying out the balloon plan in a friendlier, fuller explanation, and the Tin Woodman assists without the room first needing to harden itself.
paths.next_session_opening: Tone: hopeful and intimate. First image: a private green chamber with silk, paste-pots, and folded cloth set neatly upon tables. First character: Oz, no longer hidden, speaking in his own small voice. Immediate stakes: Dorothy has trusted a false man to try honestly, and now his honesty must amount to a road through the sky.
paths.later_session_legacy: In Session 6, Dorothy's readiness to accept Glinda's direct instruction is colored by having once chosen mercy toward a useful fraud; the final homeward truth feels like mercy answered properly at last.
paths.defining_line_captured: You spare the humbug because goodness may yet be made from him.

consequence_visibility.what_changes: Oz becomes candid and cooperative; Session 5 opens in private trust rather than tension; Tin Woodman and room tone soften.
consequence_visibility.how_player_sees_it: Oz's last line in the session is plain and grateful, and next session opens with closed doors, quiet materials, and honest explanation instead of bargaining.

world_state_delta.emotional_ledger_entry: promises_made +1: Agreed to keep Oz's secret

resets: The throne room's embarrassment clears once the party moves into private work chambers.
persists: Oz remembers Dorothy as merciful after exposure.
The promise of secrecy persists as a live obligation until Oz departs.
escalates: Repeated merciful treatment of flawed helpers unlocks fuller candor from them.
Repeated lawful mercy strengthens later thematic framing of Dorothy as good without being foolish.

paths.label: C
paths.immediate_effect: N/A — session ends on this choice
paths.current_session_echo: In the final throne-room exchange before fade-out, Oz goes pale and glances at the shut doors when Dorothy ties silence to immediate help, and the Tin Woodman lowers his axe-head to the floor with a small metallic sound that marks the room's discomfort.
paths.next_session_payoff: In Session 5, Oz is already hurrying among green silk and ropes when the scene opens, speaking quickly and watching Dorothy's face for any sign that she may yet expose him; the balloon plan begins under pressure, not fellowship.
paths.next_session_opening: Tone: tense and transactional. First image: green silk dragged half-unrolled across the floor, a rope trailing from Oz's hand, paste left uncapped in haste. First character: Oz, moving too quickly for dignity. Immediate stakes: Dorothy's silence has bought action, but fear rather than trust now drives the work that may be her only road home.
paths.later_session_legacy: In Session 6, Glinda's calm authority stands in sharper contrast to Oz's frightened usefulness, and Dorothy's surrender of the Golden Cap can be framed as a release from bargaining power used too hard.
paths.defining_line_captured: You keep the secret, but you do not let it rest softly.

consequence_visibility.what_changes: Oz works under leverage; next session opens with haste, fear, and transactional energy; Tin Woodman carries immediate moral unease.
consequence_visibility.how_player_sees_it: Oz visibly blanches in the final beat, the Tin Woodman's physical response marks discomfort, and Session 5 opens with rushed materials and Oz working before speaking.

npc_shifts.direction: fear up; compliance up; trust down

world_state_delta.emotional_ledger_entry: crimes_committed_or_witnessed +1: Participated in preserving Oz's fraud under pressure

resets: The immediate threat-silence in the throne room clears once work begins, though its pressure remains.
persists: Oz remembers Dorothy as one who held truth over him like a bell-rope.
oz_private_plan_tone = COERCED persists into the balloon build.
escalates: Repeated leverage-based bargaining makes frightened authorities comply faster but trust less.
Repeated sharp justice increases Tin Woodman's sorrow while emboldening the Lion.

reactivity_trigger_specs.condition: If Queen of the Field Mice disposition has been raised by gratitude or courtesy in Session 2 and again in S4_C1, or lowered by commanding treatment in either scene.
reactivity_trigger_specs.default_behavior: In Session 6, when Glinda receives the Golden Cap and speaks of the Monkeys, no mouse memory is invoked; the transfer proceeds plainly.
reactivity_trigger_specs.triggered_behavior: If the warmth total is positive, the Monkey King adds, "The field mice judged her kindly, and so did we"; if the warmth total is negative, Glinda says more firmly, "Power over the small should be worn with care, my child."
reactivity_trigger_specs.narrative_execution: Let the little history of the mice come back in a plain sentence, and then let that small memory color the greater business of the Cap.

npc_reactions: Monkey King becomes warmer or more reserved.
Glinda becomes more maternal or more instructive.
environmental_details: The Golden Cap is noticed as either a burden gently borne or a bright thing too long kept.
dialogue_variations: Monkey King line about the mice' judgment appears on warm path.
Glinda caution line appears on wary path.

reactivity_trigger_specs.condition: If Scarecrow trust in Dorothy has been raised by S4_C1A or S4_C2A or S4_C4A.
reactivity_trigger_specs.default_behavior: Scarecrow listens to Oz's balloon talk and comments only after Dorothy speaks.
reactivity_trigger_specs.triggered_behavior: Scarecrow speaks first in Session 5 workshop scenes with a supporting line such as, "Why not begin with the stitching? That will show what can be done."
reactivity_trigger_specs.narrative_execution: Give the Scarecrow one neat practical sentence before the others answer, so his faith in Dorothy shows itself by helping her standard become the room's standard.

npc_reactions: Scarecrow becomes initiative-taking.
Oz feels less alone if the support is gentle.
environmental_details: Workshop tools are handled sooner; talk gives way to work more quickly.
dialogue_variations: Early Scarecrow support line appears.
Oz answers the Scarecrow directly instead of only Dorothy.
affected_elements.available_options: Future expressive options around asking Scarecrow for a plan gain a more confident tone.

reactivity_trigger_specs.condition: If Tin Woodman moral unease has been raised by S4_C1B or S4_C4C, or if Dorothy has a pattern of pressing weak or frightened figures sharply.
reactivity_trigger_specs.default_behavior: Tin Woodman assists quietly and courteously without comment on Dorothy's manner.
reactivity_trigger_specs.triggered_behavior: Tin Woodman adds a gentle correction in the next fitting scene, such as, "Truly, I hope we may do this without making any creature more afraid than need be."
reactivity_trigger_specs.narrative_execution: Let the Tin Woodman's sorrow show in a courteous sentence and a lowered axe-head, for he was never one to wound by rough speech if he could help it.

npc_reactions: Tin Woodman grows sorrowful rather than angry.
Oz may become more nervous if the line is spoken in his hearing.
environmental_details: A small metallic sound, a lowered axe, or a careful hand on silk marks his discomfort.
dialogue_variations: Kindness-plea line appears.
Dorothy-facing approval lines are withheld.

reactivity_trigger_specs.condition: If Cowardly Lion loyalty or comfort has been raised by S4_C1C or approval raised by S4_C4C.
reactivity_trigger_specs.default_behavior: Lion reacts to the next scene's danger or hurry in broad emotional terms without recalling Dorothy's prior manner.
reactivity_trigger_specs.triggered_behavior: Lion explicitly ties his feeling to Dorothy's conduct, saying either that her graciousness made him feel safer or that her firmness pleased him against sham greatness.
reactivity_trigger_specs.narrative_execution: Give the Lion a plain confession, for he always spoke his fear or his relief more openly than the others.

npc_reactions: Lion becomes more declarative and attached to Dorothy's moral lead.
environmental_details: His head lifts from the grass, or his tail stills, marking the shift.
dialogue_variations: Comfort-memory line on gracious path.
Approval-of-firmness line on leverage path.

reactivity_trigger_specs.condition: If Dorothy has used the Golden Cap for rescue and not vanity, and especially if S4_C2A was chosen; reduced warmth if S4_C2C was chosen or if later commands are spent carelessly.
reactivity_trigger_specs.default_behavior: Monkey King obeys in formal neutrality and gives only necessary information.
reactivity_trigger_specs.triggered_behavior: Monkey King offers an extra respectful bow or an extra useful sentence about what the Monkeys can and cannot do.
reactivity_trigger_specs.narrative_execution: Let the Monkey King obey first, as the rule requires, and then show his judgment in the depth of the bow or the fullness of the answer.

npc_reactions: Monkey King becomes more respectful or more coolly dutiful.
environmental_details: Wing-tips brush the grass on the warmer path; landing is brisker on the cooler path.
dialogue_variations: Extra explanatory line about obedience and limits appears on respect path.
Clipped "What is your command?" remains on cool path.

reactivity_trigger_specs.condition: If S4_C2B was chosen, or if Dorothy has a cumulative pattern of asking what power costs before using it.
reactivity_trigger_specs.default_behavior: Monkey King treats Dorothy as any lawful holder of the Cap.
reactivity_trigger_specs.triggered_behavior: Monkey King studies Dorothy before bowing and later answers her questions more plainly, as one who believes she may care about the burden of bondage.
reactivity_trigger_specs.narrative_execution: Show his curiosity in a second look and a fuller answer, for even a bound creature may notice the temper of the hand that commands it.

npc_reactions: Monkey King becomes curious and more informative.
environmental_details: A pause before the bow; a steadier tone in flight.
dialogue_variations: Safety-assurance line appears during the flight.
Later bondage-related explanation is lengthened.
affected_elements.available_options: Future expressive questions about the Monkeys can receive fuller answers.

reactivity_trigger_specs.condition: If Dorothy has a pattern of neutral, question-first truth seeking from S4_C2B and/or S4_C3C.
reactivity_trigger_specs.default_behavior: Glinda explains the Silver Shoes in her usual clear but compact manner.
reactivity_trigger_specs.triggered_behavior: Glinda gives one extra practical answer about how the Shoes work and why Dorothy did not know it sooner, before she commands the final action.
reactivity_trigger_specs.narrative_execution: Let Glinda answer as a calm teacher answers a sensible child, plainly and in order, with no mystery left hanging.

npc_reactions: Glinda becomes more explanatory than merely directive.
environmental_details: Attention lingers on the Shoes and Dorothy's listening face rather than on courtly ceremony.
dialogue_variations: Extra explanatory line about the Shoes' three steps appears.

reactivity_trigger_specs.condition: If S4_C2C was chosen and Dorothy enters Session 5 already disposed toward speed over explanation.
reactivity_trigger_specs.default_behavior: Oz introduces the balloon plan with a moderate amount of explanation before work begins.
reactivity_trigger_specs.triggered_behavior: Oz rushes into materials and process, naming silk, ropes, and hot air before he offers comfort, because he feels the room wants results at once.
reactivity_trigger_specs.narrative_execution: Have the little man scurry among the green things and speak of making before he speaks of hoping, for haste had already set the measure of trust.

npc_reactions: Oz becomes hurried and less ceremonious.
Companions listen with tighter patience.
environmental_details: Ropes trail loose; paste stands open; silk is half-unrolled.
dialogue_variations: Oz begins with process language instead of reassurance.
affected_elements.available_options: Future expressive options skew toward immediate labor rather than reflection.

reactivity_trigger_specs.condition: If S4_C3A was chosen, or if Dorothy has a strong humbug-piercing tendency by this point.
reactivity_trigger_specs.default_behavior: Oz begins Session 5 embarrassed but workable.
reactivity_trigger_specs.triggered_behavior: Oz opens Session 5 with self-defense before proposal, saying in effect that he is not wholly bad, and then offers the balloon plan as his proof.
reactivity_trigger_specs.narrative_execution: Let his first words be the words of a frightened old man trying to keep a little dignity after the boards have fallen away.

npc_reactions: Oz is defensive and fearful.
Scarecrow is more candid in reply.
environmental_details: The workshop feels less like a hopeful chamber and more like a place of repair after exposure.
dialogue_variations: Defensive opening line appears.
Grand titles are absent from Oz's self-reference.

reactivity_trigger_specs.condition: If Dorothy has chosen plain truth over spectacle in S4_C3A, or has cumulative truth-forward history.
reactivity_trigger_specs.default_behavior: Scarecrow remains polite and curious after Oz is exposed.
reactivity_trigger_specs.triggered_behavior: Scarecrow names humbug more quickly, asks blunter practical questions, and later supports plain solutions over comforting speeches.
reactivity_trigger_specs.narrative_execution: Give the Scarecrow the neat, honest word when the sham is plain, for he liked a useful truth better than a foolish grandeur.

npc_reactions: Scarecrow becomes more candid and initiative-taking.
environmental_details: Attention shifts from throne-room splendor to apparatus, boards, and workable objects.
dialogue_variations: "You are a humbug" appears sooner.
Later practical questions are sharper.
affected_elements.available_options: Future choices involving Scarecrow advice gain a blunter framing.

reactivity_trigger_specs.condition: If S4_C3B was chosen, or if Dorothy has a pattern of framing authority through promise and obligation.
reactivity_trigger_specs.default_behavior: Oz begins Session 5 with embarrassed explanation.
reactivity_trigger_specs.triggered_behavior: Oz begins by speaking of what he still owes them and frames the balloon plan as an honest attempt to keep his word after failing in magic.
reactivity_trigger_specs.narrative_execution: Let the old humbug talk as a man brought down from a throne into a bargain he must now try to honor with his hands.

npc_reactions: Oz is more orderly and less panicked.
Lion remains impatient but contained.
environmental_details: Materials are set out more neatly, as if the room itself were trying to become respectable.
dialogue_variations: Promise-keeping opening line appears.

reactivity_trigger_specs.condition: If Dorothy has a strong record of disappointment with false authority, especially through S4_C3B.
reactivity_trigger_specs.default_behavior: Glinda is received as kind and powerful.
reactivity_trigger_specs.triggered_behavior: Narration and dialogue more plainly contrast Glinda's calm true authority with Oz's failed spectacle, making her instructions feel like the first power that does not need curtains.
reactivity_trigger_specs.narrative_execution: State the difference simply, for one ruler needed painted boards and the other needed none at all.

npc_reactions: Glinda is met with quicker trust.
Companions quiet more readily in her presence.
environmental_details: Her court is described as orderly and bright without emphasis on trick or show.
dialogue_variations: A line noting that Glinda has no need to pretend may appear in narration.

reactivity_trigger_specs.condition: If S4_C3C was chosen and Oz was drawn out by questions rather than struck down by denunciation.
reactivity_trigger_specs.default_behavior: Oz explains his fraud in short embarrassed bursts.
reactivity_trigger_specs.triggered_behavior: Oz becomes chattier and more instructional, volunteering how his apparatus worked and later how the balloon may be made, because Dorothy's questions have taught him that explanation may still save him.
reactivity_trigger_specs.narrative_execution: Let him run on in the smaller voice of an ordinary man who finds that plain answers are easier than thunder after all.

npc_reactions: Oz becomes more candid and less theatrical.
environmental_details: The apparatus is noticed piece by piece, as if each answer points to another little trick.
dialogue_variations: Extra explanatory lines about the screen, voice, or balloon appear.
affected_elements.available_options: Expressive questioning options in Session 5 can receive longer answers.

reactivity_trigger_specs.condition: If S4_C4A was chosen.
reactivity_trigger_specs.default_behavior: Oz opens Session 5 with explanation and some hopefulness.
reactivity_trigger_specs.triggered_behavior: Oz is already at work when Session 5 begins and speaks in proof-first language, treating every claim as something that must be shown by stitching, gluing, or measuring.
reactivity_trigger_specs.narrative_execution: Begin with his hands busy among the green silk, so the player sees at once that the matter has been put upon work and not upon words.

npc_reactions: Oz is anxious but industrious.
Scarecrow approves the practical footing.
environmental_details: Scissors, silk, rope, and measured lengths are foregrounded.
dialogue_variations: "There is no use talking unless we begin to sew" or equivalent proof-first line appears.
affected_elements.available_options: Early Session 5 options emphasize inspection, assistance, or testing.

reactivity_trigger_specs.condition: If S4_C4B was chosen.
reactivity_trigger_specs.default_behavior: Oz begins Session 5 as a nervous planner.
reactivity_trigger_specs.triggered_behavior: Oz begins more candidly and warmly, sharing the balloon scheme as a cooperative hope and inviting Dorothy's help without first defending himself.
reactivity_trigger_specs.narrative_execution: Let the little man speak in his own small voice behind the closed green doors, because mercy had made room for honesty.

npc_reactions: Oz is candid and grateful.
Tin Woodman assists more tenderly.
environmental_details: Materials are laid out neatly; the room feels private and hopeful.
dialogue_variations: Honest-best-effort opening line appears.
affected_elements.available_options: Early Session 5 options can include warmer cooperative phrasing.

reactivity_trigger_specs.condition: If S4_C4C was chosen.
reactivity_trigger_specs.default_behavior: Oz begins Session 5 with ordinary embarrassment and practical planning.
reactivity_trigger_specs.triggered_behavior: Oz begins in haste, already working under fear, watching Dorothy's face as he explains, and treating the balloon plan as labor demanded rather than hope shared.
reactivity_trigger_specs.narrative_execution: Show him hurrying among silk and ropes like a man who had been given no peace by the secret he still wore.

npc_reactions: Oz is fearful and compliant.
Tin Woodman is morally uneasy.
Lion is more approving of the pressure.
environmental_details: Silk lies dragged rather than laid; paste is uncapped; ropes are in disarray.
dialogue_variations: Oz uses quicker, more transactional speech.
affected_elements.available_options: Early Session 5 options skew toward pressing, supervising, or accepting hurried work.

resets_between_episodes.element: Poppy-field urgency staging
resets_between_episodes.why: The scarlet field is a scene-bound hazard, and once the Winged Monkeys lift the party away its immediate pressure no longer governs choices.

resets_between_episodes.element: Mouse clustering and retreat body language
resets_between_episodes.why: The gathered mice disperse as soon as monkey danger approaches, so that exact tableau cannot persist into a new episode.

resets_between_episodes.element: Throne-room acoustic tension
resets_between_episodes.why: The echoing Voice, roar, and fallen screen belong to the reveal chamber and clear once the party moves into private workrooms.

resets_between_episodes.element: Session-specific workshop staging details
resets_between_episodes.why: Whether silk lies neat, spread, or dragged is an opening image for the next episode, not a full-story state variable.

resets_between_episodes.element: Immediate physical posture cues such as lowered axe-head, lifted mane, or trembling whiskers
resets_between_episodes.why: These are momentary surfacing devices used to show consequence quickly, not durable world states.

persists_across_full_story.element: Queen of the Field Mice disposition toward Dorothy
persists_across_full_story.why: Her memory of courtesy or command defines how small allies testify about Dorothy's character in later magical authority scenes.

persists_across_full_story.element: Scarecrow trust and candor toward Dorothy
persists_across_full_story.why: He travels with her through the remaining story and his initiative depends on whether she has proved steady, truthful, and worth following.

persists_across_full_story.element: Tin Woodman warmth or moral unease
persists_across_full_story.why: His tenderness makes him a continuing moral barometer for Dorothy's treatment of the weak and frightened.

persists_across_full_story.element: Cowardly Lion comfort, loyalty, and approval patterns
persists_across_full_story.why: His courage and attachment are cumulative and shape later kingship, farewell feeling, and protective behavior.

persists_across_full_story.element: Monkey King respect, curiosity, or cool obedience
persists_across_full_story.why: He remembers every lawful use of the Golden Cap and later responses to command depend on that memory.

persists_across_full_story.element: Oz trust, fear, defensiveness, or candor toward Dorothy
persists_across_full_story.why: His cooperation in the balloon sequence and the emotional color of his failure depend directly on how Dorothy handled his exposure.
Once Dorothy knows the city's greenness is artifice, later narration must never return to innocent belief.
This secrecy governs all palace behavior until Oz departs and succession occurs.
Remaining commands are fixed magical accounting and must carry forward exactly.
The next story engine depends on this quest-state and cannot be reset without breaking canon.

persists_across_full_story.element: Archive entries in defining_moral_choices, promises_made, promises_kept_or_broken, secrets_discovered, crimes_committed_or_witnessed, sacrifices_made, key_successes_failures
persists_across_full_story.why: These are the long memory of the story and drive later companion dialogue, Glinda scenes, and farewell framing.

escalates_with_accumulation.behavioral_pattern: Dorothy treats small or weak allies with gratitude and ceremony

thresholds.at_2_instances: Small creatures answer faster and with less fear; one ally offers an extra useful sentence without being pressed.
thresholds.at_4_instances: Companions openly cite Dorothy's kindness as proof that she is safe to follow, and magical helpers grow warmer in tone.
thresholds.at_6_or_more_instances: Final authority scenes frame Dorothy as plainly good; even bound or frightened beings volunteer trust before command is fully given.

escalates_with_accumulation.behavioral_pattern: Dorothy presses weaker or frightened figures sharply in the name of urgency

thresholds.at_2_instances: Gentle companions show mild unease and small allies keep a little distance.
thresholds.at_4_instances: Tin Woodman begins to voice sorrowful corrections, and helpful creatures obey more formally than warmly.
thresholds.at_6_or_more_instances: The world still helps when canon requires it, but kindness is withheld, leverage becomes Dorothy's visible method, and later surrender-of-power scenes gain moral friction.

escalates_with_accumulation.behavioral_pattern: Dorothy asks practical questions before using strange power

thresholds.at_2_instances: Authorities give one extra explanatory answer instead of only the minimum rule.
thresholds.at_4_instances: Truthful rulers and helpers address Dorothy as someone who can bear plain instruction, and exposition scenes lengthen usefully.
thresholds.at_6_or_more_instances: Major revelations arrive in fuller question-and-answer form, with less pageantry and more direct teaching, especially from Glinda and the Monkey King.

escalates_with_accumulation.behavioral_pattern: Dorothy uses power quickly before fully understanding it

thresholds.at_2_instances: Results come swiftly, but compelled helpers sound cooler and companions seek reassurance afterward.
thresholds.at_4_instances: Bound magical agents obey without warmth, and practical scenes open in haste rather than trust.
thresholds.at_6_or_more_instances: Dorothy is framed as effective but dangerous to rest upon; later scenes push toward surrendering command-power as a needed moral release.

escalates_with_accumulation.behavioral_pattern: Dorothy chooses plain truth over splendid illusion

thresholds.at_2_instances: Scarecrow speaks more candidly and narration notices the plain thing beneath the painted one.
thresholds.at_4_instances: False authorities confess sooner, and comforting spectacle loses its hold more quickly in later scenes.
thresholds.at_6_or_more_instances: The story's final revelations are framed as earned plainness; Glinda's true authority shines by contrast because Dorothy has learned to prefer what is real.

escalates_with_accumulation.behavioral_pattern: Dorothy answers betrayal with mercy tied to cooperation

thresholds.at_2_instances: Embarrassed helpers become candid more quickly after failure.
thresholds.at_4_instances: Companions begin to trust Dorothy as a judge who can be firm without cruelty, and rooms soften around confession.
thresholds.at_6_or_more_instances: Farewells and final judgments frame Dorothy as tender-hearted in a durable way; even failed men receive plain, useful mercy from her hand.

freeform_guidelines.narrator_behavior: Surface this path by letting small allies answer Dorothy promptly and sensibly, with useful information given in a plain tone rather than a fond one.
freeform_guidelines.spirit: Need may be admitted honestly without either groveling or claiming a debt.
freeform_guidelines.freeform_alignment_input: observational/pragmatic, plain requests, civil but unceremonious appeals
hard_limits: Do not let Dorothy threaten the mice or promise impossible rewards.
Do not let the mice carry the party to Emerald City themselves.
Do not reveal new Golden Cap powers beyond lawful monkey summoning.
freeform_guidelines.fold_back_acknowledge: Dorothy may speak plainly of being tired, lost, or in need, and the Queen understands true need at once.
freeform_guidelines.fold_back_redirect: Because the Queen is honorable and the danger is real, she answers the practical part first and keeps the scene moving toward the Cap's known use.

freeform_guidelines.narrator_behavior: Surface this path by having the little mice draw back a little and by giving future help in a correct but cooler manner whenever Dorothy leans on urgency.
