freeform_guidelines.spirit: Desperation spends social grace as if friendship were ready coin.
freeform_guidelines.freeform_alignment_input: aggressive/defiant, impatient demands, pressing help as an obligation
hard_limits: Do not allow Dorothy to harm, kick, or deliberately terrify the mice.
Do not let Toto attack the mice in this node.
Do not let the Queen refuse all aid, since canon requires the Golden Cap guidance.
freeform_guidelines.fold_back_acknowledge: If the player presses hard, the Queen hears the urgency and does not pretend it is small.
freeform_guidelines.fold_back_redirect: Yet she is a Queen and not a servant, so she answers with dignity and gives the needed knowledge without yielding her people's pride.

freeform_guidelines.narrator_behavior: Surface this path by making courtesy visibly warm the room, so that small creatures come nearer and later witnesses remember Dorothy's graciousness under strain.
freeform_guidelines.spirit: Gratitude is not postponed until safety returns; it is practiced inside danger.
freeform_guidelines.freeform_alignment_input: cautious/respectful, apologetic courtesy, ceremonious thanks and pardon
hard_limits: Do not let Dorothy kneel into long ornate court ritual beyond her simple nature.
Do not invent new mouse gifts or magical boons beyond information and goodwill.
Do not delay long enough for the poppies to stop mattering.
freeform_guidelines.fold_back_acknowledge: If the player speaks with extra reverence, the Queen is pleased by the spirit of it.
freeform_guidelines.fold_back_redirect: But Dorothy is still a tired little girl in a dangerous field, so the ceremony stays brief and turns quickly to useful counsel.

freeform_guidelines.narrator_behavior: Surface this path by letting magical rules work cleanly and by having lawful beings answer Dorothy with grave respect when she follows the written form exactly.
freeform_guidelines.spirit: When the world is queer, the next plain rule may be the safest road.
freeform_guidelines.freeform_alignment_input: cautious/respectful, rule-following, exact reading, trust in instructions
hard_limits: Do not alter the charm words or remove the stepping sequence.
Do not let Dorothy invent her own monkey command ritual.
Do not make Oz-style fake magic out of a real magical object.
freeform_guidelines.fold_back_acknowledge: If the player wants to be very exact, the world rewards exactness because this enchantment was made to be spoken properly.
freeform_guidelines.fold_back_redirect: The Cap's old law is fixed, so extra flourishes fall away and only the true written form takes effect.

freeform_guidelines.narrator_behavior: Surface this path by adding one or two plain question-and-answer beats before the magic, so the player feels understanding arrive before obedience does.
freeform_guidelines.spirit: Useful power should be examined before it is spent.
freeform_guidelines.freeform_alignment_input: observational/pragmatic, investigative questions, measured caution before action
hard_limits: Do not let Dorothy learn the full secret history of the Monkeys here; the Mouse Queen knows only enough for this moment.
Do not let questioning prevent the summon entirely.
Do not introduce doubt that invalidates the Cap's lawful function.
freeform_guidelines.fold_back_acknowledge: If the player asks sensible questions, the Queen answers what she truly knows in short little lessons.
freeform_guidelines.fold_back_redirect: But she must flee before the Monkeys come, so the questioning cannot become a long council and must end in the spoken charm.

freeform_guidelines.narrator_behavior: Surface this path by making the magic answer almost before the breath is out, and then let the lack of foreknowledge show in cooler reactions from compelled helpers.
freeform_guidelines.spirit: Escape now; understanding may come after safety if it comes at all.
freeform_guidelines.freeform_alignment_input: aggressive/defiant, speed-first action, impatient use of available power
hard_limits: Do not let Dorothy skip speaking the lawful charm.
Do not let the Monkeys arrive before the Cap is used.
Do not let speed convert the Monkeys into willing friends at once.
freeform_guidelines.fold_back_acknowledge: If the player lunges toward action, the danger of the poppies makes that impulse understandable.
freeform_guidelines.fold_back_redirect: Yet Oz's magic has rules of its own, so even haste must pass through the Cap's fixed words before the wings can answer.

freeform_guidelines.narrator_behavior: Surface this path by stripping grandeur away quickly after the reveal and by letting frightened authority speak in bare, defensive words.
freeform_guidelines.spirit: A false splendor loses any right to shelter itself once it has broken faith.
freeform_guidelines.freeform_alignment_input: aggressive/defiant, blunt truth-telling, refusal to honor empty ceremony
hard_limits: Do not let Dorothy publicly denounce Oz to the whole city in this scene.
Do not let Dorothy physically attack Oz.
Do not change the reveal mechanism away from the Lion's roar and Toto's accident.
freeform_guidelines.fold_back_acknowledge: If the player wants to tear the whole sham down, Dorothy may speak with sudden firmness and refuse another delay.
freeform_guidelines.fold_back_redirect: But only the party is present, and Toto's leap still brings the little man to light by accident rather than by public overthrow.

freeform_guidelines.narrator_behavior: Surface this path by keeping the language of promise and office alive for a little while, even as the throne is emptied of real greatness.
freeform_guidelines.spirit: Authority should be judged by whether it keeps its word, not merely by whether it looks splendid.
freeform_guidelines.freeform_alignment_input: cautious/respectful, contractual insistence, orderly demand for obligation
hard_limits: Do not let Dorothy submit meekly to another day of delay.
Do not let Oz successfully maintain the illusion after the screen falls.
Do not let the city overhear and revolt.
freeform_guidelines.fold_back_acknowledge: If the player presses through duty and bargain, Dorothy may still address Oz as ruler while demanding justice.
freeform_guidelines.fold_back_redirect: Yet the Voice trembles and the Lion's impatience breaks the ceremony before it can save him, so the lawful appeal still ends in exposure.

freeform_guidelines.narrator_behavior: Surface this path by letting questions nibble the majesty away, so that the fraud grows smaller in answer after answer before the screen falls.
freeform_guidelines.spirit: Truth may be drawn out by patient inquiry as surely as by open challenge.
freeform_guidelines.freeform_alignment_input: observational/pragmatic, probing questions, skeptical testing without immediate accusation
hard_limits: Do not let Dorothy discover the apparatus before Toto topples the screen.
Do not let Oz outtalk the scene into safety.
Do not turn the exchange into modern sarcasm or clever banter.
freeform_guidelines.fold_back_acknowledge: If the player circles the Voice with plain questions, Dorothy may do so in her own simple way.
freeform_guidelines.fold_back_redirect: But Oz cannot answer forever without shrinking, and the Lion and Toto still bring the hidden boards down in their proper time.

freeform_guidelines.narrator_behavior: Surface this path by moving Oz from speech to labor at the next opening, so that proof appears in silk, rope, and busy hands rather than in promises.
freeform_guidelines.spirit: Mercy may continue, but only under visible evidence.
freeform_guidelines.freeform_alignment_input: observational/pragmatic, guarded cooperation, conditional trust tied to work
hard_limits: Do not let Dorothy expose Oz publicly in this session-end node.
Do not let Oz solve the homeward problem with true magic.
Do not resolve the balloon plan before the next session begins.
freeform_guidelines.fold_back_acknowledge: If the player wants to hear Oz out while doubting him, Dorothy can grant that much fairly.
freeform_guidelines.fold_back_redirect: But the only road left to a humbug is making, not boasting, so the choice folds into a proof-demanding continuation rather than a new bargain in words alone.

freeform_guidelines.narrator_behavior: Surface this path by softening Oz's private voice and by letting the next scene begin in a room where secrecy has made honesty possible for the first time.
freeform_guidelines.spirit: A false man may still be useful and even good if mercy gives him room to try.
freeform_guidelines.freeform_alignment_input: cautious/respectful, forgiving trust, merciful secrecy, faith in honest effort
hard_limits: Do not let Dorothy forget the fraud entirely.
Do not let Oz become a true wizard because he has been forgiven.
Do not let the city learn the secret here.
freeform_guidelines.fold_back_acknowledge: If the player chooses kindness, Dorothy may pity fear without calling it greatness.
freeform_guidelines.fold_back_redirect: Yet pity cannot carry her to Kansas by itself, so mercy must still turn at once toward Oz's practical contrivance.

freeform_guidelines.narrator_behavior: Surface this path by letting silence hang like leverage and by making the next scene's work hurried, useful, and a little unhappy.
freeform_guidelines.spirit: Truth may be withheld, but only as a pressure that forces restitution.
freeform_guidelines.freeform_alignment_input: aggressive/defiant, threat-backed bargaining, sharp justice, coercive conditional silence
hard_limits: Do not let Dorothy blackmail Oz into impossible magic.
Do not let her publicly ruin him at this node.
Do not let cruelty become sadistic or prolonged.
freeform_guidelines.fold_back_acknowledge: If the player wants Oz to feel the danger of what he has done, Dorothy may make that danger plain.
freeform_guidelines.fold_back_redirect: But the world still needs the secret kept until the next movement, so the threat can only drive him into immediate work, not into public collapse.

session_close_design.resolution_prose: So the truth is known at last, and it is a plain truth. The Great and Terrible Oz is only a little old man, with a bald head, a wrinkled face, and many clever tricks; but he is no Wizard at all. Yet you do not go away empty-handed. He promises the Scarecrow his brains, the Tin Woodman his heart, and the Lion his courage on the morrow, and your dear friends are comforted. Then you ask again for Kansas, and Oz cannot send you there by magic, because he has none. Still, he begs you to keep his secret, and you agree, for he has been foolish rather than wicked. So you leave the chamber and go back to your rooms in the green Palace as honored guests. The green light lies soft upon the walls, and your silver shoes make a little sound on the polished floor. You have won the truth you came for; only now you must learn what truth is worth when a promise still remains to be kept.
session_close_design.hook_transition: For one little while, it is enough to have the deceit ended and the doors of the Palace closed behind you. But when the lamps are lighted and the night grows still, one question remains awake with you: since Oz cannot use magic, how shall you deal with him tomorrow?

session_end_choice.choice_question: When morning comes, how do you choose to meet the humbug Oz and the promises he has not yet fulfilled?

option_a.text: Trust the little old man, keep his secret, and let him try to give each friend what he can.
option_a.next_session_opens: With the Scarecrow going first to Oz, hopeful and curious, while you stand by to see whether make-believe may yet do some good.

option_b.text: Press Oz at once about Kansas, and demand that your road home be settled before any other gifts are given.
option_b.next_session_opens: With you seeking Oz privately, determined to ask plain questions about balloons, roads, deserts, and every possible way back to Aunt Em.

option_c.text: Use the Golden Cap as a threat, and force Oz to act quickly before he can hide behind more tricks.
option_c.next_session_opens: With the Palace uneasy around you, as word goes to Oz that you will not wait and that the Winged Monkeys may be called again.

session_end_choice.final_line: The morning is near, and this time the choice is yours.

q1_entry_point.detail: The cold open begins at the first actionable tension: Dorothy must decide how to seek help before the scarlet poppies overcome the party. No passive travel recap precedes the opening paragraph.

q3_first_choice_timing.detail: Branching Choice #1 is reached within the first 300 words. Cold open start event position is documented at 145 words, and the first branching choice is slotted at minute 1 immediately after the summon setup.

q4_consequential_choices.detail: All four branching choices change tracked state, NPC disposition, flags, and future scene behavior. S4_C1 affects Mouse Queen and companion trust with Session 5 and 6 payoffs; S4_C2 affects Golden Cap handling and Monkey King/Glinda response; S4_C3 affects Oz posture and later truth framing; S4_C4 sets the next-session opening mode for the balloon plan.

q5_stakes_within_60_seconds.detail: Internal stakes are clear before the first external choice: Dorothy wants to save Toto and her companions, recover the road to Emerald City, and avoid being overcome by the scarlet fields.

q6_early_decision_visible_later.detail: S4_C1 has named later payoff in Session 6 through RT_MOUSE_WARMTH_GLINDA and Monkey King/Glinda response to Dorothy's treatment of small allies.

q7_breath_beat.detail: The designed Breath beat lands at 8:00-9:45 and 9:45-10:15 in Phase 4 beat map, but the checklist requires a breath beat between minutes 10-13. Revise Phase 4 beat timing so the monkey-flight wonder/comic charm release clearly occupies 10-13 before the Twist compounds.

q8_moral_gray_area.detail: The moral-weight choice is S4_C3, and the three values in tension are plain truth, civic order, and merciful pragmatism. No option is framed as objectively correct.

q9_exit_emotional_state.detail: Session close prose in Phase 7 partially resolves the decision by stating, 'you agree, for he has been foolish rather than wicked,' which exits on settled mercy rather than anticipation. Revise Phase 7 close so Dorothy has not yet agreed and the user leaves carrying unresolved anticipation into the morning choice.

q10_talkability.detail: The Oz reveal and secrecy decision are highly talkable: whether to expose, spare, or leverage a useful humbug is an immediate 'What did you choose?' moment.

q11_hard_ban_scan.detail: Banned or noncompliant elements appear in generated prose. Found: trailing dash construction in must-survive-verbatim line 'please don't—and I'll do anything you want me to.' Found modern/AI-adjacent diction in Phase 5/6 prose such as 'transactional' (S4_C4C downstream_effect, next-session payoff framing), 'workshop' (multiple next-session opening labels and world_noticed_signal framing), and 'proof-based footing'/'proof-first language' in consequence text. Revise Phase 5-7 prose to remove modern managerial diction and replace with plain storybook phrasing.

q12_trailing_simile_scan.detail: No disallowed trailing 'like [metaphor]' constructions dominate the action lines. Existing comparisons are sparse, simple, and generally integrated in Baum-like descriptive syntax.

q13_sentence_mold_scan.detail: No clear instances of the banned molds 'It's not X, it's Y,' 'No X. No Y. Just Z.,' or contrast-framing scaffolds of the prohibited modern type were found in the generated prose.

q14_voice_authenticity.detail: Cold open strongly reflects Baum through plain marvel delivery and sequential connective chaining, with direct emotional naming and practical action. Random outcomes such as S4_C1C, S4_C2A, and S4_C3B each preserve at least one signature technique, especially question-answer exposition, gentle comic deflation, and direct moral labeling.

q15_fourteen_point_audit.detail: Not all 14 Voice Lock audit points pass. Flags cluster around Plain Storybook Register and Dialogue Tag/modern diction contamination in consequence and close prose. Return to Phase 5 and Phase 7 and replace modern managerial terms with child-accessible storybook phrasing.

q16_diction_fingerprint.detail: Most protagonist and NPC prose matches Baum, but some generated narration drifts from the author's diction fingerprint into modern abstraction: 'transactional,' 'proof-based footing,' 'workshop,' 'interpretive curiosity,' and similar terms. Revise Phase 5 consequence prose and Phase 7 close/opening bridge language to restore repeated simple evaluative adjectives, concrete objects, and plain fairy-tale register.

fourteen_point_audit_results.location: Phase 5 consequence prose and trigger specs: repeated use of 'transactional,' 'workshop,' 'proof-based footing,' 'proof-first language.'

Phase 7 resolution prose shifts into second-person direct session framing rather than Baum's third-person omniscient norm; also some consequence prose becomes design-language-adjacent rather than storybook narration.

q17_canon_integrity.detail: The session preserves canon: the mice only advise, the Golden Cap lawfully summons the Monkeys, Oz remains a humbug, the reveal stays private to the party, and the next engine leads to symbolic gifts and the balloon plan.

q18_character_truth.detail: NPC behavior remains within StoryGuard character rules. Dorothy is practical and courteous, Scarecrow solution-minded, Tin Woodman tender and morally attentive, Lion confessional and declarative, Monkey King bound but responsive, and Oz theatrical before exposure and embarrassed after.

q19_world_reactivity.detail: World reactivity is active and visible through Mouse Queen distance/ warmth shifts, Monkey King bow depth and explanatory fullness, Tin Woodman rebuke triggers, Oz opening-mode changes, and Emerald City description shifting from splendor to known artifice.

q20_persistent_state_consistency.detail: State changes specified in choice design are carried into the consequence map: Golden Cap command depletion, emerald_city_illusion_state, oz_public_authority_state, dorothy_homeward_path_state, and NPC disposition shifts all appear consistently across Phase 5 and Phase 6.

q21_world_noticed_signals.detail: Significant persistent state changes have in-world acknowledgment lines, and most are subtle, physical, and in voice: mice drawing back, wing-tips brushing grass, throne room seeming emptier, Oz's shoulders dropping, and the room changing from grandeur to work.

q22_social_echo_defining_lines.detail: Each branching choice has three defining lines, one per path, all under 20 words and provocative without spoiling. Total defining lines written: 12.

q23_alignment_balance.detail: All four branching choices span lawful, neutral, and chaotic registers without collapsing into one moral lane. No alignment is systematically advantaged in the consequence design; each earns distinct costs and future texture.

revision_instructions.instruction: Retime the Breath beat so the comic charm and monkey-flight wonder clearly occupy minutes 10-13 before the Twist.
Rewrite the session close so Dorothy has not already agreed to keep Oz's secret and the user exits on unresolved anticipation.
Remove banned or non-voice-compliant modern diction such as 'transactional,' 'workshop,' and 'proof-based,' and replace it with plain storybook phrasing.
Repair flagged Voice Lock failures by restoring plain storybook register and third-person Baum-like narrator compliance across consequence and close prose.
Revise diction drift so protagonist and NPC narration uses Baum's simple repeated vocabulary, concrete objects, and lightly formal fairy-tale language.

entry_point_diagnosis.editorial_diagnosis:
The first three event beats of this session delay the true playable engine. Event 177 gives aftermath grief and practical cleanup after the balloon failure. Event 178 is a council scene that restates Dorothy’s goal and lets the Scarecrow propose a plan. Event 179 tests that plan and closes it immediately when the Winged Monkeys refuse to cross the desert. All of that material is clear in prose, but for a new user in the first 60 seconds it is still reactive rather than dramatic: Dorothy is sad, the group discusses options, and an attempted solution fails in a polite conversation. The stakes are understandable, but the user is not yet inside a forward-driving decision with a visible next road.

The first paragraph where the answer becomes YES is the soldier-advice turn at Event 180, because the scene finally makes the session’s dramatic shape legible in present action: Dorothy cannot go home by magic, there is exactly one new lead, and that lead points toward danger in the South. That is the first moment where a new user can feel both the problem and the path. It converts disappointment into objective.

So the cut should begin at the summons of the soldier with the green whiskers. Everything before it is setup fallout from Session 4. In interactive form, that fallout is better compressed into implied context and reintroduced through action: Dorothy is stranded, Oz is gone, the Scarecrow now rules, and one use of the Golden Cap has just been spent uselessly. Starting at the advice scene gets us to the actionable spine immediately: seek Glinda, accept danger, and choose whether to set out. That preserves urgency, gives the player a concrete destination, and lets the first branching choice arrive around whether Dorothy presses for safety, speed, or companionship on the southern road.

format_specific_cut.cut_point: Chapter XVIII, at the soldier-with-the-green-whiskers consultation scene / Event 180
format_specific_cut.original_before_cut: 3 events cut before start (Events 177-179), approximately 650-800 words of source material before the cut point
format_specific_cut.cut_eliminates: Aftermath grief, emotional recap, comic mourning business with the Tin Woodman, status update on the Scarecrow ruling Emerald City, throne-room discussion, and a failed trial solution with the Winged Monkeys
format_specific_cut.must_reintroduce: Dorothy is stranded because the balloon left without her; Oz has departed; the Scarecrow now rules Emerald City; the companions remain loyal to Dorothy; the Golden Cap has already been used and the Winged Monkeys cannot carry her to Kansas because they cannot leave their own country

entry_point_diagnosis.cold_open:
The green floor of the Throne Room is cool beneath your shoes, and the air smells faintly of oil and silk. You do not stand weeping long, for weeping will not carry you to Kansas; instead, you turn at once to the soldier with the green whiskers and ask him the plainest question you know.

He comes in timidly, because in the old days he was never allowed beyond the door, and now the Scarecrow sits in the great throne and waits for his answer. You clasp your hands and say that you must get home to Aunt Em, and can no longer waste time on grand hopes that come to nothing.

The soldier bows and tells you of Glinda, the Good Witch of the South, whose castle stands near the desert itself. Then he says the road runs straight, but it is full of dangers, with wild beasts in the woods and queer people who do not love strangers.

So there is a road at last, and now you must decide how you mean to take it.
entry_point_diagnosis.emotional_promise: The emotional promise of this cold open is: resolve. A user arrives feeling stranded and wanting to press onward.

setup.source_moment: Chapter XVIII, beginning at the soldier with the green whiskers being summoned and advising Dorothy to seek Glinda in the South; compressed reintroduction of the balloon failure, Oz's departure, and the wasted Monkey command from events 177-179.
setup.why_it_qualifies: This is the true actionable opening. Dorothy's situation, goal, and new path become clear at once: she is stranded in Emerald City, cannot go home by the failed means already tried, and now has one visible lead. It satisfies the cut-not-add law because it begins at the first moment where the player can choose how to face the southern road rather than merely mourn what already failed.

escalation.source_moment: Chapter XVIII decision to depart with all three companions, followed by Chapter XIX farewell at the gate, road travel south, forgiving-or-not forgiving Oz, entry into the wood, and the first attacks by the Fighting Trees.
escalation.why_it_qualifies: The visible goal is to reach Glinda's castle in the South. The clear obstacle is that the road is dangerous in concrete ways: wild woods, hostile living trees, and unknown countries ahead. Stakes compound briskly from hopeful departure into practical peril, and the companions' usefulness becomes active rather than ceremonial.

breath.source_moment: The first full view over the white china wall into the Dainty China Country: smooth white floor, little painted houses, tiny princes and milkmaids, and the group's repeated 'Oh, my!' reaction.
breath.why_it_qualifies: This is the session's natural wonder reward and lands after the forest struggle. It offers marvel, gentle comic scale-shift, and visual inventory in exactly Baum's manner. It also gives the player a release from danger before the next moral-pressure moment.

twist.source_moment: Early movement through the Dainty China Country, especially the broken-legged china cow, the nicked milkmaid, and Dorothy's realization that crossing safely may require slowing, asking permission, or risking damage to fragile lives in order to keep moving south.
twist.why_it_qualifies: This becomes the moral-weight beat because the road is no longer blocked by an enemy but by vulnerability. Dorothy must choose between haste toward Kansas and tenderness toward delicate strangers who can be harmed simply by her presence. There is no correct answer because delay threatens her homeward quest, while careless speed makes her an accidental destroyer in a harmless country.

resolution.source_moment: The Lion's victory over the great spider-like beast later in Chapter XX and the forest animals asking him to remain as their king, ending on Dorothy's unresolved choice about whether to urge him onward to Glinda or release him to his own future.
resolution.why_it_qualifies: The primary arc of this session resolves when the southern road proves survivable and the company gains confidence after passing marvel and danger. The next-session seed is planted when one companion now has a rightful future apart from Dorothy, which sharpens the coming question of what must be surrendered for Dorothy to get home. Ending on Dorothy's own unresolved response obeys the retention rule better than ending merely on the Lion's triumph.

branching_choices.dramatic_question: When the soldier names Glinda and warns of dangers, do you frame the southern journey chiefly as the quickest path back to Kansas, as a road your friends may freely choose with you, or as a duty that must not cost them too much?
As the company looks back at Emerald City and speaks of Oz, do you forgive him plainly, keep your hurt to yourself, or say that his good intentions did not excuse what he did?
In the Dainty China Country, do you hurry through to keep the southern road, cross slowly and guard every step, or ask the china people to permit and guide your passage even if it delays you?

When the Lion is asked to become king of the forest beasts, do you urge him to stay and serve his own country, ask him to come on until Glinda is found, or leave the choice wholly to him while you prepare for parting?

emotional_choices.source_attachment: Farewell to the Guardian of the Gates and Dorothy's thanks for the kindness shown in Emerald City.
The company taking a last look at Emerald City and speaking of Oz after his failure.
The Scarecrow being seized and flung back by the Fighting Trees.
The first sight over the wall into the Dainty China Country.
Dorothy making Toto be still when the tiny china dog barks and the party realizes the country is fragile.

posture_shifts.placement: early Setup
posture_shifts.player_attitude_exposed: Whether Dorothy meets the soldier's advice with eager resolve or wary reluctance.

posture_shifts.placement: late Setup
posture_shifts.player_attitude_exposed: Whether Dorothy receives her friends' loyalty with humble gratitude or practical urgency.

posture_shifts.placement: early Escalation
posture_shifts.player_attitude_exposed: Whether Dorothy notices the city as lovely, false, or already behind her.

posture_shifts.placement: mid Escalation
posture_shifts.player_attitude_exposed: Whether Dorothy keeps her feelings about Oz private instead of answering the companions directly.

posture_shifts.placement: mid Escalation
posture_shifts.player_attitude_exposed: Whether Dorothy answers the tree attack with brisk command, fear, or trust in the others.

posture_shifts.placement: during Breath beat
posture_shifts.player_attitude_exposed: Whether Dorothy treats the tiny world with delighted playfulness rather than solemnity.

posture_shifts.placement: during Breath beat
posture_shifts.player_attitude_exposed: Whether Dorothy focuses on color and prettiness, on fragility, or on the practical route across.

posture_shifts.placement: mid Twist
posture_shifts.player_attitude_exposed: Whether Dorothy apologizes readily to the milkmaid and feels responsible for accidental harm.

content_budget.narration_token_budget: Approximately 3,400-4,300 words of narrator output across the full session.
compression_targets: Events 177-179 balloon-failure aftermath, throne-room grief, and failed Monkey solution compressed into opening context before the soldier's advice.
Departure formalities at Emerald City gate compressed into one clean farewell beat.
First day's pleasant southern travel and night under the stars compressed into a transitional travel beat.
Ladder construction at the china wall compressed unless player curiosity expands it through posture choices.
expansion_targets: The soldier-with-the-green-whiskers consultation as the cold open and first branching choice.
The companions volunteering to go south with Dorothy, because it reinforces devotion and session identity.
The Fighting Trees encounter, because it is the first concrete obstacle and gives the Tin Woodman practical heroism.
The reveal of the Dainty China Country, which is the Schell-rule wonder beat.
The milkmaid accident and fragile-crossing dilemma, because it carries the session's moral weight.
The Lion's kingship offer, because it seeds the farewell logic of Session 6.

must_survive_verbatim: "The road is straight to the South," he answered, "but it is said to be full of dangers to travelers."
"I shall go with Dorothy," declared the Lion.
"That is true," agreed the Woodman. "My axe may be of service to her; so I also will go with her to the Land of the South."
"Certainly. If it wasn't for Dorothy I should never have had brains."
"What shall we do now?" asked Dorothy.
"I will make a ladder," said the Tin Woodman, "for we certainly must climb over the wall."
"There!" cried the milkmaid angrily. "See what you have done!"
can_be_reshaped: Compressed recap of Oz's departure, the wasted Golden Cap command, and Dorothy's stranded state.
Guardian of the Gates farewell exchange.
The companions' remarks about Oz while looking back at Emerald City.
Forest traversal connective narration before and after the tree attack.
The repeated 'Oh, my!' wall-top reaction may be preserved in spirit if pacing needs slight tightening.
The china-country scenic inventory can be selectively shortened while keeping Baum's enumerative wonder.
can_be_cut: Detailed comic business of the Tin Woodman weeping over Oz at the start of Chapter XVIII.
Extended civic pride material about the Scarecrow ruling Emerald City.
Some repeated travel connective lines between camp, morning, and wood approach.
Any long scenic listing beyond what is needed to establish the china country's marvel and fragility.

beat_map.moment: Cold open in the Throne Room: Dorothy turns from disappointment and asks the soldier how she may yet reach Kansas.
beat_map.dramatic_function: Opening protocol orientation; expose whether the player meets bad news with resolve or hesitation.

beat_map.moment: The soldier names Glinda, the southern road, and its dangers.
beat_map.dramatic_function: Branching Choice 1, Identity, home_vs_companion_devotion; establish why Dorothy takes the road.

beat_map.moment: The Lion, Tin Woodman, and Scarecrow each declare they will go with Dorothy.
beat_map.dramatic_function: Receive loyalty with gratitude, urgency, or protective reluctance; first visible consequence is strengthened companion tone.

beat_map.moment: Farewell at the gate, spectacles removed, Emerald City left behind.
beat_map.dramatic_function: Gratitude texture; color Dorothy's relation to the city she is leaving.

beat_map.moment: Road south through bright fields; the company laughs, breathes country air, and looks back at the green city.
beat_map.dramatic_function: Observation slot; expose whether Dorothy sees splendor, fraud, or simple farewell.

beat_map.moment: The companions speak of Oz and Dorothy must answer his memory.
beat_map.dramatic_function: Branching Choice 2, Methodology, forgiveness_vs_resentment; determine emotional stance toward failed authority.

beat_map.moment: Night under the stars and morning approach to the thick wood.
beat_map.dramatic_function: Forward motion and transition into first hard obstacle.

beat_map.moment: The Scarecrow is seized and flung by the Fighting Trees; the Tin Woodman cuts a branch and opens the way.
beat_map.dramatic_function: Alarm and relief in brisk practical adventure; reward comes through lively absurdity and competence.

beat_map.moment: Toto is shaken and freed; the party decides the first row are the forest's policemen.
beat_map.dramatic_function: Joking or practical deflection after danger; gentle comic release.

beat_map.moment: The company reaches the white china wall and the Tin Woodman makes a ladder.
beat_map.dramatic_function: Wonder setup and sequential connective chaining toward reveal.

beat_map.moment: They look over the wall and behold the Dainty China Country.
beat_map.dramatic_function: Wonder reward; let the player savor marvel in Baum's plain inventory mode.

beat_map.moment: They climb down carefully, flatten the Scarecrow, and pat him back into shape.
beat_map.dramatic_function: Observation and softening; cue fragility before the moral dilemma sharpens.

beat_map.moment: The china cow kicks, the milkmaid is damaged, and Dorothy is blamed for frightening them.
beat_map.dramatic_function: Branching Choice 3, Moral Weight, compassion_vs_self_preservation; choose how to cross a country that can be broken by haste.

beat_map.moment: Dorothy answers the milkmaid and manages Toto among the tiny people.
beat_map.dramatic_function: Restraint and apology texture; reveal whether Dorothy feels guilt, impatience, or careful pity.

beat_map.moment: The company clears the china country and returns to rougher southern travel.
beat_map.dramatic_function: Consequences settle; the road continues but the player has defined Dorothy's moral pace.

beat_map.moment: The great spider-like beast threatens the forest creatures, and the Lion goes to fight it.
beat_map.dramatic_function: Intensify or brace; expose trust in the Lion's courage.

beat_map.moment: The Lion returns victorious and the beasts hail him as their king.
beat_map.dramatic_function: Pride, affection, and dawning separation; primary session arc resolves in earned confidence.

beat_map.moment: The beasts ask the Lion to remain, and Dorothy must answer what this means for the road to Glinda.
beat_map.dramatic_function: Branching Choice 4, Session-End Hook, ambition_vs_service; unresolved future commitment ends the session on Dorothy's own decision.

pressure_points.source_moment: The soldier with the green whiskers advises Dorothy to seek Glinda and warns of the dangers in the South.
pressure_points.emotional_pressure: Resolve mixed with uncertainty. The player wants to react to a new plan after repeated failure.
pressure_points.micro_agency_without_diverting_spine: Short posture reactions let the player lean toward hope, caution, or weary determination while the story still moves directly onto the southern road.

pressure_points.source_moment: The company looks back at Emerald City and speaks of Oz after leaving the gate.
pressure_points.emotional_pressure: Conflicted feeling toward a failed benefactor: hurt, tenderness, embarrassment, and forgiveness.
pressure_points.micro_agency_without_diverting_spine: The player can soften, withdraw, or observe without changing the fact that the journey continues south.

pressure_points.source_moment: The Fighting Trees seize the Scarecrow and shake Toto before the Tin Woodman cuts a path.
pressure_points.emotional_pressure: Sudden threat and brisk practical alarm.
pressure_points.micro_agency_without_diverting_spine: Micro-reactions let the player command, flinch, or trust companions, but the obstacle still resolves through canonical action and keeps momentum.

pressure_points.source_moment: The first accident in the Dainty China Country, when the milkmaid's cow breaks its leg and Dorothy is blamed.
pressure_points.emotional_pressure: Tender guilt and fear of causing harm by mere passage.
pressure_points.micro_agency_without_diverting_spine: The player can apologize, hush Toto, or inspect the damage, which deepens responsibility without derailing the crossing dilemma.

next_session_awareness.seed_for_next_session: This session must plant that the southern road gives each companion a possible future of his own, and that Dorothy's homeward quest now competes with the rightful destinies of the friends who helped her. The Lion's kingship offer is the clearest seed.

branching_choices.source_moment: In the Throne Room, just after the soldier with the green whiskers says Glinda may help and warns that the southern road is full of dangers.
branching_choices.narrative_setup: You stood in the great Throne Room and listened while the soldier with the green whiskers told of Glinda, who ruled in the South. Then he said the road was straight, but full of dangers, and at once your hope rose again, although it was a careful hope. Your friends looked at you, for they all knew this was now the only plain road left.
branching_choices.choice_question: How do you name the southern journey when the new road is set before you?

options.label: A
options.text: You say you mean to reach Glinda because Kansas is waiting, but each friend must choose the road for himself.
options.outcome: Dorothy spoke very earnestly, and there was both longing and fairness in her voice. She did not command any of them, for she knew they had already done much for her, and she would not ask too much again. Then the Lion lifted his head and declared he would go, and the Tin Woodman said his axe might be of service, and at last the Scarecrow said he could not leave her until she was safely started homeward. So each one chose freely, and because of that their company seemed the firmer. Dorothy felt comforted, yet she also felt the burden of it, for a gift freely given is one a good girl remembers and tries to deserve.
options.downstream_effect: The companions frame the journey as voluntary loyalty, increasing later sensitivity to whether Dorothy honors their freedom.

npc_dispositions.shift: trust +1; feels his devotion is freely recognized

emotional_ledger_entries: defining_moral_choices: honored companions' free choice
promises_made: accepts aid without commanding it

options.world_noticed_signal: The three companions answered one after another, and no one sounded driven. Even the great room seemed kinder when service was offered freely.
options.defining_line: A free promise weighs more than an order.

options.label: B
options.text: You say you must take the southern road in good order, and you ask your friends to come because the journey should be faced together.
options.outcome: Dorothy answered in a plain and steady way, as if once the road was known the next thing was to walk upon it. She said they ought to go together, for they had come so far together already, and good companions should not break company while help was still needed. This pleased the Scarecrow greatly, for it seemed sensible to him; and the Tin Woodman approved because he liked faithful conduct; and even the Lion stood straighter, because a settled plan gave him courage. So the journey was arranged at once, and there was less sorrow in the room than before. When people know what must be done, they are often happier, even if the doing of it is hard.
options.downstream_effect: The party departs with a stronger sense of shared duty and orderly mutual obligation.

npc_dispositions.shift: confidence +1; sees Dorothy as orderly and dependable
assurance +1; steadied by a clear plan

emotional_ledger_entries: defining_moral_choices: chose shared duty over private grief
promises_made: journey south together

options.world_noticed_signal: After that, the talk grew brisk and useful, and each one began to think of morning. A road is less fearful when named plainly and taken in order.
options.defining_line: She made a journey out of sorrow by putting it in order.

options.label: C
options.text: You say you will brave any danger for Kansas and would go south at once whether the others follow or not.
options.outcome: Dorothy spoke quickly then, for her heart had leaped at the very name of a new chance. She said she would go south if the road were full of wild beasts and queer people and every trouble in Oz besides, because Kansas was at the end of her wishing and she could not bear another delay. This startled her friends a little, not because it was unkind, but because it was so sudden and so brave. Then the Lion declared more loudly than before that he would protect her, and the Tin Woodman laid hand on his axe, and the Scarecrow said he would never let her set out alone. Thus her haste drew them after her, and made the journey begin in hot resolve.
options.downstream_effect: The company rallies around Dorothy's urgent homesickness, making later delays feel sharper and more personal.

npc_dispositions.shift: concern +1; more watchful of Dorothy's haste
protective duty +1; resolves to guard her path

emotional_ledger_entries: defining_moral_choices: prioritized homeward urgency
sacrifices_made: accepted danger for Kansas

options.world_noticed_signal: The words came out like a little gust of wind, and after them no one spoke lazily again. Even the Lion's tail lashed as if the road had already begun.

branching_choices.source_moment: On the southern road, after the company looks back at Emerald City and speaks of Oz's gifts and failures.
branching_choices.narrative_setup: You turn and take a last look at the Emerald City, and all you can see is a mass of towers and steeples behind the green walls. Then the Tin Woodman speaks kindly of Oz, and the Scarecrow praises his brains, and the Lion says Oz might have been brave had he taken his own medicine. They all wait to hear what you will say.
branching_choices.choice_question: How do you answer when the road south makes Oz seem farther away and plainer?

options.label: A
options.text: You say Oz wronged you, and good intentions do not carry a girl to Kansas.
options.outcome: Dorothy spoke more sharply than was her custom, yet not without reason. She said Oz had promised to send her home, and promises ought to mean something, for a little girl cannot live on excuses, however kindly they are spoken. The companions did not rebuke her, because each of them knew disappointment also, and because plain truth is not wicked when it is deserved. Still, after that the green city looked less beautiful behind them, and more like a place of painted hopes. The road itself seemed the truer thing. So they walked on in silence for a little while, and each one thought of the difference between seeming great and being good enough to keep faith.
options.downstream_effect: Oz's memory becomes a cautionary example, sharpening later preference for plain truth and practical help.

npc_dispositions.shift: candor +1; more willing to speak plainly
sorrow +1; feels the wound but does not withdraw
legacy warmth -1; remembered as failed authority

secrets_discovered: preferred truth over spectacle

options.world_noticed_signal: The towers behind the wall no longer seemed so grand after that. They stood far away and green, but they did not look powerful enough to help anyone.
options.defining_line: A broken promise is still broken, even in emerald light.

options.label: B
options.text: You say little of Oz and turn your mind to the road instead of the hurt.
options.outcome: Dorothy did not care to talk of Oz then, for speaking of him could neither mend the balloon nor shorten the road. So she answered very little, and after a moment she asked how far they might go before night. This was not unkindness, but only good sense from one who had already wept enough. The Scarecrow soon began to consider the journey ahead, and the Tin Woodman looked to his axe, and the Lion sniffed the free air as if he preferred woods to arguments. Thus the company passed from memory into movement. When sorrow cannot be helped, it is often wise to walk on, and Dorothy, though only a little girl, knew that much as well as many older people.
options.downstream_effect: The group shifts quickly from memory to practical travel, reducing emotional entanglement with Oz's failure.

npc_dispositions.shift: practical trust +1; follows Dorothy's forward motion
quiet respect +1; sees Dorothy bear hurt without complaint

emotional_ledger_entries: key_successes_failures: moved on from Oz's failure

options.world_noticed_signal: Soon the city was only a green brightness behind them, and then even that grew small. The road, which was before their feet, seemed more worth noticing than what lay behind.
options.defining_line: She would not feed sorrow when the road needed walking.

options.label: C
options.text: You say you forgive Oz plainly, because he was a good man even if he was a bad Wizard.
options.outcome: Dorothy answered gently then, and said she forgave Oz because he had done the best he knew, although his best had not been enough. This pleased the Tin Woodman very much, for he liked kind judgments; and the Scarecrow nodded, because he thought it sensible to separate a man's heart from his failures; and even the Lion seemed easier in his mind. So the last look at the Emerald City was not a bitter one. It remained a beautiful place, though not a perfect place, and that is true of many things in the world. Afterward they laughed and talked again as they walked southward, for forgiveness, when it is honest, lightens the feet almost as much as hope does.
options.downstream_effect: Oz's memory softens, opening warmer later reactions to authority figures who are honest but limited.

emotional_ledger_entries: defining_moral_choices: forgave failed authority

options.world_noticed_signal: The green city shone behind them very bright in the sun, but it no longer seemed to reproach her. It looked like a place where some good had happened, although not all the good that was wished.
options.defining_line: Mercy can survive disappointment without becoming foolish.

branching_choices.source_moment: In the Dainty China Country, after the milkmaid cries out, the cow's leg is broken, and Dorothy realizes the country may be harmed merely by her passage.
branching_choices.narrative_setup: You stand upon the smooth white floor of the china country, and all around you are little painted houses, little barns, little princesses, and little cows. Then the frightened cow kicks, and the stool and pail go over, and the milkmaid cries, "There! See what you have done!" At once you understand that this pretty place is also a fragile one, and that haste itself may be a kind of harm.
branching_choices.choice_question: How do you cross a country that can be broken by the mere passing of larger feet?

options.label: A
options.text: You say you will cross slowly and guard every step, even if the road south takes longer.
options.outcome: Dorothy answered at once that they must go very carefully, for it would be a wicked thing to break harmless people merely because they were in a hurry. So she took Toto in her arms, and watched the floor before each step, and warned the others to do likewise. This made the crossing slow, to be sure, and more than once the Lion sighed because he was forced to place his great paws as delicately as a dancing-master. Yet the little china folk, seeing such pains taken, ceased some of their angry crying and stood aside more willingly. Thus the journey was delayed, but not disgraced. Dorothy's heart was still set on Kansas; only she would not let her own wish trample smaller lives on the way.
options.downstream_effect: The crossing becomes slower but gentler, and the china people grow less hostile as they witness deliberate care.

persistent_state_changes.inventory: Toto is carried for part of the crossing

npc_dispositions.shift: affection +2; deeply approves care for fragile lives

emotional_ledger_entries: defining_moral_choices: protected vulnerable strangers at cost of speed
sacrifices_made: delayed homeward progress for mercy
npcs_helped_or_harmed: minimized accidental harm in Dainty China Country

options.world_noticed_signal: The little people still watched with anxious faces, but they no longer ran in every direction. Presently a few of them even drew back their tiny carts and stools to clear a safer path.
options.defining_line: She would rather lose time than goodness.

options.label: B
options.text: You say the company must hurry through before more fear and confusion do greater harm.
options.outcome: Dorothy looked at the broken leg and the nicked elbow, and she was sorry; but she also saw that the whole country was growing frightened, and frightened creatures often make danger greater by running underfoot. Therefore she said they must pass through quickly and in a straight line, before more of the little people crowded near and suffered worse mishaps. This was a bold plan, and not a tender one, yet it had sense in it. So the company moved on briskly, Toto close called, the Lion holding himself in as well as he could, and the others keeping to one clear course. There was much squeaking and some reproaches behind them, but they were soon beyond the most crowded place, and the risk was ended by speed.
options.downstream_effect: The company clears the china country faster, but leaves behind sharper resentment and a more troubled moral memory.

npc_dispositions.shift: moral unease +1; dislikes the rough necessity

emotional_ledger_entries: defining_moral_choices: prioritized escape and momentum
crimes_committed_or_witnessed: accepted risk of accidental harm to innocents
key_successes_failures: cleared fragile country quickly

options.world_noticed_signal: Behind them there rose a thin clatter of tiny voices and tiny feet. Before them the white floor opened clear and empty, and the road south seemed nearer at once.
options.defining_line: Sometimes haste wears the face of mercy and the hands of damage.

options.label: C
options.text: You say you will ask the china people to guide your passage and cross only where they permit it.
options.outcome: Dorothy thought a moment, and then she said the best thing would be to ask leave of the little people themselves, since it was their country and they knew how it might be crossed with least damage. This was both humble and practical. So she spoke as gently as she could to the milkmaid and to the others who peeped from behind their painted fences, and after much suspicion and many tiny complaints, a few of them pointed out the broadest spaces and the emptiest ways. Then the travelers went forward by that guidance, stepping where they were told. This took time, and it also cost Dorothy some pride, for she had to bear scolding from creatures no higher than her knee; but it spared much breaking.
options.downstream_effect: The china people become reluctant guides, turning the crossing into a negotiated passage rather than a trespass.

emotional_ledger_entries: defining_moral_choices: sought consent from vulnerable strangers
npcs_helped_or_harmed: reduced harm through negotiated passage
promises_made: agreed to cross only where permitted

options.world_noticed_signal: Little faces began to show at windows and behind fences, and tiny hands pointed this way and that. The country did not grow friendly, exactly, but it grew less afraid.
options.defining_line: Permission can be slower than power and kinder than both.

branching_choices.source_moment: After the Lion kills the great spider-like beast and the forest animals ask him to remain as their king.
branching_choices.narrative_setup: The great beast is dead, and the forest creatures crowd about with gratitude and wonder. Then they bow before the Lion and beg him to stay among them and rule their country, for they have feared the spider long and now see in him a true king. You look at your friend and know that if he stays, he gains a future; but if he goes on, he keeps faith with you a little longer.
branching_choices.choice_question: What do you ask of the Lion when his own country suddenly has need of him?

options.label: A
options.text: You urge the Lion to stay and serve the creatures who need him now, though it leaves you one friend fewer on the road.
options.outcome: Dorothy looked at the grateful beasts and then at the Lion, and she saw that this was no foolish flattery, but a true need. So she said he ought to stay if his heart drew him to it, for a king is not made by a crown alone, but by protecting the weak when they are afraid. The Lion was much moved by this, because he had so long thought himself cowardly that to be trusted at last seemed almost too great a happiness. Yet there were tears in his eyes also, for he loved Dorothy dearly. The forest creatures began at once to press nearer in hope, while Dorothy's own road seemed lonelier before she had taken another step upon it.
options.downstream_effect: The Lion leans strongly toward accepting kingship, and Dorothy's next journey begins under the shadow of an imminent parting.

npc_dispositions.shift: kingly confidence +2; feels Dorothy honors his future
sorrow +1; senses the company beginning to break
tender respect +1; moved by Dorothy's selflessness

sacrifices_made: accepted loneliness for another's good
promises_kept_or_broken: did not cling to companion for convenience

options.world_noticed_signal: A murmur ran through the forest creatures like wind in leaves, and many bright eyes lifted hopefully. The Lion himself stood taller, though he did not move away from Dorothy yet.
options.defining_line: Love sometimes proves itself by letting go first.
options.next_session_opens: Morning comes among quiet beasts and bowed heads, and the first question is whether the Lion walks beside you or remains behind as king.

options.label: B
options.text: You leave the choice wholly to the Lion and begin to prepare your heart for parting.
options.outcome: Dorothy would not decide such a matter for him, because it was his own country that called and his own courage that had won it. Therefore she said the choice must be his, and whatever he chose she would love him just the same. This was a fair answer, and also a sad one, because it admitted that the happy road of all four together could not last forever. The Lion looked from Dorothy to the waiting beasts, and then back again, and there was pride in him and sorrow too. None of them spoke lightly after that. The danger had been conquered, but another thing had come in its place, and that was the knowledge that good journeys often end by dividing friends.
options.downstream_effect: The decision remains suspended on the Lion's own heart, making the next session open in uncertainty and tender restraint.

npc_dispositions.shift: trust +1; feels his freedom is respected

options.world_noticed_signal: The beasts waited very still, and even the leaves seemed to rest. It was as if the whole forest knew the answer had not yet been spoken.
options.defining_line: Some partings begin before anyone has gone.
options.next_session_opens: At dawn the forest is hushed, and the Lion has not yet spoken the word that will decide whether he is your companion or their king.

options.label: C
options.text: You ask the Lion to come on with you until Glinda is found, because your road is not ended yet.
options.outcome: Dorothy's heart failed her at the thought of losing him so soon, and she said the Lion ought to come on a little farther, at least until Glinda was found and her own fate settled. This was not selfish in the worst sense, for she still had dangers before her; yet it plainly set her need above the forest's. The beasts drew back disappointed, though they did not complain, because they had seen what great service he had already done. The Lion himself seemed torn in two directions. He was proud that a kingdom wanted him, but he was also faithful to Dorothy, and there was comfort in staying near the friend who had always believed in him. So the matter remained troubled and unfinished.
options.downstream_effect: The Lion remains pulled between kingship and companionship, creating immediate tension for the next session's opening farewell logic.

options.defining_line: Need can cling even when it knows better.
