emotional_choices.source_moment: At the gate of Emerald City, when the Guardian of the Gates unlocks the green spectacles and Dorothy says farewell.
emotional_choices.narrative_lead_in: The Guardian of the Gates gives them many good wishes, and the green spectacles are taken away at last. You stand at the outer gate, leaving behind a city where you were kindly treated, though it could not keep you.
emotional_choices.choice_question: How do you thank the city as you leave it?

options.label: A
options.text: You thank him carefully for every kindness shown to you in the lovely City.
options.outcome: Dorothy spoke very prettily then, and named the kindness of the City as well as the kindness of the man before her. This pleased the Guardian, who was formal by habit but good-natured at heart, and he bowed lower than before. The leave-taking became orderly and warm, and even the gate itself seemed less like a boundary than a courteous farewell. Her companions waited patiently, for gratitude is never wasted when it is sincere, and Dorothy, though in haste for Kansas, did not think good manners a hindrance to any journey.
options.tonal_effect: Narration leans ceremonious, warm, and approving of good order.

emotional_ledger_entries: npcs_helped_or_harmed: honored Emerald City kindness

options.world_noticed_signal: The Guardian smiled in a way that made him seem less official and more kind.

options.label: B
options.text: You thank him simply and say you hope to remember the City kindly.
options.outcome: Dorothy answered in a plain way, neither making too much of the matter nor too little. She said she would remember the City kindly, and that was true, for there had been both disappointment and goodness within its walls. The Guardian seemed content with this, because simple thanks are often the best sort. Then the gate was opened wide, and the road outside looked broad and honest after all the green splendor within. So they went forward, carrying memory with them, but not lingering over it.
options.tonal_effect: Narration grows plain, balanced, and forward-looking.

emotional_ledger_entries: key_successes_failures: left Emerald City on good terms

options.world_noticed_signal: The gate swung back without creaking, as if even it did not wish to delay them.

options.label: C
options.text: You thank him quickly and turn your face to the road before your voice can fail.
options.outcome: Dorothy gave thanks, but very quickly, for if she had lingered she might have cried again, and she did not wish to begin the new journey in tears. So she turned at once toward the South, and the briskness of the act made the others follow without more ceremony. This was not ungrateful, only eager and a little sore-hearted. The Guardian understood enough of such feelings to trouble her no further. Thus the farewell was short, but it was not cold, and the road gained them sooner.
options.tonal_effect: Narration sharpens into brisk motion with feeling kept under control.

emotional_ledger_entries: sacrifices_made: cut short farewell to keep moving

options.world_noticed_signal: Behind them the green gate closed gently, and no one called them back.

emotional_choices.all_paths_arrive_at: The company leaves Emerald City and travels south through bright fields.

emotional_choices.source_moment: On the road after leaving the gate, when the companions look back at Emerald City and think of Oz.
emotional_choices.narrative_lead_in: The sun shines bright, and the country air does the Lion good. Yet when the green city grows small behind you, the talk turns soft for a little while.
emotional_choices.choice_question: What feeling do you let show when Oz is remembered?

options.label: A
options.text: You let the sadness be there, but you do not speak much of it.
options.outcome: Dorothy's face grew quiet then, and although she did not say many words, the others could see she was sorry as well as disappointed. This made the moment tender without making it heavy, for no one pressed her to explain what was plain enough already. They walked on side by side, and their companionship itself became the answer. There are times when silence is not emptiness at all, but only feeling that has no need of many names.
options.tonal_effect: Narration softens and notices companionship more than speech.

emotional_ledger_entries: defining_moral_choices: carried sadness without complaint

options.world_noticed_signal: NO SIGNAL — state change is minor

options.label: B
options.text: You say you are sorry for Oz, though he failed you sadly.
options.outcome: Dorothy spoke with kindness then, and said she was sorry for Oz because he had meant well and had come to a poor ending. This gave the company a gentler mood, for pity is a quiet thing and often settles people better than blame. The Tin Woodman was especially pleased, and his bright face looked almost cheerful again. So the green city behind them became less a place of fraud than a place where a weak man had tried and failed.
options.tonal_effect: Narration warms toward mercy and mild moral clarity.

emotional_ledger_entries: defining_moral_choices: pitied one who failed

options.world_noticed_signal: NO SIGNAL — state change is minor

options.label: C
options.text: You say you had rather trust roads than Wizards now.
options.outcome: Dorothy answered with a little firmness, and said she would sooner trust a plain road under her feet than another grand promise from a Wizard. This made the Lion laugh, and even the Scarecrow smiled, for there was truth in it and no great malice either. The feeling of the company grew brisker after that, as if plain things had won a small victory over splendid ones. So they walked on with lighter steps, preferring what could be seen and used.
options.tonal_effect: Narration turns plainer and a little more humbug-piercing.

emotional_ledger_entries: secrets_discovered: favors plain truth over spectacle

options.world_noticed_signal: NO SIGNAL — state change is minor

emotional_choices.all_paths_arrive_at: The company camps under the stars and reaches the thick wood next morning.

emotional_choices.source_moment: The Scarecrow is seized by the Fighting Trees and flung back, and Toto is shaken before the Tin Woodman cuts him free.
emotional_choices.narrative_lead_in: The branches bend down as if they were arms, and the Scarecrow comes flying back among you. Then Toto howls, and the Tin Woodman raises his axe.
emotional_choices.choice_question: How does your feeling break out in the first surprise of the tree attack?

options.label: A
options.text: You cry out sharply and call for the others to run through at once.
options.outcome: Dorothy gave a quick cry then, and her alarm ran ahead of her like a little bell. Yet it was useful alarm, for it made everyone move the instant the Tin Woodman opened a way. The Scarecrow scrambled up, the Lion gathered himself, and Toto was snatched free before worse could happen. Afterward Dorothy was still trembling a little, but the danger had passed, and there was no harm in being frightened when one also has the sense to act quickly.
options.tonal_effect: Narration becomes brisk, urgent, and practical after the fright.

emotional_ledger_entries: key_successes_failures: reacted quickly under sudden danger

options.world_noticed_signal: The branches whipped and shook, but your companions were already in motion.

options.label: B
options.text: You call to Toto and the others in as steady a voice as you can manage.
options.outcome: Dorothy was frightened, to be sure, but she tried to keep her voice steady, because frightened voices often make danger worse. So she called to Toto and to the others as clearly as she could, and this steadied the whole company while the Tin Woodman did his work. The odd battle was over in very little time, and then even the absurdity of fighting trees began to show itself. For in Oz, as you know, many strange things happen and are afterward managed quite practically.
options.tonal_effect: Narration emphasizes composure and practical mastery over absurd danger.

emotional_ledger_entries: defining_moral_choices: steadied companions in danger

options.world_noticed_signal: The Lion's ears lifted at your voice, and even Toto turned toward it through his howling.

options.label: C
options.text: You gasp, then trust the Tin Woodman to clear the way.
options.outcome: Dorothy could only gasp at first, for the thing was so sudden and so queer that it took her breath away. But when she saw the Tin Woodman stride forward with his axe, she knew at once what help looked like and did not hinder him with needless cries. This gave the moment a strange calm inside the confusion. Then the branch was cut, Toto was freed, and the whole matter was ended as quickly as it had begun. Dorothy was very glad indeed to have such useful friends.
options.tonal_effect: Narration highlights trust in companions and relief after absurd peril.

emotional_ledger_entries: npcs_helped_or_harmed: relied on companion competence

options.world_noticed_signal: NO SIGNAL — state change is minor

emotional_choices.all_paths_arrive_at: The company passes the first row of Fighting Trees and reaches the white china wall.

emotional_choices.source_moment: The company sits atop the wall and looks down upon the Dainty China Country for the first time.
emotional_choices.narrative_lead_in: You look over the wall and see a white shining floor, little painted houses, and tiny princes and milkmaids all made of china. It is a marvelous sight, and so queer that even the Lion can only cry, "Oh, my!"
emotional_choices.choice_question: What part of the marvel lays hold of you first?

options.label: A
options.text: You marvel most at how small and complete the whole country is.
options.outcome: Dorothy stared with wide eyes, not because any one thing was grand, but because everything was little and finished together. The houses, the fences, the cows, the people, and even the tiny dog seemed made to fit one another exactly, like pieces in a very pretty plaything. This made her wonder quiet and deep, and she forgot the rough wood behind them for a moment. Such marvels in Oz often came upon one suddenly, and then stood plain before the eyes as if they had always been there.
options.tonal_effect: Narration leans into plain marvel and enumerative scenic delight.

emotional_ledger_entries: key_successes_failures: received wonder with attentive curiosity

options.world_noticed_signal: NO SIGNAL — state change is minor

options.label: B
options.text: You cannot help smiling at a country where even the dog barks in a tiny voice.
options.outcome: Dorothy's wonder came with a little laugh, for the whole country was so dainty and exact that it seemed almost too queer to be solemn over. The tiny dog with the large head pleased her especially, and she had much ado not to laugh again when it barked in such a small voice and then ran away. This did not lessen the marvel; indeed it made it kinder. For some wonders are so pretty that they ask to be loved as much as admired.
options.tonal_effect: Narration grows lighter and more gently comic without losing wonder.

emotional_ledger_entries: defining_moral_choices: met marvel with delighted playfulness

options.world_noticed_signal: NO SIGNAL — state change is minor

options.label: C
options.text: You marvel, but you also notice at once how easily such a country might be broken.
options.outcome: Dorothy thought the country beautiful, but she also saw at once how smooth and hard the white floor was, and how frail the little creatures must be. Therefore her wonder was touched with caution from the first. This did not make the place less lovely; it only made it more serious. A pretty thing that may be broken asks for better behavior than one that can bear rough handling, and Dorothy felt this before any harm was done at all.
options.tonal_effect: Narration braids wonder with early moral caution.

emotional_ledger_entries: secrets_discovered: recognized fragility within beauty

options.world_noticed_signal: NO SIGNAL — state change is minor

emotional_choices.all_paths_arrive_at: The company climbs down into the china country and begins to cross it.

emotional_choices.source_moment: After the milkmaid's angry outcry, when Dorothy must answer and keep Toto still among the tiny china people.
emotional_choices.narrative_lead_in: The milkmaid goes off sulkily with the broken-legged cow, and little reproachful eyes seem to peep at you from every painted corner. Toto is eager and curious, which is not always the safest thing in a fragile land.
emotional_choices.choice_question: What feeling governs your next small act in the china country?

options.label: A
options.text: You hush Toto at once and speak another gentle apology.
options.outcome: Dorothy put her hand upon Toto and made him be still, and then she repeated her apology as gently as she could. This did not mend the cow's leg, of course, but it showed the little people that she was not careless of what had happened. The mood of the place remained touchy, yet it grew less sharp around them. Even in Oz, where strange things happen every day, kindness after a mistake is better than pride.
options.tonal_effect: Narration becomes tender, corrective, and morally plain.

emotional_ledger_entries: promises_kept_or_broken: accepted responsibility after accidental harm

options.world_noticed_signal: A few shutters that had been slammed quite tight opened a very little again.

options.label: B
options.text: You keep Toto close and watch the tiny roads for the safest way through.
options.outcome: Dorothy caught Toto close to her skirt and turned her eyes to the ground and the little roads, for she saw that feeling sorry must now be followed by doing wisely. This practical care suited the moment well. The china people were still angry, but anger matters less when one stops giving it fresh cause. So the company gathered itself and moved with better sense than before.
options.tonal_effect: Narration turns practical, observant, and careful.

emotional_ledger_entries: key_successes_failures: shifted quickly from apology to caution

options.world_noticed_signal: NO SIGNAL — state change is minor

options.label: C
options.text: You whisper to Toto that this is no place for barking, and try to smile away the trouble.
options.outcome: Dorothy bent down to Toto and whispered very earnestly that this dainty country was no place for barking, and then she tried to smile a little, as if the trouble might be made smaller by gentleness and cheer. The smile did not please the milkmaid, who was too angry for that, but it kept Dorothy herself from growing frightened or cross. Thus the company's spirits did not sink entirely, even while they walked more carefully than before.
options.tonal_effect: Narration stays light enough to preserve breath within tension.

emotional_ledger_entries: defining_moral_choices: used cheer to soften fear

options.world_noticed_signal: NO SIGNAL — state change is minor

emotional_choices.all_paths_arrive_at: The company continues through the china country under the consequences of the crossing method already chosen.

posture_shifts.placement: Cold open in the Throne Room, just before the soldier finishes warning that the southern road is full of dangers.
posture_shifts.narrator_line: At the word dangers, you felt your little heart give a jump, though whether it leaped forward or drew back was another matter.

options.label: A
options.text: You lift your head at once.
options.narration_adjustment: Dorothy looked up quickly, as if even danger were better than waiting. A road with trouble on it was still a road, and that was more than she had a moment before.

options.label: B
options.text: You hold your breath before speaking.
options.narration_adjustment: Dorothy was not eager for more dangers, and no sensible girl would be. But she listened all the more carefully because fear, when it is honest, likes to know what lies ahead.

posture_shifts.placement: After the Lion, Tin Woodman, and Scarecrow each offer to go south with Dorothy.
posture_shifts.narrator_line: Their kindness came so freely that you could not help showing in your face what it meant to you.

options.label: A
options.text: You smile at them with gratitude.
options.narration_adjustment: Dorothy smiled very sweetly upon them, and her thanks were plain enough even before she spoke. Such looks are a kind of gift, and good friends know their value.

options.label: B
options.text: You nod and speak of starting soon.
options.narration_adjustment: Dorothy was grateful, but she turned gratitude quickly into movement. For when the way home is spoken of, a little girl may well think first of setting out.

options.label: C
options.text: You look troubled at asking so much.
options.narration_adjustment: Dorothy's pleasure was touched with concern, because she knew the road might cost them dear. She was not one to take devotion as if it were her right.

posture_shifts.placement: After leaving the gate, when the company turns for a last look at Emerald City.
posture_shifts.narrator_line: You looked back at the green city, and what you saw there depended less on the walls than on your own mind.

options.label: A
options.text: You see beauty still.
options.narration_adjustment: The towers and steeples still looked very bright and beautiful in the sun. A humbug may live in a fair city, and yet the city remain fair to the eye.

options.label: B
options.text: You see painted make-believe.
options.narration_adjustment: Dorothy could not forget the green spectacles and the little old man behind the screen. So the beauty looked thinner to her now, because she had seen what lay beneath it.

options.label: C
options.text: You see only something left behind.
options.narration_adjustment: The city was already becoming a thing behind her, and behind things have less hold than roads before one's feet. Dorothy's thoughts were turned southward now.

posture_shifts.placement: During the talk of Oz, just before the branching answer lands.
posture_shifts.narrator_line: For one little moment you might have kept your feelings close, as children often do when they are most in earnest.

options.label: A
options.text: You look down before answering.
options.narration_adjustment: Dorothy's eyes fell for a moment, and the others could see there was feeling in her which she did not spend lightly. Then she answered as she thought best.

options.label: B
options.text: You meet them plainly at once.
options.narration_adjustment: Dorothy did not hide her mind when it was time to speak it. She looked from one to another and answered plainly, as was her custom when things mattered.

posture_shifts.placement: At the tree attack, when the Tin Woodman has just cut the first branch and calls, 'Be quick!'
posture_shifts.narrator_line: The odd battle was not the sort that allowed much thinking, but even then your body chose its own manner of courage.

options.label: A
options.text: You dart forward at once.
options.narration_adjustment: Dorothy ran the instant there was a way, and that was very sensible. In some dangers, quick feet are as good as brave speeches.

options.label: B
options.text: You snatch first for Toto.
options.narration_adjustment: Dorothy's first thought was for the little dog, as it often was. Even when branches were flying about, she did not forget her own.

options.label: C
options.text: You trust the others and follow their opening.
options.narration_adjustment: Dorothy saw that each friend had his proper use, and she did not spoil good help by confusion. So she followed where their courage had already made a passage.

posture_shifts.placement: After Toto is freed and the party decides the first row of trees were the forest's policemen.
posture_shifts.narrator_line: Now that the danger was past, the queer side of it began to show, for there is something very odd in being turned back by policemen who grow out of the ground.

options.label: A
options.text: You laugh a little in relief.
options.narration_adjustment: Dorothy laughed then, because the thing was over and because it was too strange to remain solemn forever. Relief often comes dressed in a smile.

options.label: B
options.text: You smooth Toto and keep walking.
options.narration_adjustment: Dorothy was glad enough, but she spent that gladness in quiet ways. She soothed Toto and kept the company moving, which was useful and kind.

posture_shifts.placement: On top of the china wall, before climbing down into the Dainty China Country.
posture_shifts.narrator_line: The little country lay below like a painted dish, and your eyes settled first where your nature sent them.

options.label: A
options.text: You stare at the bright colors.
options.narration_adjustment: The reds and blues and golds pleased Dorothy greatly, for the whole country shone like a toy in the sun. It was a cheerful sort of marvel.

options.label: B
options.text: You notice how delicate everything is.
options.narration_adjustment: Dorothy could see at once that all this pretty finery might be broken by rough hands or careless feet. So her wonder had caution in it from the beginning.

options.label: C
options.text: You search for the easiest path across.
options.narration_adjustment: Even while she wondered, Dorothy looked for a way through. Marvels were well enough, but she had still to get to the other side of them.

posture_shifts.placement: Just after the milkmaid's accusation and before the crossing method is chosen.
posture_shifts.narrator_line: The milkmaid's little anger pricked you more than a larger person's might have done, because small hurt is often the sharpest to look at.

options.label: A
options.text: You blush and step back carefully.
options.narration_adjustment: Dorothy was truly ashamed of the mischief, though it had not been meant. She drew back at once, as if even her shadow ought to tread more softly there.

options.label: B
options.text: You kneel to inspect the damage.
options.narration_adjustment: Dorothy bent down to see exactly what harm had been done, for she liked to know the truth of a trouble before trying to mend it. This was a useful habit in Oz as elsewhere.

options.label: C
options.text: You gather Toto closer without a word.
options.narration_adjustment: Dorothy said nothing for a moment, but took better charge of Toto. Sometimes the quickest apology is to stop the mischief from growing.

present_npcs.current_disposition: polite, practical, slightly timid in the throne room

scene_rules_layer_4.character_knowledge_limits: No one knows a sure route home except that Glinda may know more; the Monkey King has already refused Kansas travel.
scene_rules_layer_4.emotional_context: Fresh disappointment after balloon failure, then a sharp return of hope.

scene_rules_layer_4.character_knowledge_limits: The company knows only what they can see about fragility and local anger; they do not know the full layout except by observation or asking.
scene_rules_layer_4.emotional_context: Wonder turned delicate and morally charged by accidental harm.

The china people remain tiny and local to their country
