present_npcs.current_disposition: relieved, thoughtful about the company's future
grateful, hopeful, eager for a king

scene_rules_layer_4.character_knowledge_limits: The beasts know the Lion saved them; no one knows yet whether he will stay or go on. Dorothy still does not know how Glinda will solve Kansas.
scene_rules_layer_4.emotional_context: Earned triumph giving way to the sadness of possible parting.
canon_boundaries_active: The Lion defeats the spider-like beast
The beasts ask him to remain as king
The session must end before the matter is settled
freeform_risk_areas: Player tries to refuse the beasts' plea outright for all parties
Player attempts to crown another companion
Player tries to resolve Glinda's answer before next session

paths.label: A
paths.immediate_effect: In the Throne Room reply chain, the Lion says, "Then I will go, if you will have me," before the Tin Woodman offers his axe and the Scarecrow says he will see Dorothy safely started; each speaks as if volunteering, not obeying.
paths.current_session_echo: Fighting Trees scene: when the branches begin to strike, the Scarecrow says, "We chose this road with you, so let us choose the next step also," and offers a plan before anyone rushes forward.
paths.next_session_payoff: Farewell to the Lion in Glinda's country: the Lion says, "You never drove me, Dorothy; so I may stay a king and still know I was your friend by my own choice."
paths.later_session_legacy: Final farewell cluster in Session 6: the Scarecrow frames each goodbye as something freely given, and Dorothy's parting lines are answered with gratitude rather than duty.
paths.defining_line_captured: A free promise weighs more than an order.

consequence_visibility.what_changes: Companion language shifts from obligation to volunteered loyalty.
consequence_visibility.how_player_sees_it: The three companions answer one by one with self-chosen phrasing, and later the Scarecrow explicitly says they chose the road with her during the Fighting Trees obstacle.

npc_shifts.direction: trust up; feels his devotion is freely recognized
loyalty up; companionship chosen without pressure

world_state_delta.emotional_ledger_entry: defining_moral_choices +1; promises_made +1

resets: Throne Room volunteer cadence clears after departure from Emerald City because it is scene-specific court staging.
persists: Companions remember that Dorothy let them choose the road for themselves.
home_vs_companion_devotion path memory remains available for Session 6 farewell logic.
escalates: Repeated respect for companion autonomy makes later companions speak more boldly and with less apology.

paths.label: B
paths.immediate_effect: At once after Dorothy speaks, the Scarecrow begins arranging the morning in order, the Tin Woodman asks that the axe and oil-can be made ready, and the Lion says, "A settled plan is a comfort."
paths.current_session_echo: China wall ascent: before the climb, the Tin Woodman checks that Toto is lifted first, then Dorothy, then the others, and remarks that they have done best whenever they kept good order.
paths.next_session_payoff: At Glinda's gate in Session 6, the guardian maidens admit the party with little delay because Dorothy presents herself plainly and in order, and Glinda's first tone toward her is, "You have come properly, my child."
paths.later_session_legacy: Session 6 farewell sequencing: Dorothy says goodbye in a composed order, and the narration notices that she turns sorrow into one thing after another instead of letting it master her.
paths.defining_line_captured: She made a journey out of sorrow by putting it in order.

consequence_visibility.what_changes: The party immediately behaves like an organized company with named roles and sequence.
consequence_visibility.how_player_sees_it: Companions start practical preparation talk at once, and later the wall-crossing is narrated as an orderly procedure rather than a scramble.

npc_shifts.direction: confidence up; sees Dorothy as orderly and dependable
assurance up; steadied by a clear plan

world_state_delta.emotional_ledger_entry: defining_moral_choices +1; promises_made +1

resets: Morning departure logistics clear at the next episode boundary because the packing itself is not story-level.
persists: The party's sense of shared duty carries into Session 6 audience and farewell scenes.
home_vs_companion_devotion path memory remains available for final parting cadence.
escalates: Repeated orderly choices make gatekeepers and rulers answer Dorothy with quicker trust and clearer instruction.

paths.label: C
paths.immediate_effect: The Lion's tail lashes, he steps forward first, and says, "Then none of us shall let you go alone"; the Tin Woodman grips his axe at once, and the room's talk turns from counsel to immediate departure energy.
paths.current_session_echo: In the thick wood before the Fighting Trees, the Lion pushes ahead of the others sooner than usual and warns Dorothy back with, "Keep close to me," showing heightened protectiveness born of her urgent speech.
paths.next_session_payoff: Session 6 opening on the road to Glinda: the Lion is the first to speak, pacing before Dorothy and asking whether she is weary, because he still treats every delay as a threat to her getting home.
paths.later_session_legacy: At the final farewell, the Lion's grief lands more sharply because he has spent the southern journey in a heightened protective posture toward Dorothy's homesick haste.
paths.defining_line_captured: Homesickness can be as bold as any sword.

consequence_visibility.what_changes: The Lion becomes visibly more protective and the group's tempo turns urgent.
consequence_visibility.how_player_sees_it: His body language changes at once in the Throne Room, and in the next hazard he physically places himself ahead of Dorothy before anyone asks him to.

npc_shifts.direction: protectiveness up; stirred by Dorothy's boldness
concern up; more watchful of Dorothy's haste
protective duty up; resolves to guard her path

world_state_delta.emotional_ledger_entry: defining_moral_choices +1; sacrifices_made +1

resets: Throne Room surge of immediate departure talk clears once the road scenes begin.
persists: The Lion's heightened protectiveness toward Dorothy persists into Session 6.
session5_homeward_urgency_emphasized remains available for farewell tone.
escalates: Repeated home-first urgency makes delays feel personally sharper in narration and provokes more protective companion interruptions.

paths.label: A
paths.immediate_effect: After Dorothy says Oz wronged her, the company falls into a marked silence; the Emerald City behind them is described not as splendid but as far green towers that cannot help her, and the Scarecrow does not answer at once.
paths.current_session_echo: China Country entry: Dorothy notices the pretty houses first as things that may be broken, not things to admire, and the narration strips the marvel to its practical danger more quickly than on other paths.
paths.next_session_payoff: When Glinda explains the Silver Shoes in Session 6, Dorothy answers with plain urgency rather than ceremony, and Glinda says, "That is true enough," before giving the instruction without delay.
paths.later_session_legacy: The final use of the Shoes is framed as plain truth at last, not as a marvelous reward; the narration stresses usefulness over splendor.
paths.defining_line_captured: A broken promise is still broken, even in emerald light.

consequence_visibility.what_changes: Oz's memory becomes visibly colder, and descriptions of Emerald City and later marvels lose some softness.
consequence_visibility.how_player_sees_it: The companions go quiet immediately, the city is described in barer terms, and the next wonder scene in China Country is framed first as a hazard of breakage.

npc_shifts.direction: candor up; more willing to speak plainly
sorrow up; feels the wound but does not withdraw
legacy warmth down; remembered as failed authority

world_state_delta.emotional_ledger_entry: defining_moral_choices +1; secrets_discovered +1

resets: The immediate road silence after speaking of Oz clears once the next obstacle demands action.
persists: Oz is remembered as a broken promiser in later authority scenes.
forgiveness_vs_resentment path memory carries into Dorothy's tone with Glinda.
escalates: Repeated humbug-piercing choices make later descriptions barer and make honest authorities answer Dorothy more directly.

paths.label: B
paths.immediate_effect: Dorothy cuts off the subject by asking how far they may go before night, and the Scarecrow answers with distance instead of opinion while the Tin Woodman checks his axe and the Lion sniffs the air.
paths.current_session_echo: After the Fighting Trees, the party does not pause to moralize over the attack; they move straight on, and the narration passes briskly from danger to travel again.
paths.next_session_payoff: In Session 6, when Glinda first asks what Dorothy desires, Dorothy answers at once, "To go home," without dwelling on Oz at all, which moves Glinda quickly to practical instruction.
paths.later_session_legacy: The final homeward resolution carries a spare, practical tone; Dorothy spends little breath on past hurts and more on what must be done next.
paths.defining_line_captured: She would not feed sorrow when the road needed walking.

consequence_visibility.what_changes: Conversation pivots immediately from grievance to travel logistics.
consequence_visibility.how_player_sees_it: Dorothy's first follow-up line asks about distance, the companions answer in practical terms, and later obstacle aftermaths are handled with brisk onward movement.

npc_shifts.direction: practical trust up; follows Dorothy's forward motion
quiet respect up; sees Dorothy bear hurt without complaint

world_state_delta.emotional_ledger_entry: key_successes_failures +1; sacrifices_made +1

resets: The specific Emerald City look-back conversation ends once the road enters the next marvel.
persists: Dorothy's habit of moving from hurt to practical action informs Session 6 audience pacing.
session5_oz_memory_subdued remains available for tonal shading.
escalates: Repeated pragmatic deferral of grief makes narration shorten complaint beats and lengthen useful action beats.

paths.label: C
paths.immediate_effect: The Tin Woodman brightens at once and says, "That is kindly said," the Scarecrow nods, and the company begins talking again instead of walking in silence; the Emerald City remains beautiful behind them, though imperfect.
paths.current_session_echo: In the Dainty China Country, Dorothy's first impulse is apology before method, and the little place is described with more pretty detail before its danger is weighed.
paths.next_session_payoff: Glinda's first private tone in Session 6 is warmer: she says, "You have been just, as well as sorrowful," after hearing Dorothy speak of Oz without bitterness, and she handles the Golden Cap discussion more maternally.
paths.later_session_legacy: The final revelation of the Shoes lands as a benevolent correction rather than a sharp irony; the narrator treats the long delay as sad but not reproachful.
paths.defining_line_captured: Mercy can survive disappointment without becoming foolish.

consequence_visibility.what_changes: Companion mood visibly lightens, and wonder scenes keep more beauty in their framing.
consequence_visibility.how_player_sees_it: The Tin Woodman speaks immediately with approval, the group resumes cheerful talk, and the next marvel scene lingers a little longer on painted prettiness before danger.

npc_shifts.direction: affection up; moved by Dorothy's kindness

world_state_delta.emotional_ledger_entry: defining_moral_choices +1; promises_kept_or_broken +1

resets: The immediate lightening of road talk clears once the next hazard begins.
persists: Dorothy's merciful memory of Oz affects Glinda's reading of her character in Session 6.
Tin Woodman's warmer affection persists into farewell scenes.
escalates: Repeated forgiveness toward failed authority unlocks warmer ruler responses and gentler explanatory scenes.

paths.label: A
paths.immediate_effect: Dorothy lifts Toto into her arms at once, fixes her eyes on the white floor, and the Lion gives a long sigh because he must place each great paw as neatly as he can; presently a tiny cart is pulled aside by one of the china folk to clear a safer strip.
paths.current_session_echo: At the far edge of the China Country, the milkmaid no longer cries after them; instead she stands by the broken-legged cow and watches in silence while two little figures keep the path clear.
paths.next_session_payoff: When Glinda first sees Dorothy in Session 6, she notices how Dorothy still carries herself carefully among lesser folk and says, "You have not trodden roughly through my country, I think," before offering welcome.
paths.later_session_legacy: Dorothy's final farewell scenes are shaded as those of a girl who delayed herself rather than hurt the harmless; Glinda's trust in her surrender of power comes more easily.
paths.defining_line_captured: She would rather lose time than goodness.

consequence_visibility.what_changes: The crossing slows visibly, Toto is carried, and the china people reduce open panic.
consequence_visibility.how_player_sees_it: Dorothy's movement changes at once, the Lion complains about delicate footing, and tiny residents begin moving carts and stools aside instead of only shrieking.

world_state_delta.inventory: Toto is carried for part of the crossing

npc_shifts.direction: affection up; deeply approves care for fragile lives

world_state_delta.emotional_ledger_entry: defining_moral_choices +1; sacrifices_made +1; npcs_helped_or_harmed +1

resets: Toto being carried ends when the China Country crossing is over.
persists: china_people_hostility_softened remains in the archive as proof Dorothy minimized harm.
Tin Woodman's increased warmth persists into Session 6 farewell tenderness.
compassion_vs_self_preservation path memory remains available for Glinda's judgment.
escalates: Repeated protection of the small and fragile makes tiny allies and gentle rulers trust Dorothy more quickly.

paths.label: B
paths.immediate_effect: Dorothy calls Toto close and the whole party moves in one brisk line; behind them rises a thin clatter of china voices, and the Tin Woodman turns his head once as if he would rather not hear it.
paths.current_session_echo: When the travelers step out of the China Country, the narration notes a nicked fence-post and an overturned tiny stool left behind them, though they themselves are safely through.
paths.next_session_payoff: At Glinda's first audience in Session 6, Dorothy's account of the southern road includes a brief troubled pause over the China Country, and Glinda answers more firmly than warmly before turning to instruction.
paths.later_session_legacy: The final farewell carries a faint moral bruise; Dorothy gets home no less truly, but the narration remembers that haste can save and harm together.
paths.defining_line_captured: Sometimes haste wears the face of mercy and the hands of damage.

consequence_visibility.what_changes: The crossing becomes visibly faster, but leaves audible reproach and small signs of damage.
consequence_visibility.how_player_sees_it: The party moves in a straight rush immediately, tiny voices clatter behind them, and the exit shot includes specific broken or overturned china details.

npc_shifts.direction: moral unease up; dislikes the rough necessity
admiration up; responds to decisive movement

world_state_delta.emotional_ledger_entry: defining_moral_choices +1; crimes_committed_or_witnessed +1; key_successes_failures +1

resets: The immediate clatter and pursuit of china voices end once the party leaves the country behind.
persists: china_people_reproach_lingers remains as a remembered moral stain in Dorothy's travel history.
Tin Woodman's unease persists into Session 6 emotional shading.
escalates: Repeated speed-over-mercy choices make later narratorial judgment plainer and kind-hearted companions more visibly troubled.

paths.label: C
paths.immediate_effect: Dorothy stops, speaks to the milkmaid, and after several tiny complaints a little shepherd boy points with his crook toward a broader white space; then the company steps only where the china folk direct.
paths.current_session_echo: Midway through the crossing, little faces appear at windows and behind painted fences, and one tiny princess waves them away from a narrow lane toward a safer open square.
paths.next_session_payoff: In Session 6, Glinda responds well to Dorothy's account of asking leave in another people's country and says, "That was sensible, my child," before discussing the Golden Cap and the proper release of power.
paths.later_session_legacy: Dorothy's last acts with Glinda and the Monkeys are framed through consent and rightful transfer, echoing the negotiated crossing of the China Country.
paths.defining_line_captured: Permission can be slower than power and kinder than both.

consequence_visibility.what_changes: The china people become reluctant guides and visibly point out a route.
consequence_visibility.how_player_sees_it: Named tiny figures answer Dorothy, point with hands and crooks, and later continue to redirect the party through windows, fences, and lanes.

world_state_delta.emotional_ledger_entry: defining_moral_choices +1; npcs_helped_or_harmed +1; promises_made +1

resets: The specific pointed route through the China Country ends once the white floor is behind them.
persists: china_people_grudging_cooperation remains as proof Dorothy sought consent.
Scarecrow's admiration for negotiated practical method persists into Session 6 banter.
escalates: Repeated negotiated solutions make later helpers volunteer guidance rather than needing to be pressed.

paths.label: A
paths.immediate_effect: N/A — session ends on this choice
paths.current_session_echo: Forest coronation close: the beasts press nearer in hope, and the Lion stands taller without yet leaving Dorothy's side.
paths.next_session_payoff: At dawn in Session 6, the Lion speaks in a steadier kingly voice and says, "If you think I ought, Dorothy, I will stay and guard them," while the beasts bow low around him.
paths.next_session_opening: Quiet, sorrowful, and noble; first image is dew on the forest clearing and many beasts lying in a ring about the Lion; first character is the Lion, already half a king; immediate stakes are whether Dorothy will bless his staying and lose him before Glinda is found.
paths.later_session_legacy: At the final farewell, the Lion's departure from Dorothy is cleaner and more dignified, and his kingship feels earned rather than accidental.
paths.defining_line_captured: Love sometimes proves itself by letting go first.

consequence_visibility.what_changes: The forest beasts' hope rises visibly and the Lion's posture shifts toward kingship.
consequence_visibility.how_player_sees_it: The session closes on the beasts murmuring and lifting bright eyes, and the next session opens with bows, ringed attendance, and the Lion speaking more declaratively.

npc_shifts.direction: kingly confidence up; feels Dorothy honors his future
sorrow up; senses the company beginning to break
tender respect up; moved by Dorothy's selflessness

world_state_delta.emotional_ledger_entry: defining_moral_choices +1; sacrifices_made +1; promises_kept_or_broken +1

resets: The beasts' immediate coronation murmur clears once the formal morning decision is spoken.
persists: ambition_vs_service path memory governs the Lion's farewell stance in Session 6.
Lion's kingly confidence persists through the end of the story.
forest_beasts_political_state trending LION_ACCEPTED_AS_KING remains active into Session 6 opening.
escalates: Repeated release-of-companions choices make later farewells cleaner, sadder, and more ceremonially accepted by the world.

paths.label: B
paths.immediate_effect: N/A — session ends on this choice
paths.current_session_echo: Forest hush close: the beasts wait very still, and the Lion looks from Dorothy to them and back again without speaking the answer.
paths.next_session_payoff: Session 6 opens on a suspended breakfast scene where no one speaks lightly; the Lion asks, "What do you think now?" because Dorothy left the choice to his own heart, and the beasts do not press him at once.
paths.next_session_opening: Tender and uncertain; first image is a gray morning fire and untouched forest fruit set before the travelers; first character is the Lion, wakeful and unspeaking; immediate stakes are that his decision still hangs, and Dorothy must answer his uncertainty without deciding for him.
paths.later_session_legacy: The farewell to the Lion remains the most bittersweet version, because both freedom and loss are felt at the same time and neither was forced.
paths.defining_line_captured: Some partings begin before anyone has gone.

consequence_visibility.what_changes: The decision remains visibly suspended, and the forest itself seems to wait.
consequence_visibility.how_player_sees_it: The closing image is stillness rather than acclaim, and the next session opens with quiet, delayed speech, and the Lion asking for no command.

npc_shifts.direction: trust up; feels his freedom is respected

world_state_delta.emotional_ledger_entry: defining_moral_choices +1; promises_made +1

resets: The exact night of waiting clears once the Lion speaks in Session 6.
persists: ambition_vs_service path memory governs the Lion's opening indecision and farewell tone.
Cowardly Lion's trust that Dorothy will not command his future persists.
escalates: Repeated autonomy-granting choices make companions ask Dorothy for counsel rather than obedience.

paths.label: C
paths.immediate_effect: N/A — session ends on this choice
paths.current_session_echo: Forest disappointment close: the glad noise among the beasts sinks lower, and the Lion breathes deeply as if two roads lay beneath one pair of feet.
paths.next_session_payoff: Session 6 opens with waiting beasts standing back from the road while the Lion remains beside Dorothy; he is more tender and more troubled, and one old tiger says, "Will our king go away again?"
paths.next_session_opening: Troubled and affectionate; first image is the southern road with beasts gathered on either side but not blocking it; first character is an old tiger speaking for the forest; immediate stakes are whether Dorothy may still borrow the Lion's strength without wronging those who need him.
paths.later_session_legacy: The Lion's farewell in Session 6 carries the strongest note of torn attachment, and the beasts' acceptance of his kingship comes later and with more sadness.
paths.defining_line_captured: Need can cling even when it knows better.

consequence_visibility.what_changes: The beasts' joy visibly falls, and the Lion's conflict becomes the dominant emotional image.
consequence_visibility.how_player_sees_it: The closing forest sound drops at once, and the next session opens with disappointed beasts flanking the road and speaking their uncertainty aloud.

npc_shifts.direction: attachment up; feels wanted by Dorothy
disappointment up; defer but do not rebel
concern up; senses Dorothy's fear of parting

world_state_delta.emotional_ledger_entry: defining_moral_choices +1; sacrifices_made +0; promises_made +1

resets: The beasts' gathered waiting line clears once the Lion's morning course is settled.
persists: ambition_vs_service path memory governs the Lion's conflicted farewell and the beasts' tone in Session 6.
Cowardly Lion's attachment to Dorothy remains heightened through his goodbye.
escalates: Repeated cling-to-companion choices make separations more painful and increase social disappointment from those left waiting.

reactivity_trigger_specs.condition: If S5_C1 path A was chosen for home_vs_companion_devotion.
reactivity_trigger_specs.default_behavior: In Session 6 farewells, companions thank Dorothy for leading them and speak as faithful followers.
reactivity_trigger_specs.triggered_behavior: The Lion says, "You never ordered me, Dorothy," and the Scarecrow adds, "I stayed because I wished to," making the farewell explicitly about chosen loyalty.
reactivity_trigger_specs.narrative_execution: Let the truth come in plain little speeches, one after another, so that their freedom is felt by the very ease with which they give it.

npc_reactions: Cowardly Lion speaks with grateful freedom instead of solemn duty.
Scarecrow frames his devotion as self-chosen.
Tin Woodman answers with warmer gratitude.
environmental_details: Farewell space feels less ceremonial and more companionable.
The road seems sad, but not burdened by obligation.
dialogue_variations: Replace duty-forward lines with voluntary phrases such as "I chose" and "of my own will."

reactivity_trigger_specs.condition: If S5_C1 path A was chosen and the Session 6 home_vs_companion_devotion choice later asks Dorothy to part from companions.
reactivity_trigger_specs.default_behavior: The Lion's farewell responds chiefly to Dorothy's homesickness.
reactivity_trigger_specs.triggered_behavior: The Lion's farewell balances Dorothy's homesickness with his own chosen loyalty, making his acceptance of parting gentler and less wounded.
reactivity_trigger_specs.narrative_execution: Name both Kansas and friendship plainly, and then let the Lion place his own will beside hers without complaint.

npc_reactions: Lion is sad but not clingful.
Scarecrow supports the parting with less hesitation.
environmental_details: The farewell clearing feels calm rather than strained.
dialogue_variations: Lion line variation: "A friend may stay a friend, even when he turns another way."

reactivity_trigger_specs.condition: If S5_C1 path B was chosen for home_vs_companion_devotion.
reactivity_trigger_specs.default_behavior: Glinda's attendants admit Dorothy after ordinary formal exchange.
reactivity_trigger_specs.triggered_behavior: Dorothy's orderly company is received more smoothly; the maidens open the way at once after one clear announcement, and Glinda's first tone is approving of her steadiness.
reactivity_trigger_specs.narrative_execution: Show one thing following another in due order, so the welcome itself seems to open because the travelers have come properly.

npc_reactions: Glinda's attendants are prompt and respectful.
Glinda speaks with immediate calm approval.
environmental_details: The gate opens without delay.
The approach to Glinda's hall feels composed and ceremonial.
dialogue_variations: Glinda line variation: "You have come properly, my child."

reactivity_trigger_specs.condition: If S5_C1 path C was chosen for home_vs_companion_devotion.
reactivity_trigger_specs.default_behavior: The Lion travels beside Dorothy with ordinary loyalty.
reactivity_trigger_specs.triggered_behavior: The Lion repeatedly places himself ahead of Dorothy in danger and opens Session 6 by asking first whether she is weary or delayed, showing sharpened protectiveness around her homesick haste.
reactivity_trigger_specs.narrative_execution: Make his great body answer before his words do, for he had once been stirred by her bold longing and had not forgotten it.

npc_reactions: Lion becomes physically more protective.
Scarecrow watches Dorothy's haste with mild concern.
environmental_details: Hazard scenes place the Lion at the front of the group more often.
dialogue_variations: Lion line variation: "Keep close to me," and "Are you tired, Dorothy?"

reactivity_trigger_specs.condition: If S5_C2 path A was chosen for forgiveness_vs_resentment.
reactivity_trigger_specs.default_behavior: Glinda gives the Silver Shoes explanation with her usual calm benevolence.
reactivity_trigger_specs.triggered_behavior: Glinda answers Dorothy's bluntness with equal plainness, shortening ceremony and moving quickly to the useful truth of the Shoes.
reactivity_trigger_specs.narrative_execution: Strip away all needless splendor in the telling, and let the good Witch answer a plain hurt with a plain remedy.

npc_reactions: Glinda is calm but brisker.
Dorothy speaks with less softness and more direct need.
environmental_details: The hall's beauty is noticed less than the Shoes and Glinda's instruction.
dialogue_variations: Glinda line variation: "That is true enough; and now I will tell you what the shoes can do."

reactivity_trigger_specs.condition: If S5_C2 path A was chosen and Scarecrow is present in Session 6 farewell scenes.
reactivity_trigger_specs.default_behavior: Scarecrow speaks with affectionate sadness and modesty.
reactivity_trigger_specs.triggered_behavior: Scarecrow speaks more candidly about Oz, saying his brains were useful whether Oz was great or not, and he encourages Dorothy to trust plain truth over old spectacle.
reactivity_trigger_specs.narrative_execution: Give the Scarecrow one sensible speech in which the painted green grandeur falls away and only the little useful fact remains.

npc_reactions: Scarecrow is more direct and less ceremonious.
environmental_details: References to Emerald City emphasize artifice rather than wonder.
dialogue_variations: Scarecrow line variation: "A humbug may still hand one a thing, but he cannot make it true by shouting over it."

reactivity_trigger_specs.condition: If S5_C2 path B was chosen for forgiveness_vs_resentment.
reactivity_trigger_specs.default_behavior: Dorothy answers Glinda with some emotional account of Oz's failure before asking for home.
reactivity_trigger_specs.triggered_behavior: Dorothy speaks first and chiefly of getting home, so Glinda moves at once to practical instruction and shortens reflective talk about Oz.
reactivity_trigger_specs.narrative_execution: Let Dorothy's wish go straight to Kansas, and then let Glinda, who was wise, answer the wish before the sorrow can spread itself out again.

npc_reactions: Glinda becomes more solution-forward.
Companions interrupt less with memories of Oz.
environmental_details: Audience scene advances briskly from greeting to remedy.
dialogue_variations: Dorothy line variation: "I wish to go home," spoken before any longer account.

reactivity_trigger_specs.condition: If S5_C2 path C was chosen for forgiveness_vs_resentment.
reactivity_trigger_specs.default_behavior: Glinda receives Dorothy kindly but formally.
reactivity_trigger_specs.triggered_behavior: Glinda receives Dorothy with warmer maternal approval after hearing she judged Oz mercifully despite disappointment, and she softens the Golden Cap discussion.
reactivity_trigger_specs.narrative_execution: Let Glinda's kindness widen by a little, as good faces often do when they see mercy where bitterness might well have grown.

npc_reactions: Glinda is warmer and more maternal.
Tin Woodman is visibly pleased by Dorothy's fairness being recognized.
environmental_details: The hall feels more welcoming than judicial.
dialogue_variations: Glinda line variation: "You have been just, as well as sorrowful."

reactivity_trigger_specs.condition: If S5_C3 path A was chosen for compassion_vs_self_preservation.
reactivity_trigger_specs.default_behavior: Glinda welcomes Dorothy as a brave traveler from the South Road.
reactivity_trigger_specs.triggered_behavior: Glinda specifically notes Dorothy's careful passage through fragile places and treats her as a girl who can be trusted with dangerous power only long enough to surrender it rightly.
reactivity_trigger_specs.narrative_execution: Have the good Witch gather the meaning from small signs, for wise people in fairy countries often know a gentle foot from a trampling one.

npc_reactions: Glinda's trust rises at once.
Tin Woodman glows with approval when Dorothy's care is noticed.
environmental_details: Glinda's court feels safe and unthreatened by Dorothy's presence.
dialogue_variations: Glinda line variation: "You have not trodden roughly through my country, I think."

reactivity_trigger_specs.condition: If S5_C3 path A was chosen and Tin Woodman is present in Session 6 farewell scenes.
reactivity_trigger_specs.default_behavior: Tin Woodman is sorrowful and affectionate at parting.
reactivity_trigger_specs.triggered_behavior: Tin Woodman becomes especially tender, recalling Dorothy's care for helpless little lives and speaking as if her goodness proved his heart had not been misplaced in loving her.
reactivity_trigger_specs.narrative_execution: Let his courtesy carry the feeling plainly, and join his heart to the smallest remembered act, for that is the way he measured goodness.

npc_reactions: Tin Woodman is more openly affectionate.
environmental_details: Farewell pauses longer on gentle touch and careful gestures.
dialogue_variations: Tin Woodman line variation: "You were kind even where no one would have blamed haste."

reactivity_trigger_specs.condition: If S5_C3 path B was chosen for compassion_vs_self_preservation.
reactivity_trigger_specs.default_behavior: Glinda's instruction is benevolent and untroubled.
reactivity_trigger_specs.triggered_behavior: Glinda remains kind but grows firmer in tone when Dorothy recounts the hurried crossing, making the audience feel like gentle correction rather than simple welcome.
reactivity_trigger_specs.narrative_execution: Name the sorrow without dwelling on it, and then let Glinda's goodness take the shape of clear instruction instead of soft praise.

npc_reactions: Glinda is more instructive than maternal.
Tin Woodman lowers his eyes or grows quiet.
environmental_details: The hall's beauty remains, but the mood is soberer.
dialogue_variations: Glinda line variation: "Haste sometimes serves, my child; but it should not be loved for its own sake."

reactivity_trigger_specs.condition: If S5_C3 path C was chosen for compassion_vs_self_preservation.
reactivity_trigger_specs.default_behavior: Glinda praises Dorothy's courage in reaching the South.
reactivity_trigger_specs.triggered_behavior: Glinda praises Dorothy's sense of asking leave in another people's country and links that judgment to the rightful transfer of the Golden Cap.
reactivity_trigger_specs.narrative_execution: Let one plain lesson answer another, so that the girl who asked permission in a little country may now understand why power also must be passed on rightly.

npc_reactions: Glinda is approving and instructive.
Scarecrow looks pleased by the practical wisdom being recognized.
environmental_details: Golden Cap discussion feels like a continuation of proper conduct, not a separate lecture.
dialogue_variations: Glinda line variation: "That was sensible, my child, and also just."

reactivity_trigger_specs.condition: If S5_C3 path C was chosen and Scarecrow is present in Session 6.
reactivity_trigger_specs.default_behavior: Scarecrow praises Dorothy's kindness in general terms.
reactivity_trigger_specs.triggered_behavior: Scarecrow specifically recalls that Dorothy solved the China Country by asking those who lived there, and he uses it as proof she knows how to think as well as feel.
reactivity_trigger_specs.narrative_execution: Give him a neat little piece of reasoning, for he liked best the plans that were humble enough to work.

npc_reactions: Scarecrow becomes more admiring and initiative-taking.
environmental_details: Farewell talk includes a remembered image of tiny hands pointing the way.
dialogue_variations: Scarecrow line variation: "Why, you see, the best road was the one the little people themselves showed us."

reactivity_trigger_specs.condition: If S5_C4 path A was chosen for ambition_vs_service.
reactivity_trigger_specs.default_behavior: Session 6 opens with the Lion still uncertain and the beasts watchful.
reactivity_trigger_specs.triggered_behavior: Session 6 opens with the beasts already gathered in respectful ring and the Lion speaking in a steadier, more kingly manner because Dorothy urged him toward service.
reactivity_trigger_specs.narrative_execution: Show the morning plainly, and let the change be seen first in the beasts' bowed heads and then in the Lion's firmer speech.

npc_reactions: Lion speaks more declaratively.
Forest beasts are hopeful and ceremonious.
Tin Woodman respects Dorothy's selflessness more openly.
environmental_details: Beasts form a ring around the clearing.
The road looks lonelier because the forest already claims the Lion.
dialogue_variations: Lion line variation: "If you think I ought, Dorothy, I will stay and guard them."
affected_elements.available_options: Available options in Session 6 lean toward blessing the Lion's stay rather than persuading him to delay again.

reactivity_trigger_specs.condition: If S5_C4 path A was chosen and Dorothy's cumulative home_vs_companion_devotion tendency is home-forward by Session 6.
reactivity_trigger_specs.default_behavior: The Lion's farewell emphasizes his kingship and Dorothy's selflessness equally.
reactivity_trigger_specs.triggered_behavior: The farewell adds a sharper note that Dorothy released him because Kansas still called her onward, making both home and sacrifice stand in the same line.
reactivity_trigger_specs.narrative_execution: Set Kansas and the forest side by side in simple words, and let the sadness come from the fact that both were true and neither could wait for the other.

npc_reactions: Lion admires Dorothy's resolve more deeply.
environmental_details: The southern road is noticed as still waiting beyond the clearing.
dialogue_variations: Lion line variation: "You let me stay, though your own road was not ended."

reactivity_trigger_specs.condition: If S5_C4 path B was chosen for ambition_vs_service.
reactivity_trigger_specs.default_behavior: Session 6 opens with the Lion already leaning clearly toward staying or going.
reactivity_trigger_specs.triggered_behavior: Session 6 opens in hush and suspension; the Lion asks Dorothy for no command, the beasts hold back, and the first stakes are emotional rather than practical.
reactivity_trigger_specs.narrative_execution: Keep the morning quiet, for the forest itself had not yet heard the answer and so all living things behaved as if they must wait for it.

npc_reactions: Lion is tender and uncertain.
Scarecrow and Tin Woodman speak softly and little.
Forest beasts do not press their claim at once.
environmental_details: Untouched fruit or breakfast remains between them.
Leaves and beasts are still.
dialogue_variations: Lion line variation: "What do you think now?"
affected_elements.available_options: Available options preserve uncertainty and do not force immediate coronation language.

reactivity_trigger_specs.condition: If S5_C4 path C was chosen for ambition_vs_service.
reactivity_trigger_specs.default_behavior: Session 6 opens with the beasts welcoming the Lion's kingship.
reactivity_trigger_specs.triggered_behavior: Session 6 opens with beasts lining the road in disappointment while the Lion remains beside Dorothy, and an old tiger voices the forest's fear of losing him.
reactivity_trigger_specs.narrative_execution: Let the trouble be visible in the arrangement of bodies, for the beasts did not rebel, but they could not help standing where the road might still take their king away.

npc_reactions: Forest beasts are disappointed but submissive.
Lion is more torn and affectionate toward Dorothy.
Tin Woodman watches Dorothy with concern.
environmental_details: Beasts stand on either side of the road.
The clearing feels divided between forest and journey.
dialogue_variations: Old tiger line variation: "Will our king go away again?"
affected_elements.available_options: Available options in Session 6 include stronger emotional pressure around whether Dorothy continues to borrow the Lion's company.

reactivity_trigger_specs.condition: If S5_C4 path C was chosen and S5_C1 path C previously emphasized homeward urgency.
reactivity_trigger_specs.default_behavior: Dorothy's request that the Lion stay longer reads chiefly as fear of parting.
reactivity_trigger_specs.triggered_behavior: The contradiction becomes visible: Dorothy, who once urged the southern road at any cost, now asks delay for the sake of companionship, and the Lion notices both her need and her conflict.
reactivity_trigger_specs.narrative_execution: Do not explain the contradiction too deeply; simply place her old haste beside her present clinging, and the truth of her heart will show itself.

npc_reactions: Lion becomes more tender and more troubled.
Scarecrow notices the inconsistency quietly.
environmental_details: The road is repeatedly noticed as waiting while the beasts wait also.
dialogue_variations: Lion line variation: "You were once so eager for the South Road, Dorothy."

resets_between_episodes.element: Throne Room volunteer reply order and palace departure bustle
resets_between_episodes.why: It belongs to the Emerald City setup scene and does not survive once the party is well upon the South Road.

resets_between_episodes.element: Immediate look-back silence or cheer while Emerald City is still visible
resets_between_episodes.why: That emotional weather is tied to the last sight of the green walls and fades once the city drops from view.

resets_between_episodes.element: Toto being carried during the careful China Country crossing
resets_between_episodes.why: It is a local movement solution for one fragile place, not a permanent inventory state.

resets_between_episodes.element: The beasts' night-long waiting posture around the Lion after the spider's death
resets_between_episodes.why: It is the closing tableau of Session 5 and resolves into a spoken morning decision in Session 6.

persists_across_full_story.why: It defines how Dorothy frames companionship and therefore directly shapes the moral color of her final farewells.
It governs Dorothy's tone toward failed authority and changes how Glinda reads her honesty, mercy, and readiness for plain truth.
It records whether Dorothy would slow herself, negotiate, or risk harm among the helpless, which is central to Glinda's judgment of her character.
It determines the Lion's emotional stance, the beasts' political expectation, and the shape of one of the story's last separations.

persists_across_full_story.element: NPC disposition shifts for Scarecrow, Tin Woodman, and Cowardly Lion
persists_across_full_story.why: These relationships are the heart of the book's ending and must be paid in farewell dialogue, posture, and willingness to part.

persists_across_full_story.element: Environmental flags such as session5_homeward_urgency_emphasized, session5_oz_memory_forgiven, china_people_reproach_lingers, and session6_open_on_lion_indecision
persists_across_full_story.why: They are compact story memory markers that drive opening tone, audience tone, and farewell asymmetry in Session 6.

escalates_with_accumulation.behavioral_pattern: Repeated respect for companion autonomy

thresholds.at_2_instances: Companions volunteer help with less hesitation and use phrases such as "if you will have me" or "I choose to go."
thresholds.at_4_instances: Farewell scenes become less duty-bound; companions speak their own wishes plainly and without resentment.
thresholds.at_6_or_more_instances: Dorothy is treated across the story as a leader who never clutches at others, and partings become nobler, sadder, and more freely given.

escalates_with_accumulation.behavioral_pattern: Repeated orderly, lawful travel and audience conduct

thresholds.at_2_instances: Gatekeepers and attendants become quicker to admit Dorothy and explain rules.
thresholds.at_4_instances: Rulers such as Glinda answer her with immediate procedural trust and clearer instruction.
thresholds.at_6_or_more_instances: The world itself seems to open in due order around Dorothy, with fewer frictions in formal spaces and more ceremonial respect.

escalates_with_accumulation.behavioral_pattern: Repeated humbug-piercing bluntness and preference for plain truth

thresholds.at_2_instances: Descriptions of spectacle grow barer, and candid companions like the Scarecrow speak more directly.
thresholds.at_4_instances: False or theatrical authorities become embarrassed sooner, while honest rulers stop wasting words on ceremony.
thresholds.at_6_or_more_instances: Narration consistently strips marvel down to its little useful fact, and Dorothy's final revelations land with strong plainness rather than pageantry.

escalates_with_accumulation.behavioral_pattern: Repeated forgiveness toward failed or limited authority

thresholds.at_2_instances: Kind-hearted companions answer Dorothy with warmer affection after disappointments.
thresholds.at_4_instances: Good rulers, especially Glinda, respond more maternally and with greater trust in Dorothy's moral balance.
thresholds.at_6_or_more_instances: The story's last judgments fall gently; even failures are remembered with fairness, and the ending carries more benevolent calm than reproach.

escalates_with_accumulation.behavioral_pattern: Repeated protection of the small, fragile, or vulnerable at cost to speed

thresholds.at_2_instances: Tiny allies and gentle companions become more openly grateful and trusting.
thresholds.at_4_instances: Tin Woodman and similar tender-hearted figures grow markedly more affectionate and approving.
thresholds.at_6_or_more_instances: Dorothy's reputation as tender-hearted becomes unmistakable, and later scenes of power transfer or farewell are framed as safe in her hands.

escalates_with_accumulation.behavioral_pattern: Repeated haste that accepts collateral harm for momentum

thresholds.at_2_instances: Kind companions show mild unease after Dorothy's quickest solutions.
thresholds.at_4_instances: Narration begins to mark small damages and moral aftertastes more plainly.
thresholds.at_6_or_more_instances: Merciful figures answer Dorothy more firmly, and the world remembers her speed as useful but not wholly gentle.

escalates_with_accumulation.behavioral_pattern: Repeated release of companions toward their own futures

thresholds.at_2_instances: Companions part with gratitude even when sorrowful.
thresholds.at_4_instances: Farewell scenes gain ceremonial dignity, and those who leave do so with clearer self-possession.
thresholds.at_6_or_more_instances: Dorothy becomes a figure who helps others into their rightful places, and the ending's separations feel fully earned rather than merely sad.

escalates_with_accumulation.behavioral_pattern: Repeated clinging to companionship when parting would serve others

thresholds.at_2_instances: Companions show tenderness mixed with concern when Dorothy asks them to remain.
thresholds.at_4_instances: Outside groups, such as beasts or citizens, begin to show visible disappointment when Dorothy delays another's future.
thresholds.at_6_or_more_instances: Partings become more conflicted and socially costly, with stronger pressure from the world for Dorothy to release what she loves.

freeform_guidelines.narrator_behavior: Keep surfacing this path by having companions speak as if they came of their own will, and by letting Dorothy notice the weight of freely given help.
freeform_guidelines.spirit: She honors loyalty best by not claiming ownership over it.
freeform_guidelines.freeform_alignment_input: observational, fair-minded, permission-giving, respectful of others' choice
hard_limits: Do not let Dorothy refuse the southern journey.
Do not let any companion actually stay behind in Session 5.
Do not invent a new route home besides Glinda.
Do not turn Dorothy cold or indifferent to Kansas.
freeform_guidelines.fold_back_acknowledge: Dorothy may answer that she will not command a friend who has already done so much for her.
freeform_guidelines.fold_back_redirect: But the Scarecrow, Tin Woodman, and Lion each insist, in his own way, that they will not let her go south alone now that the road is known.

freeform_guidelines.narrator_behavior: Surface this path by making future group movement feel arranged, steady, and proper, with one thing following another in good order.
freeform_guidelines.spirit: She masters sorrow by turning it into shared duty.
hard_limits: Do not let Dorothy become military, harsh, or domineering.
Do not break the company apart before the road begins.
Do not allow palace bureaucracy to replace the southern departure.
Do not modernize the speech into command jargon.
freeform_guidelines.fold_back_acknowledge: Dorothy may frame the matter as something that ought to be done properly and together.
freeform_guidelines.fold_back_redirect: The companions are glad of a plain plan, and so the scene settles into preparation for a shared departure rather than debate.

freeform_guidelines.narrator_behavior: Surface this path by letting Dorothy's homesickness quicken scenes and by making the Lion answer her urgency with visible protectiveness.
freeform_guidelines.spirit: Her longing for home becomes a brave force that pulls others after her.
freeform_guidelines.freeform_alignment_input: aggressive, urgent, defiant, impatient with delay, home-first
hard_limits: Do not let Dorothy actually run off alone.
Do not let the party abandon preparation so fully that the road logic breaks.
Do not introduce reckless violence in the Throne Room.
Do not weaken Dorothy's courtesy into cruelty.
freeform_guidelines.fold_back_acknowledge: Dorothy may speak as if she would brave any danger rather than bear another delay.
freeform_guidelines.fold_back_redirect: Yet her friends, who love her, close ranks at once and refuse to let such haste send her southward alone.

freeform_guidelines.narrator_behavior: Surface this path by stripping grandeur from remembered things and by letting broken promises be named plainly when authority is discussed.
freeform_guidelines.spirit: She will not let kindness excuse failure where faith was owed.
hard_limits: Do not let Dorothy denounce Oz publicly to the city from afar.
Do not retcon Oz into malice beyond canon.
Do not stop the journey to brood indefinitely.
Do not use modern sarcasm or sneering wit.
freeform_guidelines.fold_back_acknowledge: Dorothy may say that a promise is no less broken because it was spoken kindly.
freeform_guidelines.fold_back_redirect: But the road still lies before them, and the companions, though sobered, can only carry that truth onward into the next miles.

freeform_guidelines.narrator_behavior: Surface this path by moving quickly from hurt into practical questions, distances, weather, and the next obstacle on the road.
freeform_guidelines.spirit: She refuses to spend strength on sorrow that cannot mend itself.
hard_limits: Do not make Dorothy emotionally numb or uncaring.
Do not erase the pain of Oz's failure altogether.
Do not invent a new practical solution in this scene.
Do not let silence become hostility among the companions.
freeform_guidelines.fold_back_acknowledge: Dorothy may decline to dwell on Oz and turn at once to what must be done before night.
freeform_guidelines.fold_back_redirect: Since speaking of him cannot mend the balloon or shorten the road, the company naturally falls to travel talk and keeps moving south.

freeform_guidelines.narrator_behavior: Surface this path by preserving some beauty in disappointed things and by letting kind judgments lighten companion speech after failure.
freeform_guidelines.spirit: She can forgive without pretending the hurt was small.
freeform_guidelines.freeform_alignment_input: merciful, fair, respectful, charitable, morally balanced
hard_limits: Do not rewrite Oz as competent or truly magical.
Do not let forgiveness erase the broken promise.
Do not turn the scene into sermon or abstraction.
Do not make Dorothy grandly saintly or self-congratulatory.
freeform_guidelines.fold_back_acknowledge: Dorothy may separate Oz's good heart from his poor wizardry and forgive him for failing her.
freeform_guidelines.fold_back_redirect: The companions, especially the Tin Woodman, gladly receive that fairness, and so the road grows lighter though the failure remains true.

freeform_guidelines.narrator_behavior: Surface this path by slowing the prose around footsteps, little obstacles, and careful hands, and by rewarding mercy with reduced panic from the china folk.
freeform_guidelines.spirit: No wish, however dear, should trample the harmless.
hard_limits: Do not let Dorothy remain in the China Country indefinitely.
Do not magically repair the broken china by new powers.
Do not shrink the party to china size.
Do not remove the need to continue south.
freeform_guidelines.fold_back_acknowledge: Dorothy may insist that every step must be guarded, even if it costs time.
freeform_guidelines.fold_back_redirect: Because the country is truly so fragile, the only workable answer is to cross it slowly, with Toto lifted and each foot placed with care.

freeform_guidelines.narrator_behavior: Surface this path by letting speed clear danger but leaving behind little audible reproaches and one or two plain signs of damage.
freeform_guidelines.spirit: She chooses momentum under pressure, even when innocence may suffer for it.
hard_limits: Do not allow gleeful cruelty toward the china people.
Do not turn the crossing into graphic destruction.
Do not let the party stop and fight the china folk.
Do not bypass the country entirely.
freeform_guidelines.fold_back_acknowledge: Dorothy may judge that fear itself now makes the place more dangerous and that a swift straight passage will do the least total harm.
freeform_guidelines.fold_back_redirect: Since the little people keep crowding near in fright, the company can only lessen the danger by moving briskly through and out of the crowded part.

freeform_guidelines.narrator_behavior: Surface this path by making local people point the way, complain, and still cooperate, so consent itself becomes the visible mechanism of progress.
freeform_guidelines.spirit: Right passage through another's country begins with asking leave.
hard_limits: Do not let the china people become major companions.
Do not let Dorothy surrender the whole quest to them.
Do not invent a magical escort beyond their tiny guidance.
Do not make them suddenly trusting without complaint.
freeform_guidelines.fold_back_acknowledge: Dorothy may decide the wisest thing is to ask those little people where larger feet may safely go.
freeform_guidelines.fold_back_redirect: Because it is their country and they know its broad spaces best, the scene folds naturally into a grudging guided crossing rather than a free scramble.

freeform_guidelines.narrator_behavior: Surface this path by letting the forest answer Dorothy's self-denial with hopeful stillness, bowed heads, and a Lion who begins to look kingly.
freeform_guidelines.spirit: She proves love by releasing a friend toward the duty that belongs to him.
hard_limits: Do not settle the Lion's full decision before Session 6 opens.
Do not have Dorothy abandon her own journey to remain in the forest.
Do not let the beasts crown him completely on the spot.
Do not invent a way for him to be both full-time king and immediate companion at once.
freeform_guidelines.fold_back_acknowledge: Dorothy may say that a true king ought to stay where frightened creatures need him most.
freeform_guidelines.fold_back_redirect: Yet because this is the session-end hook, the forest can only lean toward that answer now, saving the spoken morning decision for the next opening.

freeform_guidelines.narrator_behavior: Surface this path by preserving hush, suspended feeling, and the sense that the forest itself waits upon the Lion's own heart.
freeform_guidelines.spirit: She refuses to possess another's future, even when parting hurts.
hard_limits: Do not let Dorothy become indifferent to losing the Lion.
Do not force the Lion to answer immediately.
Do not let the beasts disperse as if nothing is at stake.
Do not resolve the hook before the episode boundary.
freeform_guidelines.fold_back_acknowledge: Dorothy may say the matter belongs to the Lion and that she will love him whichever way he turns.
freeform_guidelines.fold_back_redirect: Since his own country and courage are in question, the scene naturally settles into waiting rather than immediate resolution.

freeform_guidelines.narrator_behavior: Surface this path by making the beasts' disappointment visible and by keeping the Lion close to Dorothy in a troubled, affectionate uncertainty.
freeform_guidelines.spirit: Her need for companionship clings even when she knows another claim may be juster.
freeform_guidelines.freeform_alignment_input: pleading, attached, fearful of parting, urgent for one more stretch together
hard_limits: Do not let Dorothy deny the beasts' need altogether.
Do not let the Lion reject kingship forever in Session 5.
Do not create rebellion or violence among the beasts.
Do not resolve the conflict before Session 6.
freeform_guidelines.fold_back_acknowledge: Dorothy may ask for the Lion a little longer, until Glinda is found and her own fate is settled.
freeform_guidelines.fold_back_redirect: But because the forest has a true claim on him, the request can only delay and trouble the matter, not cancel it, and so the session closes on divided need.

session_close_design.resolution_prose: At last the matter is settled, and your heart grows lighter, though Oz has failed you. You stand in the great Throne Room, with its green light and its polished floor, and hear your friends speak one after another. The Scarecrow says he will leave his high throne. The Tin Woodman says his axe shall serve you. The Lion says he will guard you on the road. Then you know plainly that you are not to be left alone in the Emerald City, nor asked to be content where you do not belong. You are going South to seek Glinda, who is good and powerful, and that is now your true road home. This is a real comfort to you, for hope has come back again, simple and strong. Yet the road to the South is said to be full of dangers, and Glinda is still far away. So you are glad, and grateful, and eager also; for tomorrow morning you set out together.
session_close_design.hook_transition: For one little moment, it is enough merely to have faithful friends beside you. Then the journey itself rises before you, long and unknown, and all look toward you to see how this company shall begin.

session_end_choice.choice_question: When morning comes, how do you choose to lead your company away to the South?

option_a.text: Set out at once by the straight road, for the quickest way is best when home is waiting.
option_a.next_session_opens: The next session opens as you leave the Emerald City early and press southward in good spirits, before the first danger appears.

option_b.text: Take time to prepare carefully, asking each friend what help he can give before you begin the long journey.
option_b.next_session_opens: The next session opens with your company making ready in an orderly way, and then departing with a clearer plan for the dangers ahead.

option_c.text: Pause first to say your farewells properly, for a kind leave-taking may give courage for the road.
option_c.next_session_opens: The next session opens with your last good-byes in the Emerald City, and then your company passes out through the gate toward the South.

session_end_choice.final_line: The morning waits, and the South lies before you.

q1_entry_point.detail: The cold open begins at the first actionable tension point: Dorothy asks the Soldier with the Green Whiskers for a real way home after Oz's failure. The prior grief, recap, and failed Monkey attempt are cut and only compressed into necessary context.

q3_first_choice_timing.detail: Branching Choice #1 is reached within approximately 170-190 words of the cold open, well under the 300-word limit.

q4_consequential_choices.detail: All four branching choices alter tracked state and have named downstream payoffs in Session 6 via Phase 6 consequence maps and the cross-session payoff plan: S5_C1 home_vs_companion_devotion affects farewell logic; S5_C2 forgiveness_vs_resentment affects Dorothy's tone with Glinda; S5_C3 compassion_vs_self_preservation affects Glinda's judgment and companion warmth; S5_C4 ambition_vs_service governs Session 6 opening and Lion farewell stance.

q5_stakes_within_60_seconds.detail: Before the first external branch, the cold open establishes Dorothy's internal stake clearly: she must get home to Aunt Em and cannot bear more wasted hopes. The external path, Glinda in the South, is then introduced.

q6_early_decision_visible_later.detail: S5_C1 has explicit visible later impact: its Session 6 payoff changes companion farewell language and the Lion's protectiveness or autonomy framing. This is named specifically in Phase 6 under RT_S5_C1_* triggers.

q7_breath_beat.detail: A clear breath beat exists between minutes 10-13: the white china wall reveal and the Dainty China Country wonder inventory, with comic release from the Fighting Trees immediately before it.

q8_moral_gray_area.detail: The moral-weight choice is S5_C3 and presents no correct answer. The three values in tension are: mercy toward the fragile, homeward urgency, and respect for another people's country.

q9_exit_emotional_state.detail: The full session architecture exits on anticipation through S5_C4, but Phase 7 session_close_design contradicts it by ending the session earlier in the Throne Room on a departure-planning choice. Failing element: session_close_design.resolution_prose and session_end_choice. Phase: 7. Revision: replace the entire close package so the session ends on the Lion's unresolved kingship decision, not on setting out from Emerald City.

q10_talkability.detail: At least two choices are strongly talkable: the China Country crossing method and whether Dorothy asks the Lion to stay, choose freely, or continue with her.

q11_hard_ban_scan.detail: Banned or disallowed voice elements appear in generated prose. Found: (1) trailing simile/action comparison in S5_C1 option C world_noticed_signal: "The words came out like a little gust of wind"; (2) trailing simile in S5_C4 option A world_noticed_signal: "A murmur ran through the forest creatures like wind in leaves"; (3) trailing simile in S5_C4 option C world_noticed_signal: "as one who has two roads under one pair of feet"; (4) sentence mold close to contrast-framing scaffold in session_close_design.resolution_prose: "You are going South... and that is now your true road home." Phase: 5/7. Revision: remove all action-line similes and recast those lines into plain declarative Baum-style physical description.

q12_trailing_simile_scan.detail: Failing elements: S5_C1.C world_noticed_signal ("like a little gust of wind"), S5_C4.A world_noticed_signal ("like wind in leaves"), and S5_C4.C world_noticed_signal ("as one who has two roads under one pair of feet"). Phase: 5. Revision: rewrite each signal without simile, using direct physical description and plain consequence.

q13_sentence_mold_scan.detail: No clear instances of the banned molds "It's not X, it's Y," "No X. No Y. Just Z.," or balanced modern contrast scaffolds dominate the prose.

q14_voice_authenticity.detail: Cold open strongly reflects Baum through at least two signature techniques: plain marvel delivery and question-answer exposition, with sequential connective chaining also present. Random outcomes also sustain voice markers: S5_C2.B uses direct emotional naming and practical moral explanation; S5_C3.C uses question-answer social logic and plain moral labeling; S5_C4.B uses compact emotional summary and storybook omniscient commentary. Attribution test passes overall.

q15_fourteen_point_audit.detail: The 14-point audit is not clean because Point 2 Matter-of-Fact Marvels is flagged by repeated simile-heavy action phrasing, and Point 6 Emotion by Naming and Gesture is weakened in the non-canonical Phase 7 close by summary that exits too neatly instead of preserving the designed unresolved hook. Phase: 5/7. Revision: strip similes from world-noticed lines and replace the Phase 7 close with the actual Lion-hook ending.

q16_diction_fingerprint.detail: Dorothy's internal and spoken language remains plain, courteous, and home-oriented. Scarecrow is explanatory and practical, Tin Woodman courteous and tender, Lion confessional then kingly. Diction clusters of little/great/good/road/south/kind/queer/pretty are consistently maintained.

fourteen_point_audit_results.location: Phase 5 world_noticed_signals: S5_C1.C, S5_C4.A, S5_C4.C use simile-forward phrasing instead of plain declarative description.
Phase 7 session_close_design resolves emotion into summary comfort rather than preserving the designed unresolved decision at the Lion hook.

q17_canon_integrity.detail: Failing element: Phase 7 session_close_design introduces a non-canonical session ending before the southern journey begins, contradicting the confirmed Session 5 map and the designed coverage through the China Country and Lion kingship offer. Phase: 7. Revision: align the close with canon coverage by ending after the spider-beast victory and Lion kingship request, not at Emerald City departure.

q18_character_truth.detail: NPC behavior remains within StoryGuard Layer 3 truth. Soldier is polite and practical, Scarecrow solution-minded, Tin Woodman tender and courteous, Lion emotionally transparent and kingly when needed, china milkmaid vexed rather than implausibly forgiving.

q19_world_reactivity.detail: World reactivity is active and visible: companion language shifts after S5_C1, Emerald City framing changes after S5_C2, China Country residents alter behavior after S5_C3, and forest beasts' social posture changes after S5_C4. Environmental, NPC, factional, and symbolic reactivity are all represented.

q20_persistent_state_consistency.detail: Failing element: state continuity between Phase 5 and Phase 6 is broken by the contradictory Phase 7 close, which ignores later Session 5 state changes such as china_country_crossing_mode_* and forest_beasts_political_state outcomes. Phase: 7. Revision: rebuild session_close_design so it reflects the final persistent state after S5_C4 rather than resetting to pre-departure Emerald City conditions.

q21_world_noticed_signals.detail: Most significant state changes have signals, but not every signal passes the voice/immersion standard because several rely on simile-heavy phrasing rather than grounded physical noticing. Failing elements: S5_C1.C, S5_C4.A, S5_C4.C world_noticed_signals. Phase: 5. Revision: rewrite those signals as subtle in-world physical acknowledgments without simile.

q22_social_echo_defining_lines.detail: All four branching choices have three defining lines each, all under 20 words, provocative, and non-spoilery. Count verifies at 12 total.

q23_alignment_balance.detail: All branching choices span lawful/neutral/chaotic distinctly, and no single register is systematically advantaged. Each triad maps to a different human value set rather than offering three tonal variants of the same impulse.

revision_instructions.instruction: Replace the current session close so the session ends on S5_C4, the Lion's unresolved kingship decision, instead of the Emerald City departure choice.
Remove all action-line similes from world-noticed signals and recast them into plain Baum-style declarative physical description.
Rewrite S5_C1.C, S5_C4.A, and S5_C4.C world-noticed signals without any simile construction.
Clear the flagged 14-point audit failures by stripping similes from flagged lines and replacing the noncanonical resolved close with the designed unresolved Lion hook.
Realign session_close_design to the confirmed Session 5 canon coverage through the Lion kingship offer rather than ending before the southern road journey.
Update the close so it preserves and reflects final Session 5 state changes, especially China Country crossing outcomes and forest beasts political state.
Rewrite the flagged world-noticed signals as subtle, grounded physical acknowledgments in author voice, without figurative flourish.

entry_point_diagnosis.editorial_diagnosis: The first paragraph that passes the 60-second stakes test is the paragraph beginning, “The Lion became quite angry at the laughter...” At that moment a new user can immediately feel the problem: the road is blocked, the party has already failed once, another companion is now in danger, and brute force will not work. Everything before that is functional approach material, but it delays the live dramatic engine. The forest exit, the hill description, the first verbal exchange with the unseen guardians, and the physical description of the Hammer-Head all serve orientation and curiosity rather than urgent playable tension. They are not bad prose, but they are passive setup for an interactive cold open. By cutting to the Lion’s charge and immediate defeat, the session begins at the first undeniable proof that the travelers cannot proceed by ordinary means. This also sharpens the session’s true dramatic function: Dorothy is at the last barrier before the answer home, and she must decide how to spend her final magical resource. The cut therefore removes explanatory runway and starts on consequence-bearing action. Crucial lost information must be folded back in quickly through action: that Dorothy is traveling with the Scarecrow, Tin Woodman, Lion, and Toto; that strange armless Hammer-Heads guard the hill by striking with their heads; and that Dorothy still possesses one final command over the Winged Monkeys through the Golden Cap.

format_specific_cut.cut_point: Chapter XXII, paragraph beginning: “The Lion became quite angry at the laughter caused by the Scarecrow’s mishap...”
format_specific_cut.original_before_cut: 7 paragraphs, approximately 230 words before the cut
format_specific_cut.cut_eliminates: Approach description, threshold dialogue, creature reveal, scenic setup, and explanatory obstacle introduction before the danger becomes immediate
format_specific_cut.must_reintroduce: Need to reintroduce through action that the Scarecrow has just been struck down, the attackers are Hammer-Heads who guard the hill, Dorothy is traveling with Toto, the Lion, the Scarecrow, and the Tin Woodman, and the Golden Cap still holds one last lawful summons of the Winged Monkeys

entry_point_diagnosis.cold_open:
Dust stings your face, and the rocks of the steep hill are hot beneath your hands as you run to the Scarecrow, who has just come tumbling down among them. Before you can set him straight, the Lion gives a great roar and springs upward, for he cannot bear cruel laughter.

Then one of the queer Hammer-Heads shoots forward from behind a rock, and the Lion goes rolling back as if a cannon ball had struck him. You help him up at once, though he is bruised and ashamed, and you see plainly that no courage and no strength will carry your friends over this hill.

The armless people laugh again from every side, and their flat heads bob above the rocks. Toto presses close to your skirt. The Tin Woodman says there is still one way left, for the Golden Cap may command the Winged Monkeys only once more. If you spend that last help now, you may reach Glinda at last; if you do not, the road ends here.
entry_point_diagnosis.emotional_promise: The emotional promise of this cold open is: deliverance. A user arrives feeling pressed and wanting to choose.

setup.source_moment: Chapter XXII, from the Lion charging the Hammer-Heads after the Scarecrow is struck down through Dorothy being told the Golden Cap may be used one last time.
setup.why_it_qualifies: This is the cold-open cut identified in Phase 3. The danger is immediate, the obstacle is visible, brute force has failed twice, and Dorothy must make a meaningful choice within the first 300 words: spend the last lawful summons of the Winged Monkeys or face the road ending here. It establishes place, party, peril, and agency at once.

escalation.source_moment: Chapter XXII, from Dorothy summoning the Winged Monkeys, crossing into the red country of the Quadlings, receiving food and directions at the farmhouse, and being admitted toward Glinda's castle.
escalation.why_it_qualifies: The visible goal is now clear and near: reach Glinda and ask for the way home. The obstacle is no longer raw danger but narrowing possibility: the final Monkey command is gone, the last magical reserve has been spent, and everything now depends on whether Glinda can truly solve what Oz could not. Travel, hospitality, and approach all compound anticipation while making the loss of fallback options plain.

breath.source_moment: Chapter XXIII, the preparation room before meeting Glinda, where Dorothy washes, the Lion shakes dust from his mane, the Scarecrow pats himself into shape, and the Tin Woodman polishes and oils himself.
breath.why_it_qualifies: This is the session's natural Schell Rule reward. After the hard barrier and the spending of the last Monkey summons, the party receives safety, order, and gentle comic relief through practical tidying. It is pure Baum: absurd bodies treated with calm domestic practicality. It also gives the player expressive room before the audience with Glinda.

twist.source_moment: Chapter XXIII, Glinda reveals that the Silver Shoes could have taken Dorothy home from the first day, then asks for the Golden Cap and calmly allocates the companions' futures.
twist.why_it_qualifies: This is the moral-weight turn because the answer home arrives joined to relinquishment and separation. Dorothy can go home, but only by accepting that her companions will remain in Oz and by surrendering magical authority she still possesses. There is no correct answer because both values are good: home is right, and devotion to companions is right. The revelation also reframes the whole journey without invalidating it.

resolution.source_moment: Chapter XXIII into Chapter XXIV, from the farewell sequence through Dorothy clapping the Silver Shoes and arriving in Kansas before Aunt Em sees her.
resolution.why_it_qualifies: The primary goal appears resolved: Dorothy reaches Kansas. But for session architecture, the session should end on Dorothy's own unresolved final commitment just before the heel-click, not after the homecoming image has fully closed the tale. The planted seed is the last choice to part, which functions as the emotional lock on the ending and keeps the player's agency at the threshold of fulfillment.

branching_choices.dramatic_question: Do you spend the Golden Cap's last command at once to cross the Hammer-Heads, or do you hesitate and test whether some plainer way remains?

Having spent your last magical help, how do you mean to approach Glinda's country now: hurry straight toward your answer, move carefully with your friends, or let the people of the South guide your steps?

When Glinda says the Shoes can send you home and begins settling your friends into their own kingdoms, do you press first for Kansas, linger over what must be lost, or ask whether any other way lets you remain with them longer?

With Toto in your arms and your friends waiting for your last word, do you click the Shoes now, ask for one more farewell moment, or nearly delay yourself by trying to hold both home and friendship at once?

emotional_choices.source_attachment: Dorothy thanking the Monkey King after the final crossing and hearing that this is the last time she may summon them.
First view of the rich and happy red country of the Quadlings, with fields, brooks, roads, and bright red houses.
At the farmhouse dinner, when Dorothy asks how far it is to the Castle of Glinda.
The preparation room where each companion makes himself presentable in his own queer way.
The party entering Glinda's ruby throne room together, neat and ready after all they have endured.

posture_shifts.placement: late Setup
posture_shifts.player_attitude_exposed: Whether Dorothy studies the Hammer-Heads and the terms of the problem before acting.

posture_shifts.placement: mid Escalation
posture_shifts.player_attitude_exposed: Whether Dorothy answers the Monkey King's farewell with warmth and gratitude.

posture_shifts.placement: mid Escalation
posture_shifts.player_attitude_exposed: Whether Dorothy opens herself to the marvel of the Quadling country or keeps her mind fixed only on Kansas.

posture_shifts.placement: late Escalation
posture_shifts.player_attitude_exposed: Whether Dorothy becomes shy and small before the red-uniformed guards and Glinda's castle.

posture_shifts.placement: during Breath beat
posture_shifts.player_attitude_exposed: Whether Dorothy can enjoy the practical absurdity of her companions tidying themselves.

posture_shifts.placement: during Twist
posture_shifts.player_attitude_exposed: Whether Dorothy receives Glinda's revelation with forgiveness and humility rather than complaint.

content_budget.narration_token_budget: Approximately 3,400-4,300 words of narrator output across the full session.
compression_targets: The remainder of the forest approach and pre-cut hill description are compressed into immediate action context in the cold open.
The Monkey flight over the hill and the broad scenic survey of the Quadling country become one flowing transition beat.
The farmhouse meal and directions are compressed into a short hospitality scene with one expressive pause.
The red-uniformed gate exchange and admission to the castle are compressed into a threshold beat.
expansion_targets: The cold open at the Hammer-Heads, because the first branching choice must arrive inside the first 300 words and show visible consequence quickly.
The preparation room, because it is the session's comic reward and best posture-shift cluster.
Glinda's audience, especially the revelation of the Shoes and the assignment of each companion's future.
The farewell threshold before the heel-click, because the session-end choice must land on Dorothy's own unresolved decision.

must_survive_verbatim: “What can I do for you, my child?”
“Bless your dear heart, I am sure I can tell you of a way to get back to Kansas.”
“Your Silver Shoes will carry you over the desert.”
“If you had known their power you could have gone back to your Aunt Em the very first day you came to this country.”
“You are unusual.”
“Take me home to Aunt Em!”
can_be_reshaped: The Tin Woodman's reminder that the Golden Cap may be used once more.
The Monkey King's farewell after the final summons.
The farmer's wife's directions to Glinda's castle.
Glinda's sequential allocation of the three remaining Monkey commands.
The companions' reflections that they would have missed their brains, heart, and courage.
can_be_cut: Most of the pre-cut threshold dialogue with the Hammer-Heads before the Scarecrow is struck.
Extended scenic listing that repeats the red-country motif after it is established once.
Some of the ceremonial guard business before Glinda admits the party.
The full Kansas landing aftermath beyond the first confirming image, if the session ends on the pre-click hook.

beat_map.moment: Cold open: Scarecrow has fallen, Lion charges, and the Hammer-Heads throw him back.
beat_map.dramatic_function: Immediate reaction posture establishes fear, resolve, or practical alertness.

beat_map.moment: Tin Woodman reminds Dorothy that one final Monkey summons remains.
beat_map.dramatic_function: Choice 1 identity branch on wish_spending_vs_reserve; first meaningful choice within opening protocol.

beat_map.moment: Visible consequence: if Dorothy commits, the Monkeys come at once; if she hesitates, the hill proves impassable before the summons is still forced into relevance.
beat_map.dramatic_function: Observation or intensifying posture lets the player color Dorothy's response to consequence.

beat_map.moment: The Winged Monkeys bear the company over the hill while the Hammer-Heads rage below.
beat_map.dramatic_function: Gratitude toward the Monkey King colors Dorothy's use of final magical aid.

beat_map.moment: Arrival in the rich and happy country of the Quadlings; red fields, red houses, red people, and safe roads.
